At last, he couldn’t help shutting ’em, to ease ’em a minute; and the very moment he did so, he hears Chickweed a-roaring out, ‘Here he is!’ Off he starts once more, with Chickweed half-way down the street ahead of him; and after twice as long a run as the yesterday’s one, the man’s lost again! This was done, once or twice more, till one-half the neighbours gave out that Mr. Chickweed had been robbed by the devil, who was playing tricks with him arterwards; and the other half, that poor Mr. Chickweed had gone mad with grief.”

当然，那位女士知道这件事，不是吗？布莱瑟斯先生插话道：“你总是打断别人的话，搭档！康基·奇克威德，这位先生，在巴特尔桥一带开了一家酒馆，他的酒馆地下室里经常举办斗鸡和獾拖钩等活动，活动进行得非常有序，我也曾经见过。他当时还不是这个家庭的一员。一天晚上，他被盗了三百二十七几尼，一个带着黑眼罩的高个子男人在夜半潜入他的卧室，从床下爬出来，然后从窗户跳出去。整个过程非常迅速。但是康基也很快，他拿起霰弹枪向小偷开火，并惊动了邻居。他们立即发出了呼救声，当他们四处寻找时，发现康基击中了小偷，因为到处都有血迹，直到离这里有一段距离的地方才消失。然而，小偷已经逃走了，于是，酒馆老板奇克威德的名字出现在了破产者的名单中，很多人为他举办了慈善活动，甚至设立了众筹，来帮助这个可怜的人。他陷入了极度沮丧之中，整天在街上徘徊，拼命地抓自己的头发，弄得很多人都担心他会自杀。一天，他急匆匆地跑到办公室，与法官进行了一次私密的交谈，法官听完后，叫来了热心的警官杰姆·斯派尔斯，命令他帮助康基抓住小偷。“我昨天早上看到他从我家门前经过，”康基说。“为什么你不去抓他呢？”斯派尔斯问道。“我太震惊了，你可以用牙签敲碎我的头骨，”可怜的康基回答说，“但我们一定会抓到他，因为他将在晚上十点到十一点之间再次经过。”斯派尔斯一听到这个消息，就塞了一些干净的衣服和一个梳子到口袋里，以防他需要逗留一两天，然后便出发了，他坐在一家酒馆的窗口后面的小红帘后面，戴着帽子，随时准备冲出去。他正在那里抽着烟斗，深夜时分，康基突然大喊：“他来了！抓住小偷！谋杀！”杰姆·斯派尔斯冲了出去，他看到康基正沿着街道飞奔而去，人们纷纷转身，所有人都在喊：“小偷！”，康基自己也一直像疯子一样大喊大叫。斯派尔斯追赶康基，转过一个角落，看到了一个人群，冲进去问道：“哪个人是小偷？”“该死的！”康基说，“我又把他弄丢了！”这是一件非常奇怪的事情，但那个人却怎么也找不到，所以他们只好回到酒馆。第二天早晨，斯派尔斯回到原来的位置，从窗帘后面向外张望，寻找那个带着黑色眼罩的高个子男人，直到自己的眼睛都疼了。最后，他实在忍不住闭上眼睛休息一会儿，刚一闭眼，就听到了康基再次大喊：“他来了！”于是他又开始追赶，康基在前面，跑了比昨天更远的距离，但是那个男人还是没被抓到。这件事重复发生了几次，以至于半数邻居都相信奇克威德先生是被魔鬼抢劫的，而魔鬼后来又跟他开了几个玩笑；另一半人则认为可怜的奇克威德先生因丧失财物而疯了。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Of course the lady knows that, don’t she?” demanded Mr. Blathers. “Always interrupting, you are, partner! This here Conkey Chickweed, miss, kept a public-house over Battlebridge way, and he had a cellar, where a good many young lords went to see cock-fighting, and badger-drawing, and that; and a wery intellectual manner the sports was conducted in, for I’ve seen ’em off’en. He warn’t one of the family, at that time; and one night he was robbed of three hundred and twenty-seven guineas in a canvas bag, that was stole out of his bedroom in the dead of night, by a tall man with a black patch over his eye, who had concealed himself under the bed, and after committing the robbery, jumped slap out of window: which was only a story high. He was wery quick about it. But Conkey was quick, too; for he fired a blunderbuss arter him, and roused the neighbourhood. They set up a hue-and-cry, directly, and when they came to look about ’em, found that Conkey had hit the robber; for there was traces of blood, all the way to some palings a good distance off; and there they lost ’em. However, he had made off with the blunt; and, consequently, the name of Mr. Chickweed, licensed witler, appeared in the Gazette among the other bankrupts; and all manner of benefits and subscriptions, and I don’t know what all, was got up for the poor man, who was in a wery low state of mind about his loss, and went up and down the streets, for three or four days, a pulling his hair off in such a desperate manner that many people was afraid he might be going to make away with himself. One day he came up to the office, all in a hurry, and had a private interview with the magistrate, who, after a deal of talk, rings the bell, and orders Jem Spyers in (Jem was a active officer), and tells him to go and assist Mr. Chickweed in apprehending the man as robbed his house. ‘I see him, Spyers,’ said Chickweed, ‘pass my house yesterday morning,’ ‘Why didn’t you up, and collar him!’ says Spyers. ‘I was so struck all of a heap, that you might have fractured my skull with a toothpick,’ says the poor man; ‘but we’re sure to have him; for between ten and eleven o’clock at night he passed again.’ Spyers no sooner heard this, than he put some clean linen and a comb, in his pocket, in case he should have to stop a day or two; and away he goes, and sets himself down at one of the public-house windows behind the little red curtain, with his hat on, all ready to bolt out, at a moment’s notice. He was smoking his pipe here, late at night, when all of a sudden Chickweed roars out, ‘Here he is! Stop thief! Murder!’ Jem Spyers dashes out; and there he sees Chickweed, a-tearing down the street full cry. Away goes Spyers; on goes Chickweed; round turns the people; everybody roars out, ‘Thieves!’ and Chickweed himself keeps on shouting, all the time, like mad. Spyers loses sight of him a minute as he turns a corner; shoots round; sees a little crowd; dives in; ‘Which is the man?’ ‘D—me!’ says Chickweed, ‘I’ve lost him again!’ It was a remarkable occurrence, but he warn’t to be seen nowhere, so they went back to the public-house. Next morning, Spyers took his old place, and looked out, from behind the curtain, for a tall man with a black patch over his eye, till his own two eyes ached again. At last, he couldn’t help shutting ’em, to ease ’em a minute; and the very moment he did so, he hears Chickweed a-roaring out, ‘Here he is!’ Off he starts once more, with Chickweed half-way down the street ahead of him; and after twice as long a run as the yesterday’s one, the man’s lost again! This was done, once or twice more, till one-half the neighbours gave out that Mr. Chickweed had been robbed by the devil, who was playing tricks with him arterwards; and the other half, that poor Mr. Chickweed had gone mad with grief.”

当然，女士也知道这一点，不是吗？”布拉瑟斯先生要求道。“你总是打断别人，伙伴！这位康基·奇克威德先生在巴特尔桥路开了一家客栈，他有一间地窖，很多年轻的贵族会去那里看斗鸡、狸猫狗猴和其他活动；那些运动项目都以非常有文化的方式进行，我以前见过。他当时不是这个家庭的一员，有一晚他在夜深人静的时候被偷了三百二十七几尼，它们被藏在一个帆布袋里，从他的卧室偷走了，偷盗者是一个带着黑色眼罩的高个子男人，他躲在床下，在犯完罪后从窗户跳出去：窗户离地只有一个楼层高。他动作很快。但是康基也很快，因为他朝小偷开了一枪，惊动了邻居。他们立即发出喊声，当他们到处寻找时，他们发现康基击中了小偷，因为从现场到不远处的围栏处，都有血迹可循，但他们就是再也没找到。尽管如此，小偷还是携带赃款逃跑了，因此，持牌酒店老板奇克威德先生的名字出现在《公报》上，和其他破产的人一起，有各种各样的捐款和订阅，我不知道还有什么，都被为这个可怜的人筹集起来，他因损失而精神低落，连续三四天走在街上，以一种非常绝望的方式拔自己的头发，很多人都担心他可能会自杀。一天，他匆忙地来到办公室，与法官进行了私人会面，法官在交谈了一番之后，按响了门铃，让贾姆·斯派尔斯进来（贾姆是一名积极的军官），并指示他协助奇克威德逮捕入室抢劫的人。“我昨天早上看到他从我的房子旁边经过，”奇克威德说。“你为什么不站起来抓住他呢？”斯派尔斯问道。“我被吓得全身发抖，就像被牙签击中头骨一样，”可怜的家伙说。“但我们一定会抓到他，因为他在晚上十点到十一点之间又从这里经过。”斯派尔斯一听这话，就把一些干净的衣服和一把梳子塞进口袋里，以防他需要停留一两天，然后就出发了，并在一家酒吧后面的小红窗帘后坐下，戴着帽子，随时准备冲出去。他在那里抽着烟斗，到了深夜，突然听到奇克威德大喊：“抓住他！强盗！谋杀！”贾姆·斯派尔斯冲了出去，他看到奇克威德正在大声喊叫着，飞速奔下街。斯派尔斯追赶，奇克威德在前面，人们转过身来，每个人都喊道：“小偷！”奇克威德自己也一直像疯子一样大喊大叫。斯派尔斯在拐角处失去了他的踪迹，疾驰过去，看到了一个人群，冲进去，问道：“哪个人是罪犯？”“该死的！”奇克威德说：“我又把他弄丢了！”这是一个令人惊讶的事情，但无论如何也找不到他，所以他们回到酒吧。第二天早晨，斯派尔斯回到原来的位置，从幕后向外观察，寻找一个带有黑色眼罩的高个子男人，直到自己的眼睛又开始疼痛。最后，他忍不住闭上眼睛休息一下，而就在他这样做的那一刻，他听到奇克威德又在大喊：“他来了！”他又一次出发，奇克威德在前面已经跑了一半的路程，在经过比昨天更长时间的追逐之后，罪犯又一次溜走了！这种事情发生了一两次，直到附近一半的邻居都说奇克威德先生是被魔鬼偷了，魔鬼在事后跟他开玩笑；另一半人则说，穷困潦倒的奇克威德先生因悲伤而疯了。

source: “I shall not be at home again, perhaps for some time; I wish you would write to me—say once a fort-night: every alternate Monday: to the General Post Office in London. Will you?”
domestication: 我可能有一段时间不能回家了，你能不能每隔一段时间给我写信？比如说，每隔两周，也就是每两个星期一，把信寄到伦敦的总邮局。我想知道，你愿不愿意这么做呢？
foreignization: “我也许会有一段时间不能再回到家中；我希望你可以给我写信——比如每隔一段时间：每隔一个星期一：到伦敦的总邮局。我可以吗？”

source: With these words, which were uttered with all the energy of passionate grief, Oliver fell upon his knees at the Jew’s feet; and beat his hands together, in perfect desperation.
domestication: 他说完这些话，话语中充满了痛苦和哀伤，欧利弗跪倒在犹太人的脚下，双手拼命地拍打着，完全失去了希望。
foreignization: 当着这些话语时，这些话是带着充满热情的悲痛的全部力量说的，奥利弗在犹太人的脚前跪下了；并且捶胸顿足，以完全的绝望。

source: But, the measure of his degradation was not yet full. After making a tour of the house, and thinking, for the first time, that the poor-laws really were too hard on people; and that men who ran away from their wives, leaving them chargeable to the parish, ought, in justice to be visited with no punishment at all, but rather rewarded as meritorious individuals who had suffered much; Mr. Bumble came to a room where some of the female paupers were usually employed in washing the parish linen: when the sound of voices in conversation, now proceeded.
domestication: 不过，他的没落还没到谷底。鲍恩布尔走遍了整个房子，第一次觉得穷人法令对人太残酷，觉得那些抛弃妻子让她们靠教区救济的男人不应该受到惩罚，反而应该受到奖励，因为他们都是经历了很多苦难的有功之臣。走着走着，鲍恩布尔来到了一个房间，这里通常有些贫民妇女在洗教区的衣服。这时，他听到了里面有人在聊天的声音。
foreignization: 但他的堕落还没有到达顶点。在参观了整栋房子之后，他第一次认为穷人法案对人们来说实在太苛刻了；那些抛弃妻子让她们给教区带来负担的男人，按照正义不应该受到任何惩罚，反而应该因为他们所遭受的痛苦而被当作有功之人得到奖励；班布尔先生来到了通常有几个女贫民在那里洗教区的衣物的房间：此时，一阵谈话声传了出来。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

But, the measure of his degradation was not yet full. After making a tour of the house, and thinking, for the first time, that the poor-laws really were too hard on people; and that men who ran away from their wives, leaving them chargeable to the parish, ought, in justice to be visited with no punishment at all, but rather rewarded as meritorious individuals who had suffered much; Mr. Bumble came to a room where some of the female paupers were usually employed in washing the parish linen: when the sound of voices in conversation, now proceeded.
不过，他的没落还没到谷底。鲍恩布尔走遍了整个房子，第一次觉得穷人法令对人太残酷，觉得那些抛弃妻子让她们靠教区救济的男人不应该受到惩罚，反而应该受到奖励，因为他们都是经历了很多苦难的有功之臣。走着走着，鲍恩布尔来到了一个房间，这里通常有些贫民妇女在洗教区的衣服。这时，他听到了里面有人在聊天的声音。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

But, the measure of his degradation was not yet full. After making a tour of the house, and thinking, for the first time, that the poor-laws really were too hard on people; and that men who ran away from their wives, leaving them chargeable to the parish, ought, in justice to be visited with no punishment at all, but rather rewarded as meritorious individuals who had suffered much; Mr. Bumble came to a room where some of the female paupers were usually employed in washing the parish linen: when the sound of voices in conversation, now proceeded.
但他的堕落还没有到达顶点。在参观了整栋房子之后，他第一次认为穷人法案对人们来说实在太苛刻了；那些抛弃妻子让她们给教区带来负担的男人，按照正义不应该受到任何惩罚，反而应该因为他们所遭受的痛苦而被当作有功之人得到奖励；班布尔先生来到了通常有几个女贫民在那里洗教区的衣物的房间：此时，一阵谈话声传了出来。

source: Master Charles Bates, appalled by Sikes’s crime, fell into a train of reflection whether an honest life was not, after all, the best. Arriving at the conclusion that it certainly was, he turned his back upon the scenes of the past, resolved to amend it in some new sphere of action. He struggled hard, and suffered much, for some time; but, having a contented disposition, and a good purpose, succeeded in the end; and, from being a farmer’s drudge, and a carrier’s lad, he is now the merriest young grazier in all Northamptonshire.
domestication: 马斯特·查尔斯·贝茨被赛克斯的罪行吓坏了，开始反思做个好人是不是终归还是最好的选择。最后他得出结论，确实如此，于是他转身离开了过去的生活，决心在新的领域里改过自新。他很努力，也很辛苦，但他性格乐观，目标明确，最终成功了。从前他只是农场的苦力和货车小伙，如今他已经成为了北安普顿郡最欢快的年轻牧场主。
foreignization: 贝茨少爷被塞克斯的罪行吓坏了，陷入了一种沉思，想知道老实的生活是否毕竟是最好的。得出这样的结论，他转身背对过去的场景，决心在新的行动领域改正它。他努力奋斗，遭受了很长时间的困苦；但他性格愉快，目的良好，最终成功了；从农场里的苦工和马车夫的小伙计，他现在成为了整个北安普顿郡最欢乐的年轻牧场主。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Master Charles Bates, appalled by Sikes’s crime, fell into a train of reflection whether an honest life was not, after all, the best. Arriving at the conclusion that it certainly was, he turned his back upon the scenes of the past, resolved to amend it in some new sphere of action. He struggled hard, and suffered much, for some time; but, having a contented disposition, and a good purpose, succeeded in the end; and, from being a farmer’s drudge, and a carrier’s lad, he is now the merriest young grazier in all Northamptonshire.
马斯特·查尔斯·贝茨被赛克斯的罪行吓坏了，开始反思做个好人是不是终归还是最好的选择。最后他得出结论，确实如此，于是他转身离开了过去的生活，决心在新的领域里改过自新。他很努力，也很辛苦，但他性格乐观，目标明确，最终成功了。从前他只是农场的苦力和货车小伙，如今他已经成为了北安普顿郡最欢快的年轻牧场主。

Arriving at the conclusion that it certainly was, he turned his back upon the scenes of the past, resolved to amend it in some new sphere of action. He struggled hard, and suffered much, for some time; but, having a contented disposition, and a good purpose, succeeded in the end; and, from being a farmer’s drudge, and a carrier’s lad, he is now the merriest young grazier in all Northamptonshire.
贝茨少爷被塞克斯的罪行吓坏了，陷入了一种沉思，想知道老实的生活是否毕竟是最好的。得出这样的结论，他转身背对过去的场景，决心在新的行动领域改正它。他努力奋斗，遭受了很长时间的困苦；但他性格愉快，目的良好，最终成功了；从农场里的苦工和马车夫的小伙计，他现在成为了整个北安普顿郡最欢乐的年轻牧场主。

source: At length, one morning, when Rose was alone in the breakfast-parlour, Harry Maylie entered; and, with some hesitation, begged permission to speak with her for a few moments.
domestication: 终于有一天早上，罗斯一个人在早餐室的时候，哈里·梅利走了进来，稍微犹豫了一下，就求罗斯允许他跟她聊几分钟。罗斯当然同意了，两人便一边喝茶，一边聊了起来。
foreignization: 最后，一天早晨，当罗斯一个人在早餐室里时，哈利·梅利进入了房间，并且有些犹豫，请求允许他跟她谈几分钟。

messages_domestication.content:

At length, one morning, when Rose was alone in the breakfast-parlour, Harry Maylie entered; and, with some hesitation, begged permission to speak with her for a few moments.

source: “I hope not,” rejoined the old gentleman. “I do not think you ever will. I have been deceived, before, in the objects whom I have endeavoured to benefit; but I feel strongly disposed to trust you, nevertheless; and I am more interested in your behalf than I can well account for, even to myself. The persons on whom I have bestowed my dearest love, lie deep in their graves; but, although the happiness and delight of my life lie buried there too, I have not made a coffin of my heart, and sealed it up, forever, on my best affections. Deep affliction has but strengthened and refined them.”
domestication: 这位老先生回答说：“我希望不会如此，我觉得你不会让我失望。我以前也曾被一些我想帮助的人骗过，但我还是忍不住想相信你。说实话，我对你很感兴趣，甚至我自己也说不清楚为什么。那些我曾经深爱过的人，现在都已入土为安了，我的一生中最大的幸福和乐趣也都随他们而去。但是我并没有因为这而将心封闭起来，永远不再对任何人产生感情。相反，遭遇巨大悲痛后，我的情感反而变得更加深刻和纯粹了。
foreignization: “我希望不会，”老先生回答说，“我认为你永远不会失望我，我以前曾经被那些我试图帮助的人欺骗过；但尽管如此，我还是很愿意相信你；而且我对你的情况感到非常关心，这种关心甚至超过了我自己能够解释的程度，那些我曾经给予最深爱的人，已经深埋在地下；但是，尽管我的一生中的幸福和喜悦也被埋葬在那里，但我并没有把我的心变成一个棺材，并且永远封闭上去我的最佳情感，深刻的痛苦只不过加强和提炼了它们。

source: The doctor was in the chaise; Giles (who, it had been arranged, should be left behind) held the door open in his hand; and the women-servants were in the garden, looking on. Harry cast one slight glance at the latticed window, and jumped into the carriage.

source: It was fortunate for her that the possession of money occasioned him so much employment next day in the way of eating and drinking; and withal had so beneficial an effect in smoothing down the asperities of his temper; that he had neither time nor inclination to be very critical upon her behaviour and deportment. That she had all the abstracted and nervous manner of one who is on the eve of some bold and hazardous step, which it has required no common struggle to resolve upon, would have been obvious to the lynx-eyed Fagin, who would most probably have taken the alarm at once; but Mr. Sikes lacking the niceties of discrimination, and being troubled with no more subtle misgivings than those which resolve themselves into a dogged roughness of behaviour towards everybody; and being, furthermore, in an unusually amiable condition, as has been already observed; saw nothing unusual in her demeanor, and indeed, troubled himself so little about her, that, had her agitation been far more perceptible than it was, it would have been very unlikely to have awakened his suspicions.
domestication: 幸好第二天有钱后，他整天忙着吃喝玩乐，心情也因此变得愉快，没空也没心思去品评她的行为举止。如果是贼头目法金看到她这样子，肯定会马上起疑，因为他眼力毒辣，什么都能看在眼里。但是赛克斯就不同了，他性情粗鲁，分不清细节，脑子里只有对谁都发号施令的想法，而且那天他心情格外好，所以根本没注意到她有什么异常，即使她再着急，也不可能让他产生怀疑。
foreignization: 幸运的是，对于她来说，拥有金钱在第二天让他有了很多机会去吃喝，并且对他平滑性情的发脾气有了这么好的效果；以至于他没有时间也没有愿望去对她的行为和举止进行批评。即使她表现出所有抽象的和紧张的举止，就像是一个人在即将迈出某个大胆和危险的步骤的前夕，这个步骤需要非常激烈的斗争来决定；这本来会很明显地被有锐利眼睛的费金发现，费金可能马上就会提高警惕；但是西克斯先生缺乏辨别细微差别的能力，并且受困于没有比那些变成对每个人都很粗鲁的行为更为微妙的疑虑；并且，另外，他处在一个异常和蔼的状态中，就像已经观察到的那样；他在她的举止中看不到什么不寻常的东西，并且，事实上，他根本不关心她，以至于，即使她的烦躁程度远远高于现在，也不太可能唤醒他的怀疑。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

It was fortunate for her that the possession of money occasioned him so much employment next day in the way of eating and drinking; and withal had so beneficial an effect in smoothing down the asperities of his temper; that he had neither time nor inclination to be very critical upon her behaviour and deportment. That she had all the abstracted and nervous manner of one who is on the eve of some bold and hazardous step, which it has required no common struggle to resolve upon, would have been obvious to the lynx-eyed Fagin, who would most probably have taken the alarm at once; but Mr. Sikes lacking the niceties of discrimination, and being troubled with no more subtle misgivings than those which resolve themselves into a dogged roughness of behaviour towards everybody; and being, furthermore, in an unusually amiable condition, as has been already observed; saw nothing unusual in her demeanor, and indeed, troubled himself so little about her, that, had her agitation been far more perceptible than it was, it would have been very unlikely to have awakened his suspicions.
幸好第二天有钱后，他整天忙着吃喝玩乐，心情也因此变得愉快，没空也没心思去品评她的行为举止。如果是贼头目法金看到她这样子，肯定会马上起疑，因为他眼力毒辣，什么都能看在眼里。但是赛克斯就不同了，他性情粗鲁，分不清细节，脑子里只有对谁都发号施令的想法，而且那天他心情格外好，所以根本没注意到她有什么异常，即使她再着急，也不可能让他产生怀疑。

It was fortunate for her that the possession of money occasioned him so much employment next day in the way of eating and drinking; and withal had so beneficial an effect in smoothing down the asperities of his temper; that he had neither time nor inclination to be very critical upon her behaviour and deportment. That she had all the abstracted and nervous manner of one who is on the eve of some bold and hazardous step, which it has required no common struggle to resolve upon, would have been obvious to the lynx-eyed Fagin, who would most probably have taken the alarm at once; but Mr. Sikes lacking the niceties of discrimination, and being troubled with no more subtle misgivings than those which resolve themselves into a dogged roughness of behaviour towards everybody; and being, furthermore, in an unusually amiable condition, as has been already observed; saw nothing unusual in her demeanor, and indeed, troubled himself so little about her, that, had her agitation been far more perceptible than it was, it would have been very unlikely to have awakened his suspicions.

source: “Yes, sir,” stammered the boy. The gentleman who spoke last was unconsciously right. It would have been very like a Christian, and a marvellously good Christian too, if Oliver had prayed for the people who fed and took care of him. But he hadn’t, because nobody had taught him.

source: This was the handsome offer with which Mr. Grimwig backed and confirmed nearly every assertion he made; and it was the more singular in his case, because, even admitting for the sake of argument, the possibility of scientific improvements being brought to that pass which will enable a gentleman to eat his own head in the event of his being so disposed, Mr. Grimwig’s head was such a particularly large one, that the most sanguine man alive could hardly entertain a hope of being able to get through it at a sitting—to put entirely out of the question, a very thick coating of powder.
domestication: 格里姆威格先生每次说话，都会用一句话来给自己的话增加分量，这句话就是：“我愿意把自己的脑袋吃掉。”这话在他嘴里尤其奇怪，因为，即使我们假设科学有一天会发展到能让人吃掉自己的头，格里姆威格先生的头实在太大了，哪怕是最乐观的人，也很难指望一次性地吃完它，更别说他的头上还有一层厚厚的粉。
foreignization: 这是格林维格先生支持和证实几乎每个断言的漂亮提议；而在他的情况下，这更为奇特，因为，即使为了论点而承认，在科学进步可以实现的可能性，即使一位先生想要吃掉自己的头部，也能在他有此倾向的情况下实现，格林维格先生的头部是一个如此特别的大头，即使是最乐观的活人也很难希望在一次就能全部吃完——完全不考虑到，一层很厚的粉末。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

This was the handsome offer with which Mr. Grimwig backed and confirmed nearly every assertion he made; and it was the more singular in his case, because, even admitting for the sake of argument, the possibility of scientific improvements being brought to that pass which will enable a gentleman to eat his own head in the event of his being so disposed, Mr. Grimwig’s head was such a particularly large one, that the most sanguine man alive could hardly entertain a hope of being able to get through it at a sitting—to put entirely out of the question, a very thick coating of powder.
格里姆威格先生每次说话，都会用一句话来给自己的话增加分量，这句话就是：“我愿意把自己的脑袋吃掉。”这话在他嘴里尤其奇怪，因为，即使我们假设科学有一天会发展到能让人吃掉自己的头，格里姆威格先生的头实在太大了，哪怕是最乐观的人，也很难指望一次性地吃完它，更别说他的头上还有一层厚厚的粉。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

This was the handsome offer with which Mr. Grimwig backed and confirmed nearly every assertion he made; and it was the more singular in his case, because, even admitting for the sake of argument, the possibility of scientific improvements being brought to that pass which will enable a gentleman to eat his own head in the event of his being so disposed, Mr. Grimwig’s head was such a particularly large one, that the most sanguine man alive could hardly entertain a hope of being able to get through it at a sitting—to put entirely out of the question, a very thick coating of powder.
这是格林维格先生支持和证实几乎每个断言的漂亮提议；而在他的情况下，这更为奇特，因为，即使为了论点而承认，在科学进步可以实现的可能性，即使一位先生想要吃掉自己的头部，也能在他有此倾向的情况下实现，格林维格先生的头部是一个如此特别的大头，即使是最乐观的活人也很难希望在一次就能全部吃完——完全不考虑到，一层很厚的粉末。

source: On glancing at the address, the parochial functionary observed that it contained no name. The stranger had not gone far, so he made after him to ask it.
domestication: 他随意瞄了一眼地址，发现上面竟然没有写名字。这个陌生人还没走远，工作人员便追上去问他。
foreignization: 浏览了地址后，教区职员发现里面没有名字。陌生人没有走远，所以他追上去问他。

source: Peeping out, more than once, when he reached the top, to make sure that he was unobserved, Noah Claypole darted away at his utmost speed, and made for the Jew’s house as fast as his legs would carry him.
domestication: 诺亚·克莱波尔在爬上去的时候不止一次地往外看，想确认一下四周是否有人注意到他。一旦确定没人注意，他就飞快地跑开了，尽可能快地朝犹太人的家奔去。
foreignization: 往外偷看了一眼，再往外偷看了一眼，当他到达顶部后，诺亚·克莱波尔（Noah Claypole）为了确认自己没有被人观察到，尽可能快地冲向犹太人的房子，他的腿带着他以最快的速度奔跑。

source: “Let ’em hear!” said Sikes; “I don’t care.” But as Mr. Sikes did care, on reflection, he dropped his voice as he said the words, and grew calmer.
domestication: 斯ай克斯说：“随他们怎么说去吧，我才不在乎呢！”但他转念一想，其实自己还是很介意的。于是，他把声音放低了，说完就平静下来了。
foreignization: “让他们听见吧！”赛克斯说，“我才不在乎呢。”但当斯威克斯先生反思后，他发现自己其实是关心的，所以他放低了声音说出这些话，并变得更加冷静。

source: “No, no,” replied the matron, inclining her head to catch the words, as they came more faintly from the dying woman. “Be quick, or it may be too late!”

domestication: “算了吧，我不玩了，谢谢你，费金先生，”奇特林先生回答说，“我已经受够了。那个小流氓太走运了，根本就没法跟他比。”

source: Noah looked eagerly about him for the Dodger; but although there were several women who would have done very well for that distinguished character’s mother or sister, and more than one man who might be supposed to bear a strong resemblance to his father, nobody at all answering the description given him of Mr. Dawkins was to be seen. He waited in a state of much suspense and uncertainty until the women, being committed for trial, went flaunting out; and then was quickly relieved by the appearance of another prisoner who he felt at once could be no other than the object of his visit.
domestication: 诺亚热切地四处张望，想找到小偷 HOWEVER 还是没看到。尽管有几个女人长得挺像小偷的母亲或者姐姐，有些男人也可能长得像他父亲，但就是没有人符合他得到的小偷的描述。他一直处于十分焦虑和犹豫的状态，直到那些被起诉的女人昂首阔步地离开了，之后他马上就看到了另一个囚犯，他一下子就知道，这个人一定就是他来这里要找的人。
foreignization: 诺亚渴切地四周张望，寻找道奇；尽管有几个女人可能非常适合作为这位杰出人物的母亲或妹妹，还有一个以上的男人可能被认为与他的父亲有很强的相似之处，但完全没有人符合给他描述的道金斯先生的描述。他处于非常悬而未决的状态，等到那些被控以审判的女人们昂首阔步地出去之后；然后在另一名囚犯出现后，他立即感到宽慰，并且他一下子就觉得这名囚犯不会是他此行的目的对象。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Noah looked eagerly about him for the Dodger; but although there were several women who would have done very well for that distinguished character’s mother or sister, and more than one man who might be supposed to bear a strong resemblance to his father, nobody at all answering the description given him of Mr. Dawkins was to be seen. He waited in a state of much suspense and uncertainty until the women, being committed for trial, went flaunting out; and then was quickly relieved by the appearance of another prisoner who he felt at once could be no other than the object of his visit.
诺亚热切地四处张望，想找到小偷 HOWEVER 还是没看到。尽管有几个女人长得挺像小偷的母亲或者姐姐，有些男人也可能长得像他父亲，但就是没有人符合他得到的小偷的描述。他一直处于十分焦虑和犹豫的状态，直到那些被起诉的女人昂首阔步地离开了，之后他马上就看到了另一个囚犯，他一下子就知道，这个人一定就是他来这里要找的人。

domestication: 布朗洛先生回应道：“的确会这样，你就放心吧，而且你还算走运呢。”

source: “It must be a piercer, if it finds its way through your heart,” said Mr. Sikes. “Give him something to drink, Nancy. Burn my body, make haste! It’s enough to turn a man ill, to see his lean old carcase shivering in that way, like a ugly ghost just rose from the grave.”
domestication: “这东西必定很锋利，要不然它就进不了你的心脏，”赛克斯先生说。“给他点喝的，南希。我的天啊，快点儿！看到他那个干瘦的老骨头在那样子发抖，像刚从坟墓里爬出来的鬼一样，足以让人病倒。”
foreignization: “它一定很锋利，如果它刺穿了你的心，”赛克斯先生说。“给他喝点儿东西，南希。烧我的身体，快点儿！看到他瘦老的骨架子在那样的颤抖着，就像从坟墓里刚刚升起来的丑陋的鬼魂一样，足以让人病倒。”

“It must be a piercer, if it finds its way through your heart,” said Mr. Sikes. “Give him something to drink, Nancy. Burn my body, make haste! It’s enough to turn a man ill, to see his lean old carcase shivering in that way, like a ugly ghost just rose from the grave.”
“这东西必定很锋利，要不然它就进不了你的心脏，”赛克斯先生说。“给他点喝的，南希。我的天啊，快点儿！看到他那个干瘦的老骨头在那样子发抖，像刚从坟墓里爬出来的鬼一样，足以让人病倒。”

domestication: “嘘，小声点儿，”小偷低声训斥道，同时他谨慎地环顾四周，“你想被当场抓住啊，傻瓜？”

source: One afternoon, the Dodger and Master Bates being engaged out that evening, the first-named young gentleman took it into his head to evince some anxiety regarding the decoration of his person (to do him justice, this was by no means an habitual weakness with him); and, with this end and aim, he condescendingly commanded Oliver to assist him in his toilet, straightway.
domestication: 有一天下午，小偷仔和贝茨有事要出门，小偷仔突然想起了自己也得好好打扮一下（说实话，这可不像是他的常态）。于是，他居然屈尊降贵地命令奥利弗帮他梳妆打扮。
foreignization: 一天下午，流氓和贝茨先生晚上都有事，所以流氓突然想让人帮他打扮一下（为了公平起见，这并不是他的坏习惯），然后他颐指气使地命令奥利弗立即帮助他梳妆打扮。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

One afternoon, the Dodger and Master Bates being engaged out that evening, the first-named young gentleman took it into his head to evince some anxiety regarding the decoration of his person (to do him justice, this was by no means an habitual weakness with him); and, with this end and aim, he condescendingly commanded Oliver to assist him in his toilet, straightway.
有一天下午，小偷仔和贝茨有事要出门，小偷仔突然想起了自己也得好好打扮一下（说实话，这可不像是他的常态）。于是，他居然屈尊降贵地命令奥利弗帮他梳妆打扮。

messages_foreignization.content:

One afternoon, the Dodger and Master Bates being engaged out that evening, the first-named young gentleman took it into his head to evince some anxiety regarding the decoration of his person (to do him justice, this was by no means an habitual weakness with him); and, with this end and aim, he condescendingly commanded Oliver to assist him in his toilet, straightway.
一天下午，流氓和贝茨先生晚上都有事，所以流氓突然想让人帮他打扮一下（为了公平起见，这并不是他的坏习惯），然后他颐指气使地命令奥利弗立即帮助他梳妆打扮。

source: He walked up one street, and down another, until exercise had abated the first passion of his grief; and then the revulsion of feeling made him thirsty. He passed a great many public-houses; but, at length paused before one in a by-way, whose parlour, as he gathered from a hasty peep over the blinds, was deserted, save by one solitary customer. It began to rain, heavily, at the moment. This determined him. Mr. Bumble stepped in; and ordering something to drink, as he passed the bar, entered the apartment into which he had looked from the street.
domestication: 他左转右转地走了好几条街，走着走着，最初的悲伤慢慢地平息下来，这才想起自己有点口渴了。路上看到很多酒馆，但他就是没法儿踏进去，直到走到一条僻静的小巷里，他才在一家小店面前停住了脚步，从窗帘缝隙里偷偷往里面看了一眼，发现只有一个顾客在里面。这时，外面突然下起了瓢泼大雨。于是，他下定了决心，推开门，走了进去。在吧台前边喝了点东西，然后走进了从街上看过的那间屋子。
foreignization: 他走上一条街，又走下另一条街，直到运动稍微减轻了他悲伤的初期激情；然后，情绪的转变使他感到口渴。他经过了很多酒吧；但最终，他在一条小巷子里的一家店前停了下来，他从百叶窗的快速一瞥中得知，这家的客厅里除了一位孤独的顾客外，已经没有别人了。当时，雨开始下了起来，雨下得很大。这使他下定了决心。班布尔先生走了进去，在他经过酒吧时，点了些东西喝，然后进入了他从街上看到的那个房间。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

He walked up one street, and down another, until exercise had abated the first passion of his grief; and then the revulsion of feeling made him thirsty. He passed a great many public-houses; but, at length paused before one in a by-way, whose parlour, as he gathered from a hasty peep over the blinds, was deserted, save by one solitary customer. It began to rain, heavily, at the moment. This determined him. Mr. Bumble stepped in; and ordering something to drink, as he passed the bar, entered the apartment into which he had looked from the street.
他左转右转地走了好几条街，走着走着，最初的悲伤慢慢地平息下来，这才想起自己有点口渴了。路上看到很多酒馆，但他就是没法儿踏进去，直到走到一条僻静的小巷里，他才在一家小店面前停住了脚步，从窗帘缝隙里偷偷往里面看了一眼，发现只有一个顾客在里面。这时，外面突然下起了瓢泼大雨。于是，他下定了决心，推开门，走了进去。在吧台前边喝了点东西，然后走进了从街上看过的那间屋子。

source: At this confession, Miss Nancy burst into a loud laugh; and, swallowing a glass of brandy, shook her head with an air of defiance, and burst into sundry exclamations of “Keep the game a-going!” “Never say die!” and the like. These seemed to have the effect of re-assuring both gentlemen; for the Jew nodded his head with a satisfied air, and resumed his seat: as did Mr. Sikes likewise.
domestication: 说到这儿，南希小姐突然大笑起来，一口气喝下一杯白兰地，嘴里喊着“继续干吧”、“永不放弃”之类的话，摇了摇头，摆出一副我不怕的架势。这种举动似乎让两位先生都放心了，犹太人满脸自豪地点点头，又坐了回去，赛克斯先生也跟着坐下了。
foreignization: 在这次坦白中，南希小姐突然爆发出一阵大笑；然后，她一口气喝下一杯白兰地，带着挑衅的神情摇了摇头，并突然大声地说出“继续玩下去！”“永远不要放弃！”等类似的话。这些话似乎让两位先生都感到放心了；因为犹太人满意地点了点头，然后坐回了他的座位：赛克斯先生也一样。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

These seemed to have the effect of re-assuring both gentlemen; for the Jew nodded his head with a satisfied air, and resumed his seat: as did Mr. Sikes likewise.

At this confession, Miss Nancy burst into a loud laugh; and, swallowing a glass of brandy, shook her head with an air of defiance, and burst into sundry exclamations of “Keep the game a-going!” “Never say die!” and the like. These seemed to have the effect of re-assuring both gentlemen; for the Jew nodded his head with a satisfied air, and resumed his seat: as did Mr. Sikes likewise.
在这次坦白中，南希小姐突然爆发出一阵大笑；然后，她一口气喝下一杯白兰地，带着挑衅的神情摇了摇头，并突然大声地说出“继续玩下去！”“永远不要放弃！”等类似的话。这些话似乎让两位先生都感到放心了；因为犹太人满意地点了点头，然后坐回了他的座位：赛克斯先生也一样。

source: A mist hung over the river, deepening the red glare of the fires that burnt upon the small craft moored off the different wharfs, and rendering darker and more indistinct the murky buildings on the banks. The old smoke-stained storehouses on either side, rose heavy and dull from the dense mass of roofs and gables, and frowned sternly upon water too black to reflect even their lumbering shapes. The tower of old Saint Saviour’s Church, and the spire of Saint Magnus, so long the giant-warders of the ancient bridge, were visible in the gloom; but the forest of shipping below bridge, and the thickly scattered spires of churches above, were nearly all hidden from sight.
domestication: 河面上蒙着一层薄雾，火光在水面上投下红色的影子，更显得码头边停泊的小船格外壮观。河岸两旁的破旧仓库仿佛从密密麻麻的屋顶和檐角中崛起，沉重而昏暗，似乎带着愠色凝视着下面乌黑的河水。远处的圣救主教堂的钟楼和圣马格努斯教堂的尖塔，这两座古老的建筑物曾经是老桥的守卫者，现在仍然清晰可见。但是桥下的船只林立，岸上的教堂林立，却被浓雾遮蔽了，只剩下些模糊的轮廓。
foreignization: 河上笼罩着一层雾气，深化了小型船只停泊在各个码头上面燃烧的火焰的红色光芒，并且使河岸上的浑浊建筑物变得更加黑暗和模糊。两边的旧烟污染的仓库从屋顶和山墙的密集群中沉重而单调地升起，严肃地凝视着甚至连它们笨重的形状都无法映现在其表面的黑水。旧圣救世主教堂的尖塔和圣马格努斯教堂的尖塔，曾经长期是古老桥梁的巨人守卫者，在黑暗中清晰可见；但是桥下的轮船林立和上面的教堂尖塔密集分布几乎都从视线中隐藏起来。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

A mist hung over the river, deepening the red glare of the fires that burnt upon the small craft moored off the different wharfs, and rendering darker and more indistinct the murky buildings on the banks. The old smoke-stained storehouses on either side, rose heavy and dull from the dense mass of roofs and gables, and frowned sternly upon water too black to reflect even their lumbering shapes. The tower of old Saint Saviour’s Church, and the spire of Saint Magnus, so long the giant-warders of the ancient bridge, were visible in the gloom; but the forest of shipping below bridge, and the thickly scattered spires of churches above, were nearly all hidden from sight.

source: Charlotte’s fist was by no means a light one; but, lest it should not be effectual in calming Oliver’s wrath, Mrs. Sowerberry plunged into the kitchen, and assisted to hold him with one hand, while she scratched his face with the other. In this favourable position of affairs, Noah rose from the ground, and pommelled him behind.
domestication: 夏洛特的拳头可一点也不轻，但怕打得不够劲儿，索伯利夫人赶忙冲进厨房，一边帮着按住奥利弗，一边用另一只手在他脸上乱挠。这时候，局面对他们来说很有利，诺亚从地上爬起来，在奥利弗身后狠揍了一顿。
foreignization: 夏洛特的拳头一点也不轻；但为了防止它不能有效地平息奥利弗的愤怒，索伯里夫人冲进厨房，并用一只手帮助按住他，同时用另一只手抓他的脸。在这种有利的情况下，诺亚从地上爬起来，从后面揍他。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

In this favourable position of affairs, Noah rose from the ground, and pommelled him behind.

source: Mr. Bumble had a great idea of his oratorical powers and his importance. He had displayed the one, and vindicated the other. He relaxed.
domestication: 班布尔先生非常看重自己的口才和身分地位，他觉得自己已经充分展示了自己的辩论才能，也维护了自己的威严。于是，他放松下来了，心想自己已经露了一手。
foreignization: 班布尔先生对自己的演说才能和重要性有着极高的估计。他已经展示了前者，并证明了后者。他放松下来。

source: “Frighten him!” echoed Sikes. “It’ll be no sham frightening, mind you. If there’s anything queer about him when we once get into the work; in for a penny, in for a pound. You won’t see him alive again, Fagin. Think of that, before you send him. Mark my words!” said the robber, poising a crowbar, which he had drawn from under the bedstead.
domestication: “吓唬他一顿吧！”赛克斯重复了一遍，“这回可不是开玩笑的，你听清楚了！要是他表现得不对劲，我们就要彻底解决他。进一步就要走到底，不能半途而废。你让他去的话，就别想再看到他活着回来，法金，你好好考虑考虑吧！”说完，他从床下拿出一根撬棍，架在肩上，摆出一副随时准备出手的姿势。
foreignization: “吓唬他！”斯ай克斯回应道。“这将是真正的恐吓，注意了。如果我们一旦开始行动，有什么奇怪的事情发生；无论如何都得继续下去，你不会再看到他活着，法金。在派他之前，先考虑一下这个问题。记住我的话！”那个强盗说着，从床下抽出一根撬棍，举起来示威。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Frighten him!” echoed Sikes. “It’ll be no sham frightening, mind you. If there’s anything queer about him when we once get into the work; in for a penny, in for a pound. You won’t see him alive again, Fagin. Think of that, before you send him. Mark my words!” said the robber, poising a crowbar, which he had drawn from under the bedstead.
“吓唬他一顿吧！”赛克斯重复了一遍，“这回可不是开玩笑的，你听清楚了！要是他表现得不对劲，我们就要彻底解决他。进一步就要走到底，不能半途而废。你让他去的话，就别想再看到他活着回来，法金，你好好考虑考虑吧！”说完，他从床下拿出一根撬棍，架在肩上，摆出一副随时准备出手的姿势。

source: “Oh! yes, I dare say!” said Mr. Fang, with a sneer. “Come, none of your tricks here, you young vagabond; they won’t do. What’s your name?”
domestication: “哎，你当真以为自己有什么了不起的本事吗？” 方先生冷笑着说：“别想在这里耍花招，小流氓！快说，你叫什么名字？”
foreignization: “哦，是的，我敢说！”范先生带着一丝嘲笑说。“来吧，小捣蛋鬼，别在这里耍花招，那些东西在这里不管用，你叫什么名字？”

source: “Ah! mercy indeed, ma’am,” was the reply. “I only hope this’ll teach master not to have any more of these dreadful creatures, that are born to be murderers and robbers from their very cradle. Poor Noah! He was all but killed, ma’am, when I came in.”
domestication: “哎呀，天哪，太可怜了，”她回答说。“我只希望这次教训能让老爷吸取教训，不要再养这种祸害，简直是从小就生来是为了做贼杀人。可怜的诺亚，我进来的时候他都被打得半死不活的。”
foreignization: “啊，天啊，夫人，”是回答。“我只希望这能教训主人不要再养这些可怕的生物，他们从摇篮里就注定成为凶手和小偷。可怜的诺亚！我进来的时候他几乎被杀了，夫人。”

“Ah! mercy indeed, ma’am,” was the reply. “I only hope this’ll teach master not to have any more of these dreadful creatures, that are born to be murderers and robbers from their very cradle. Poor Noah! He was all but killed, ma’am, when I came in.”
“哎呀，天哪，太可怜了，”她回答说。“我只希望这次教训能让老爷吸取教训，不要再养这种祸害，简直是从小就生来是为了做贼杀人。可怜的诺亚，我进来的时候他都被打得半死不活的。”

“Ah! mercy indeed, ma’am,” was the reply. “I only hope this’ll teach master not to have any more of these dreadful creatures, that are born to be murderers and robbers from their very cradle. Poor Noah! He was all but killed, ma’am, when I came in.”
“啊，天啊，夫人，”是回答。“我只希望这能教训主人不要再养这些可怕的生物，他们从摇篮里就注定成为凶手和小偷。可怜的诺亚！我进来的时候他几乎被杀了，夫人。”

source: All the windows in the rear of the house had been long ago bricked up, except one small trap in the room where the boy was locked, and that was too small even for the passage of his body. But, from this aperture, he had never ceased to call on those without, to guard the back; and thus, when the murderer emerged at last on the house-top by the door in the roof, a loud shout proclaimed the fact to those in front, who immediately began to pour round, pressing upon each other in an unbroken stream.
domestication: 后面房子的窗户早就被封砖了，只有一间房子里有个小天窗，是那个男孩被锁在里头的房间，然而那个天窗太小了，男孩根本就不能从那里钻出去。但是从这个小天窗里，男孩一直在呼救，提醒外面的人注意屋顶。所以，当凶手终于从屋顶的天门出来时，里面的人大声喊叫，提醒前面的守卫，于是他们立刻蜂拥而至，源源不断地围了过来。
foreignization: 房子的后面所有的窗户都被封砖了，除了男孩被锁在的房间里的一个小通风口，然而那个小通气孔甚至太小以至于他的身体无法通过。但是，从那个开口，他一直没有停止呼叫那些在外面的守卫屋后的；于是，当凶手最后从房顶的门出来时，从屋顶传来一声大喊，宣告了这个事实给那些站在前面的人，他们立即开始围拢过来，互相拥挤着形成一股源源不断的洪流。

All the windows in the rear of the house had been long ago bricked up, except one small trap in the room where the boy was locked, and that was too small even for the passage of his body. But, from this aperture, he had never ceased to call on those without, to guard the back; and thus, when the murderer emerged at last on the house-top by the door in the roof, a loud shout proclaimed the fact to those in front, who immediately began to pour round, pressing upon each other in an unbroken stream.

source: The kind tone of this answer, the sweet voice, the gentle manner, the absence of any accent of haughtiness or displeasure, took the girl completely by surprise, and she burst into tears.
domestication: 这回答的语气竟然这么温和，话语中还透着甜甜的声调，举止也那么温柔，从头到尾没有一点架子，也不见有一丝不满的意味，这让这个女孩儿完全猝不及防，她一下子就被感动得涕泪交融。
foreignization: 这回答的亲切语气，这甜美的声音，这温和的态度，没有任何居高临下的或者不愉快的口吻，让这个女孩完全猝不及防，她突然哭了起来。

source: The next morning, the public were once informed that Oliver Twist was again to let, and that five pounds would be paid to anybody who would take possession of him.

source: Mounting a stool, he cautiously applied his eye to the pane of glass, from which secret post he could see Mr. Claypole taking cold beef from the dish, and porter from the pot, and administering homeopathic doses of both to Charlotte, who sat patiently by, eating and drinking at his pleasure.

source: “I wish,” said Toby turning to Mr. Chitling, “that you had picked out some other crib when the two old ones got too warm, and had not come here, my fine feller.”

source: The girl still held Oliver fast by the hand, and continued to pour into his ear, the warnings and assurances she had already imparted. All was so quick and hurried, that he had scarcely time to recollect where he was, or how he came there, when the carriage stopped at the house to which the Jew’s steps had been directed on the previous evening.
domestication: 那姑娘仍然牢牢地握着奥利弗的手，继续在他耳边说个不停，讲述着她已经告诉他很多次的警告和保证。事情发生得如此之快和仓促，以至于奥利弗几乎没有时间回想自己身在何处，或是怎么来到这里，当马车在前一晚犹太人的脚步所指引的那栋房子前停下来时。
foreignization: 这个女孩仍然紧握着奥利弗的手，继续向他耳边倾诉她已经说过的那些警告和保证。 一切都如此快速而匆忙，以至于他几乎没有时间回忆自己在哪里，或者自己是如何来到这里，当马车在之前晚上犹太人的脚步所指引的房子前停下。

source: “But not mine,” said the Jew, submissively. “He might have become of use to me. When there are two parties to a bargain, it is only reasonable that the interests of both should be consulted; is it, my good friend?”
domestication: “但不是我，”那犹太人顺从地说道。“他本来是可以帮我忙的。做买卖的时候，双方都有自己的利益，自然应该两利相顾，否则就没法成交，你说呢，好朋友？”
foreignization: “但不是我，”犹太人谦恭地说。“他本可以对我有用。在一笔交易中，如果有两方参与，那么双方的利益都应该被考虑，这是很合理的，对吗，我的好朋友？”

source: As a proof of the fact, the Jew drew forth two keys from his pocket; and explained, that when he first went downstairs, he had locked them in, to prevent any intrusion on the conference.

source: “No, no! Oh, not ill!” replied Rose: shuddering as though some deadly chillness were passing over her, while she spoke; “I shall be better presently. Close the window, pray!”

source: As Sikes growled forth this imprecation, with the most desperate ferocity that his desperate nature was capable of, he rested the body of the wounded boy across his bended knee; and turned his head, for an instant, to look back at his pursuers.
domestication: 赛克斯在咒骂时露出了极度绝望的凶猛神情，他把受伤男孩的身体放在弯曲的膝盖上，转过头去看一眼正在追赶他的人。他那张狰狞的脸庞扭曲成一个丑陋的表情，他满怀仇恨地看着那些人，这一刻，他的眼睛似乎要喷出火来。
foreignization: 当斯派克斯以他绝望的性格所能拥有的最绝望的凶猛程度低吼着这句咒骂时，他将受伤男孩的身体横放在他的弯曲的膝盖上；然后他转过头去，回头看了一眼他的追赶者。

messages_domestication.content:

As Sikes growled forth this imprecation, with the most desperate ferocity that his desperate nature was capable of, he rested the body of the wounded boy across his bended knee; and turned his head, for an instant, to look back at his pursuers.
赛克斯在咒骂时露出了极度绝望的凶猛神情，他把受伤男孩的身体放在弯曲的膝盖上，转过头去看一眼正在追赶他的人。他那张狰狞的脸庞扭曲成一个丑陋的表情，他满怀仇恨地看着那些人，这一刻，他的眼睛似乎要喷出火来。

source: “Wide,” replied Toby, after peeping in to satisfy himself. “The game of that is, that they always leave it open with a catch, so that the dog, who’s got a bed in here, may walk up and down the passage when he feels wakeful. Ha! ha! Barney ’ticed him away tonight. So neat!”

source: This was an antic fellow, half pedlar and half mountebank, who travelled about the country on foot to vend hones, strops, razors, washballs, harness-paste, medicine for dogs and horses, cheap perfumery, cosmetics, and such-like wares, which he carried in a case slung to his back. His entrance was the signal for various homely jokes with the countrymen, which slackened not until he had made his supper, and opened his box of treasures, when he ingeniously contrived to unite business with amusement.
domestication: 这家伙挺有意思，是个半商贩半江湖医生，总是拎着一个大包袱在乡下走村串户，卖那些磨刀石、剃刀、肥皂、马匹用的化妆品、便宜的香水和其他乱七八糟的小玩意儿。一旦他进门，农民们就会开始跟他开玩笑，直到他吃完晚饭打开他的宝贝盒子，边做生意边逗人笑。
foreignization: 这是一个傻瓜一样的人，半商贩半江湖骗子，他步行在乡间，出售磨刀石、剃刀皮带、剃刀、洗脸球、马鞍糊、狗马药、廉价香水、化妆品和其他类似的商品，这些东西都装在一个背包里。他一进来，乡下人就开始各种嘲笑他，这种嘲笑持续到他吃完晚饭，打开宝箱的时候，他巧妙地将生意和娱乐结合起来。

source: With this irrepressible ebullition of mirth, Master Bates laid himself flat on the floor: and kicked convulsively for five minutes, in an ectasy of facetious joy. Then jumping to his feet, he snatched the cleft stick from the Dodger; and, advancing to Oliver, viewed him round and round; while the Jew, taking off his nightcap, made a great number of low bows to the bewildered boy. The Artful, meantime, who was of a rather saturnine disposition, and seldom gave way to merriment when it interfered with business, rifled Oliver’s pockets with steady assiduity.
domestication: 贝茨先生突然爆发出一阵无法抑制的大笑，直接趴到了地上，疯狂地抽搐着，沉浸在这一刻的欢乐中足足五分钟。之后，他一下子跳了起来，从道奇手里抢过劈开的棍子，走到奥利弗面前，打量着他。与此同时，那个犹太人摘下他的睡帽，对着困惑的奥利弗不断鞠躬。道奇则显得有些严肃，他不是一个轻易被搞笑情境影响的人，更关心的是如何从奥利弗身上得到好处，因此，他认真地开始翻找奥利弗的口袋。
foreignization: 伴随着这无法抑制的欢笑爆发，贝茨先生扑通一声躺在地板上：然后抽搐地踢了五分钟，沉浸在一个滑稽的喜悦的恍惚中。接着他跳起来，夺过道德十字军手中的劈开的棍子；并向奥利弗走去，围着他转来转去；与此同时，这个犹太人摘下他的睡帽，对着困惑的男孩不断行屈膝礼。与此同时，狡猾的人则性情严肃，很少在与生意冲突时让自己大笑，他以坚定不移的勤奋搜遍了奥利弗的口袋。

The Artful, meantime, who was of a rather saturnine disposition, and seldom gave way to merriment when it interfered with business, rifled Oliver’s pockets with steady assiduity.

source: “I went,” said Mr. Brownlow, after a short pause, “I went, when all was over, to the scene of his—I will use the term the world would freely use, for worldly harshness or favour are now alike to him—of his guilty love, resolved that if my fears were realised that erring child should find one heart and home to shelter and compassionate her. The family had left that part a week before; they had called in such trifling debts as were outstanding, discharged them, and left the place by night. Why, or whither, none can tell.”
domestication: “一切都结束后，”布朗洛先生停顿了一下才说，“我去了他曾经——我会用世人普遍使用的词语来形容，因为现在世俗的苛刻或偏爱对他来说已经没有任何区别——沉溺在罪恶之爱的地方。我当时决心如果我的担忧成真，那个迷途的孩子应该能找到一个心和家来庇护和怜悯她。那个家庭已经在一周前离开了那里，他们收回了那些微不足道的未偿债务，清算了所有债务，然后在夜里离开了。但是为什么离开，或者他们到底去了哪里，却无人知晓。”
foreignization: “我去了，”布朗洛先生在短暂的停顿后说，“我在一切都结束后，去了他——我将使用世界会轻松使用的术语，因为现在世俗的残酷或恩惠对于他来说都是同样无所谓的——罪恶之爱的现场，我决心如果我的担忧得到了证实，那个有错的孩子将找到一个可以庇护和怜悯她的心和家。这个家庭在一周前就离开了那边；他们收回了未结的微不足道的债务，偿还了它们，并在夜里离开了那里。为什么，或是去了哪里，没有人能说清楚。”

The family had left that part a week before; they had called in such trifling debts as were outstanding, discharged them, and left the place by night. Why, or whither, none can tell.”

source: With her attention not a little distracted by these and a great many other incoherent exclamations of joy, Rose read the address, which was Craven Street, in the Strand. She very soon determined upon turning the discovery to account.

source: The words no sooner escaped her lips, than Mr. Grimwig, who had been affecting to dip into a large book that lay on the table, upset it with a great crash, and falling back in his chair, discharged from his features every expression but one of unmitigated wonder, and indulged in a prolonged and vacant stare; then, as if ashamed of having betrayed so much emotion, he jerked himself, as it were, by a convulsion into his former attitude, and looking out straight before him emitted a long deep whistle, which seemed, at last, not to be discharged on empty air, but to die away in the innermost recesses of his stomach.
domestication: 她的话刚说出口，格林维格先生就把桌子上的大书碰倒在地，发出一声巨响，然后往椅子上一靠，脸上的表情顿时变得僵硬，只剩下无比震惊的神情，呆呆地盯着前方。接着，他似乎觉得自己太过激动有些丢人，于是突然抽搐了一下，恢复了刚才的姿势，直视前方，吹了一声长长的口哨，这声音似乎不是白吹的，而是消失在他肚子的深处。
foreignization: 她的话刚刚说出口，格里姆威格先生就把他假装在读的桌子上的一本大书碰倒在地，使得发出一声巨大的碰撞声，然后他往后一仰身子，瘫倒在椅子里，从他的脸上消失了所有的表情，只剩下难以抑制的惊讶，并且沉浸在一种漫长而茫然的凝视中；然后，好像是因为表现出太多的情绪而感到羞耻，他像抽搐一样突然恢复到了他之前的姿势，眼睛直直地盯着前方吹了一声长长的、低沉的口哨，这口哨似乎最后不仅仅是在空气中消散，而是渐渐消失在他的胃的最深处。

source: “I have no doubt you are, my friend,” replied the old gentleman: fixing his spectacles more firmly on his nose, and looking about him for the inkstand.

source: Oliver stared innocently in Mr. Bumble’s face at this somewhat contradictory style of address; but that gentleman prevented his offering any remark thereupon, by leading him at once into an adjoining room: the door of which was open. It was a large room, with a great window. Behind a desk, sat two old gentleman with powdered heads: one of whom was reading the newspaper; while the other was perusing, with the aid of a pair of tortoise-shell spectacles, a small piece of parchment which lay before him. Mr. Limbkins was standing in front of the desk on one side; and Mr. Gamfield, with a partially washed face, on the other; while two or three bluff-looking men, in top-boots, were lounging about.
domestication: 奥利弗无辜地盯着邦布尔先生的脸，觉得他说话的语气有点儿自相矛盾。但邦布尔先生并没有给奥利弗机会说出什么，他直接把小孩带到了旁边的一个房间，那里的门是敞开的。这间屋子挺大，有一扇很大的窗户。在一张桌子的后面，坐着两位头发上面粉末很多的老爷爷，其中一个正在看报纸，另一个戴着一副玳瑁壳眼镜，在看一张放在他面前的羊皮纸。林伯金先生站在桌子的前面的一边，脸部洗过但是还没完全干净的甘姆菲尔德先生站在另一边，还有两个或是三个长相粗鲁的男人，穿着长筒靴子，到处闲逛。
foreignization: 奥利弗无辜地盯着巴恩布尔先生的脸，凝视着他那种有些矛盾的称呼方式；但那位绅士通过立即将他带到旁边的一个房间，阻止了他对此发表任何评论：那扇门是敞开的。这是一个大房间，有一扇很大的窗户。在一张桌子后面，坐着两个头上撒着粉的老绅士：其中一个正在读报纸；而另一个则在一副龟壳眼镜的帮助下，仔细阅读面前的一小张羊皮纸。林比金斯先生站在桌子的前面的一侧；盖姆菲尔德先生带着洗了一半的脸站在另一侧；而两三个长相粗犷、穿着长筒靴的男人，在那里闲逛。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Oliver stared innocently in Mr. Bumble’s face at this somewhat contradictory style of address; but that gentleman prevented his offering any remark thereupon, by leading him at once into an adjoining room: the door of which was open. It was a large room, with a great window. Behind a desk, sat two old gentleman with powdered heads: one of whom was reading the newspaper; while the other was perusing, with the aid of a pair of tortoise-shell spectacles, a small piece of parchment which lay before him. Mr. Limbkins was standing in front of the desk on one side; and Mr. Gamfield, with a partially washed face, on the other; while two or three bluff-looking men, in top-boots, were lounging about.

source: “But, can you—oh! can you really believe that this delicate boy has been the voluntary associate of the worst outcasts of society?” said Rose.
domestication: “可是，你真的能相信吗？这个娇生惯养的孩子，竟然会自愿跟社会上的渣滓混在一起？”罗斯惊讶地问道。
foreignization: “但是，你能——哦！你真的相信这个脆弱的男孩曾经是社会中最坏的弃子的自愿伙伴吗？”罗斯说。

source: It was a cheerless morning when they got into the street; blowing and raining hard; and the clouds looking dull and stormy. The night had been very wet: large pools of water had collected in the road: and the kennels were overflowing. There was a faint glimmering of the coming day in the sky; but it rather aggravated than relieved the gloom of the scene: the sombre light only serving to pale that which the street lamps afforded, without shedding any warmer or brighter tints upon the wet house-tops, and dreary streets. There appeared to be nobody stirring in that quarter of the town; the windows of the houses were all closely shut; and the streets through which they passed, were noiseless and empty.
domestication: 那是一个阴郁的早晨，当他们走上街头时，风雨交加，乌云密布。前一晚的雨下得很大，路面上积满了水洼，排水沟里的水也都溢出来了。天边隐约透出了一丝黎明的光芒，但这反而使得整个场景显得更加阴沉。昏暗的光线只会让路灯照耀的地方变得更加黯淡，而无法为湿漉漉的屋顶和荒凉的街道带来任何温暖的气息。在他们所经过的城镇这一片区域，似乎一个人也没有，所有的窗户都紧紧地关着，街道上也静悄悄的，空无一人。整个世界仿佛陷入了一种死寂之中。
foreignization: 他们走上街头时，迎面而来的却是一个毫无生气的早晨；风在呼啸，雨下得很大，云层看起来又灰暗又暴躁。夜晚下雨下得很大，路面上积聚了大量水洼，排水沟也都溢满了水。在天空中，新的一天带着微弱的光芒即将来临，但它并没有缓解景色的阴郁，反而加剧了阴郁；昏暗的光线只使得街灯照耀的区域变暗，而没有在湿漉漉的房顶和荒凉的街道上投射出任何更温暖或更明亮的色彩。在城镇的这个地区似乎一个人都没有；所有房屋的窗户都紧紧关闭；他们经过的街道静悄悄的、空空荡荡的。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

It was a cheerless morning when they got into the street; blowing and raining hard; and the clouds looking dull and stormy. The night had been very wet: large pools of water had collected in the road: and the kennels were overflowing. There was a faint glimmering of the coming day in the sky; but it rather aggravated than relieved the gloom of the scene: the sombre light only serving to pale that which the street lamps afforded, without shedding any warmer or brighter tints upon the wet house-tops, and dreary streets. There appeared to be nobody stirring in that quarter of the town; the windows of the houses were all closely shut; and the streets through which they passed, were noiseless and empty.

那是一个阴郁的早晨，当他们走上街头时，风雨交加，乌云密布。前一晚的雨下得很大，路面上积满了水洼，排水沟里的水也都溢出来了。天边隐约透出了一丝黎明的光芒，但这反而使得整个场景显得更加阴沉。昏暗的光线只会让路灯照耀的地方变得更加黯淡，而无法为湿漉漉的屋顶和荒凉的街道带来任何温暖的气息。在他们所经过的城镇这一片区域，似乎一个人也没有，所有的窗户都紧紧地关着，街道上也静悄悄的，空无一人。整个世界仿佛陷入了一种死寂之中。

It was a cheerless morning when they got into the street; blowing and raining hard; and the clouds looking dull and stormy. The night had been very wet: large pools of water had collected in the road: and the kennels were overflowing. There appeared to be nobody stirring in that quarter of the town; the windows of the houses were all closely shut; and the streets through which they passed, were noiseless and empty.

他们走上街头时，迎面而来的却是一个毫无生气的早晨；风在呼啸，雨下得很大，云层看起来又灰暗又暴躁。夜晚下雨下得很大，路面上积聚了大量水洼，排水沟也都溢满了水。在天空中，新的一天带着微弱的光芒即将来临，但它并没有缓解景色的阴郁，反而加剧了阴郁；昏暗的光线只使得街灯照耀的区域变暗，而没有在湿漉漉的房顶和荒凉的街道上投射出任何更温暖或更明亮的色彩。在城镇的这个地区似乎一个人都没有；所有房屋的窗户都紧紧关闭；他们经过的街道静悄悄的、空空荡荡的。

source: “You won’t do nothing of the kind,” rejoined Mr. Sikes. “The Artful’s a deal too artful, and would forget to come, or lose his way, or get dodged by traps and so be perwented, or anything for an excuse, if you put him up to it. Nancy shall go to the ken and fetch it, to make all sure; and I’ll lie down and have a snooze while she’s gone.”
domestication: 斯威格尔先生驳斥说：“休想那么轻松！阿特富尔这孩子太狡猾了，你让他去办事，他准会找借口不去，比如说忘记了，或者走错路了，或者被当局摆了一道，反正不会让你如愿的。还是让南希去那家店把东西取回来，保险点，我这边先打个盹儿等她回来。”
foreignization: “你不会那样做的，”斯派克斯先生回答说。“狡猾鬼太狡猾了，他会忘记来，或是迷路，或者被陷阱阻挠，或者随便找个借口，如果你让他去干这件事。南希得去那家店把东西取回来，为了保险起见；而我将躺下睡一觉，等她回来。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“You won’t do nothing of the kind,” rejoined Mr. Sikes. “The Artful’s a deal too artful, and would forget to come, or lose his way, or get dodged by traps and so be perwented, or anything for an excuse, if you put him up to it. Nancy shall go to the ken and fetch it, to make all sure; and I’ll lie down and have a snooze while she’s gone.”
斯威格尔先生驳斥说：“休想那么轻松！阿特富尔这孩子太狡猾了，你让他去办事，他准会找借口不去，比如说忘记了，或者走错路了，或者被当局摆了一道，反正不会让你如愿的。还是让南希去那家店把东西取回来，保险点，我这边先打个盹儿等她回来。”

“You won’t do nothing of the kind,” rejoined Mr. Sikes. “The Artful’s a deal too artful, and would forget to come, or lose his way, or get dodged by traps and so be perwented, or anything for an excuse, if you put him up to it. Nancy shall go to the ken and fetch it, to make all sure; and I’ll lie down and have a snooze while she’s gone.”
“你不会那样做的，”斯派克斯先生回答说。“狡猾鬼太狡猾了，他会忘记来，或是迷路，或者被陷阱阻挠，或者随便找个借口，如果你让他去干这件事。南希得去那家店把东西取回来，为了保险起见；而我将躺下睡一觉，等她回来。”

source: They traversed the lower room, slowly, and with caution; for Monks started at every shadow; and Mr. Bumble, holding his lantern a foot above the ground, walked not only with remarkable care, but with a marvellously light step for a gentleman of his figure: looking nervously about him for hidden trap-doors. The gate at which they had entered, was softly unfastened and opened by Monks; merely exchanging a nod with their mysterious acquaintance, the married couple emerged into the wet and darkness outside.
domestication: 他们蹑手蹑脚地走过底层的房间，小心翼翼，因为蒙克斯在每个影子面前都会吓一跳，而班布尔先生则将灯笼举到离地一英尺的地方，不仅步伐十分谨慎，还出乎意料地轻盈，毕竟他这个体型不该有这么轻快的步伐。他满脸紧张地四处张望，生怕踩到隐藏的陷阱。他们进来时的那扇门，被蒙克斯悄无声息地打开，里面的人只是跟神秘的熟人点了点头，这对夫妇便走出了门，进入了外面的黑暗和雨夜中。
foreignization: 他们缓慢而谨慎地穿过底层房间；因为蒙克斯会被每一个影子吓到；班布尔先生将灯笼举在离地一英尺的高处行走，不仅步伐惊人地谨慎，而且以他这个身材的绅士来说，脚步出奇地轻，神经质地四处张望着是否有隐藏的陷阱门。在他们进入时的门，被蒙克斯轻轻地打开，夫妻二人与神秘的熟人只是交换了一个点头，于是走出了外面的雨夜。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

They traversed the lower room, slowly, and with caution; for Monks started at every shadow; and Mr. Bumble, holding his lantern a foot above the ground, walked not only with remarkable care, but with a marvellously light step for a gentleman of his figure: looking nervously about him for hidden trap-doors. The gate at which they had entered, was softly unfastened and opened by Monks; merely exchanging a nod with their mysterious acquaintance, the married couple emerged into the wet and darkness outside.
他们蹑手蹑脚地走过底层的房间，小心翼翼，因为蒙克斯在每个影子面前都会吓一跳，而班布尔先生则将灯笼举到离地一英尺的地方，不仅步伐十分谨慎，还出乎意料地轻盈，毕竟他这个体型不该有这么轻快的步伐。他满脸紧张地四处张望，生怕踩到隐藏的陷阱。他们进来时的那扇门，被蒙克斯悄无声息地打开，里面的人只是跟神秘的熟人点了点头，这对夫妇便走出了门，进入了外面的黑暗和雨夜中。

The gate at which they had entered, was softly unfastened and opened by Monks; merely exchanging a nod with their mysterious acquaintance, the married couple emerged into the wet and darkness outside.

source: “You may depend upon me, young man,” answered Mr. Bumble, bowing himself gradually towards the ladder, with excessive politeness. “On everybody’s account, young man; on my own, you know, Mr. Monks.”
domestication: “您可以放心，我会办妥的，年轻人，”班布尔先生回答道，同时他逐渐地鞠躬，行了一个过分优雅的礼，几乎都快要趴到梯子上了。“这不仅是为了大家的面子，也是为了我自己的面子，你也知道的，蒙克斯先生。”
foreignization: “你可以相信我，年轻人，”班布尔先生回答说，他逐渐地用过度礼貌的方式朝着梯子方向鞠躬。“为了每个人的缘故，年轻人；为了我自己，你知道，蒙克斯先生。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“You may depend upon me, young man,” answered Mr. Bumble, bowing himself gradually towards the ladder, with excessive politeness. “On everybody’s account, young man; on my own, you know, Mr. Monks.”
“您可以放心，我会办妥的，年轻人，”班布尔先生回答道，同时他逐渐地鞠躬，行了一个过分优雅的礼，几乎都快要趴到梯子上了。“这不仅是为了大家的面子，也是为了我自己的面子，你也知道的，蒙克斯先生。”

source: “I hope so,” replied the child. “After I am dead, but not before. I know the doctor must be right, Oliver, because I dream so much of Heaven, and Angels, and kind faces that I never see when I am awake. Kiss me,” said the child, climbing up the low gate, and flinging his little arms round Oliver’s neck. “Good-b’ye, dear! God bless you!”
domestication: “我希望如此，”孩子回答道，“在我死后，但不是在之前。我知道医生一定是对的，奥利弗，因为我做了这么多关于天堂、天使和亲切的面孔的梦，这些是我醒着时从未见过的东西。”孩子爬上矮门，张开双臂搂住奥利弗的脖子，说：“吻我，奥利弗。再见了，亲爱的，上帝保佑你！”
foreignization: “我希望如此，”孩子回答道。“在我死后，但不是在那之前。我知道医生一定是对的，奥利弗，因为我梦见了很多天堂、天使以及当我清醒时从未见过的和善面孔。亲亲我，”孩子说着爬上矮门，然后将小手臂环绕在奥利弗的脖子上。“再见，亲爱的！上帝保佑你！”

source: “If he was coming here, he’d have come with the dog,” said Kags, stooping down to examine the animal, who lay panting on the floor. “Here! Give us some water for him; he has run himself faint.”

source: Mrs. Corney drooped her head, when the beadle said this; the beadle drooped his, to get a view of Mrs. Corney’s face. Mrs. Corney, with great propriety, turned her head away, and released her hand to get at her pocket-handkerchief; but insensibly replaced it in that of Mr. Bumble.
domestication: 科尼太太听了教区执事的话后，于是低下了头。教区执事也装作若无其事地低下头，想从侧面偷看科尼太太的脸。科尼太太很有分寸，赶紧把头转到一边去，顺势抽出手来摸手帕。但是没想到，她的手不知不觉又回到了邦布尔先生的手里。
foreignization: 科尼夫人听到救济院院长的话后，低下了头，救济院院长也低下头去，以便能看到科尼夫人的脸。 科尼夫人很有礼貌地转过头去，把手抽回来拿手帕，但不知不觉中又把手放在了班布尔先生的手里。

source: He looked, wistfully, into their faces, one by one when they passed out, as though to see which way the greater number leant; but that was fruitless. The jailer touched him on the shoulder. He followed mechanically to the end of the dock, and sat down on a chair. The man pointed it out, or he would not have seen it.
domestication: 他带着惆怅的眼神，一一看着他们的脸，当他们走过时，好像想看看大多数人更倾向哪边，但那是徒劳的。狱卒拍了拍他的肩膀，他机械地跟着走到法庭的尽头，然后坐在一张椅子上。如果不是那个人指给他看，他都没注意到那张椅子。
foreignization: 他带着无奈的神情，当他们一个个走出去时，朝每个人的脸上看过去，仿佛想要看看大多数人是倾向哪一边；但那样做是没有意义的。狱吏碰了碰他的肩膀，他机械地跟着走到法庭的尽头，然后坐在一把椅子上。要不是那个男人指给他看，他是不会注意到那把椅子的。

source: He led them into a stone kitchen, fitted with coppers for dressing the prison food, and pointed to a door. There was an open grating above it, through which came the sound of men’s voices, mingled with the noise of hammering, and the throwing down of boards. They were putting up the scaffold.

source: “Go!” exclaimed the Dodger. “Why, where’s your spirit? Don’t you take any pride out of yourself? Would you go and be dependent on your friends?”
domestication: "快走！”小偷大声说，“怎么了，你没骨气吗？你就不为自己着想吗？难道你就要指望朋友接济你一辈子吗？”

source: Who can describe the pleasure and delight, the peace of mind and soft tranquillity, the sickly boy felt in the balmy air, and among the green hills and rich woods, of an inland village! Who can tell how scenes of peace and quietude sink into the minds of pain-worn dwellers in close and noisy places, and carry their own freshness, deep into their jaded hearts! Men who have lived in crowded, pent-up streets, through lives of toil, and who have never wished for change; men, to whom custom has indeed been second nature, and who have come almost to love each brick and stone that formed the narrow boundaries of their daily walks; even they, with the hand of death upon them, have been known to yearn at last for one short glimpse of Nature’s face; and, carried far from the scenes of their old pains and pleasures, have seemed to pass at once into a new state of being. Crawling forth, from day to day, to some green sunny spot, they have had such memories wakened up within them by the sight of the sky, and hill and plain, and glistening water, that a foretaste of heaven itself has soothed their quick decline, and they have sunk into their tombs, as peacefully as the sun whose setting they watched from their lonely chamber window but a few hours before, faded from their dim and feeble sight! The memories which peaceful country scenes call up, are not of this world, nor of its thoughts and hopes. Their gentle influence may teach us how to weave fresh garlands for the graves of those we loved: may purify our thoughts, and bear down before it old enmity and hatred; but beneath all this, there lingers, in the least reflective mind, a vague and half-formed consciousness of having held such feelings long before, in some remote and distant time, which calls up solemn thoughts of distant times to come, and bends down pride and worldliness beneath it.
domestication: 在一座内陆村庄中，谁能体会到那股如沐春风般的清新空气、连绵的青山和郁郁葱葱的森林带来的无比舒适与宁静？对于那些身处闹市喧嚣中、备受折磨的人来说，他们的心灵也会被这种祥和的氛围深深感染，并在疲惫的心中注入一股清新的活力。即使是那些终日在拥挤的街道中奔波劳累的人，也常常会在生命的最后时刻渴望一睹大自然的美丽。被运到远离他们痛苦和快乐的地方后，他们仿佛进入了一个新的生命状态，每天都能从某个阳光明媚的地方爬起来，并在看到蓝天、丘陵和闪闪发光的水面时，唤醒他们心中久违的回忆，这种回忆让他们在临死之前仿佛得到了天堂的滋养，让他们在最后的时刻安然辞世，就像夕阳在他们孤独的房间窗口缓缓落下，慢慢从他们模糊的视野中消失一般。 和平的乡村景色唤起的回忆并不是来自这个世界，也不是它的思绪和希望。它们的温和影响可以教我们如何为我们所爱的人的坟墓编织新的花环，可以净化我们的思绪，抹去旧有的仇恨和恩怨，但是在这之下，每个人的心里都会有一种模糊而未成形的意识，觉得自己很久以前就曾经有过这样的感觉，仿佛这是来自遥远过去的记忆，而这种感觉又会引发对未来的沉思，压制我们心中的骄傲和世俗之气。
foreignization: 谁能描述出病弱的男孩在内陆村庄中，呼吸着温暖的空气，周围是绿色的山丘和茂密的树林时，所感到的快乐和愉悦、心灵的宁静和柔和的宁谧？谁能说出那些在拥挤嘈杂的地方居住多年、饱受折磨的人们，当他们看到宁静与安逸的景象时，这些景象如何深深地印在他们的心中，并将新鲜感带入他们疲惫的心灵？即使是那些一生居住在拥挤狭窄的街道上，辛苦劳作，从未渴望改变的人；即使是那些习惯已经成为第二天性的人，他们几乎爱上了每一块每一颗石头，它们构成了他们日常散步的狭窄边界；即使是这些人，当死亡之手降临在他们身上时，也会最终渴望最后一眼瞥见大自然的面容。当他们被带离了旧日痛苦和快乐的场景时，他们似乎一下子进入了新的存在状态。他们每天从阴影中爬出，来到某个阳光明媚的绿色地点，由于看到天空、山丘、平原和闪闪发光的水面，他们内心便会唤起这样的回忆，以至于天堂的预兆抚慰了他们快速的衰落，他们死去时就像太阳在他们孤独的房间窗口几小时前落下一般，平静地消失在他们黯淡无力的视野中！ 和平的乡村景色唤起的记忆，不属于这个世界，也不属于它的思想和希望。 它们温和的影响可能会教我们如何为我们所爱的人的坟墓编织新的花环；可能净化我们的思想，打破旧有的敌意和仇恨；但是，在这一切之下，在最不反思的脑海中，仍然有一种模糊而未完全形成的意识，那就是我们曾经在遥远而久远的过去就拥有过这样的感觉，这种意识唤起了遥远时代的严肃思考，并且压抑了骄傲和世俗之心。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Who can describe the pleasure and delight, the peace of mind and soft tranquillity, the sickly boy felt in the balmy air, and among the green hills and rich woods, of an inland village! Who can tell how scenes of peace and quietude sink into the minds of pain-worn dwellers in close and noisy places, and carry their own freshness, deep into their jaded hearts! Men who have lived in crowded, pent-up streets, through lives of toil, and who have never wished for change; men, to whom custom has indeed been second nature, and who have come almost to love each brick and stone that formed the narrow boundaries of their daily walks; even they, with the hand of death upon them, have been known to yearn at last for one short glimpse of Nature’s face; and, carried far from the scenes of their old pains and pleasures, have seemed to pass at once into a new state of being. Crawling forth, from day to day, to some green sunny spot, they have had such memories wakened up within them by the sight of the sky, and hill and plain, and glistening water, that a foretaste of heaven itself has soothed their quick decline, and they have sunk into their tombs, as peacefully as the sun whose setting they watched from their lonely chamber window but a few hours before, faded from their dim and feeble sight! The memories which peaceful country scenes call up, are not of this world, nor of its thoughts and hopes. Their gentle influence may teach us how to weave fresh garlands for the graves of those we loved: may purify our thoughts, and bear down before it old enmity and hatred; but beneath all this, there lingers, in the least reflective mind, a vague and half-formed consciousness of having held such feelings long before, in some remote and distant time, which calls up solemn thoughts of distant times to come, and bends down pride and worldliness beneath it.

source: Rose Maylie lingered, but the old gentleman drew her arm through his, and led her, with gentle force, away. As they disappeared, the girl sunk down nearly at her full length upon one of the stone stairs, and vented the anguish of her heart in bitter tears.
domestication: 罗斯·梅丽留恋地徘徊，但那位老先生拉着她的手臂，用轻柔的力量把她带走了。他们渐渐消失在视野中，那女孩瘫倒在地，趴在一层石阶上，痛心疾首地哭泣。她心如刀割，痛苦万分，眼泪像断线珍珠一样直往下掉。
foreignization: 罗丝·梅丽留恋地徘徊，但老绅士把她的手臂穿过他的手臂，带着她，用温和的力量，离开了。当他们消失在视线中时，女孩几乎整个身子都趴在其中一层石阶上，并且把她心中的痛苦通过伤心的眼泪发泄出来。

messages_domestication.content:

Rose Maylie lingered, but the old gentleman drew her arm through his, and led her, with gentle force, away. As they disappeared, the girl sunk down nearly at her full length upon one of the stone stairs, and vented the anguish of her heart in bitter tears.

source: “Because they’re of no use to me, my dear,” replied the Jew, with some confusion, “not worth the taking. Their looks convict ’em when they get into trouble, and I lose ’em all. With this boy, properly managed, my dears, I could do what I couldn’t with twenty of them. Besides,” said the Jew, recovering his self-possession, “he has us now if he could only give us leg-bail again; and he must be in the same boat with us. Never mind how he came there; it’s quite enough for my power over him that he was in a robbery; that’s all I want. Now, how much better this is, than being obliged to put the poor leetle boy out of the way—which would be dangerous, and we should lose by it besides.”
domestication: 这帮孩子对我来说都是没用的，亲爱的，我是这么想的。他们长得实在太招眼了，一旦惹上麻烦，他们就会露馅儿，我也就白忙一场。要是能好好利用这个男孩，我觉得我可以从他身上得到更多的好处，而不需要再去找其他二十个孩子。另外，这个孩子现在已经跟我们有牵连了，他逃跑了也还是我们的。至于他是怎么被抓到的，谁管那些呢？他参与了抢劫的事实就足以让我对他有所掌控了。这样一来，我们还能少操点心，也不会因为处理掉他而吃亏。
foreignization: “因为他们对我来说毫无用处，亲爱的，”犹太人回答道，带有一些混乱，“不值得拿走。当他们遇到麻烦时，他们的长相会使他们有罪，我都失去了他们。有了这个男孩，如果正确地管理，我可以做到我以前用二十个也做不到的事情。另外，”犹太人恢复了镇定，“他现在已经和我们在一起了，如果他只能再次给我们保释的话；他必须和我们在同一条船上。别管他怎么到了那里；他参与了抢劫，对我来说已经足够了，这就是我想要的。现在，这比被迫除掉可怜的小男孩——那将是危险的，我们也会因此损失——要好得多。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Because they’re of no use to me, my dear,” replied the Jew, with some confusion, “not worth the taking. Their looks convict ’em when they get into trouble, and I lose ’em all. With this boy, properly managed, my dears, I could do what I couldn’t with twenty of them. Besides,” said the Jew, recovering his self-possession, “he has us now if he could only give us leg-bail again; and he must be in the same boat with us. Never mind how he came there; it’s quite enough for my power over him that he was in a robbery; that’s all I want. Now, how much better this is, than being obliged to put the poor leetle boy out of the way—which would be dangerous, and we should lose by it besides.”

这帮孩子对我来说都是没用的，亲爱的，我是这么想的。他们长得实在太招眼了，一旦惹上麻烦，他们就会露馅儿，我也就白忙一场。要是能好好利用这个男孩，我觉得我可以从他身上得到更多的好处，而不需要再去找其他二十个孩子。另外，这个孩子现在已经跟我们有牵连了，他逃跑了也还是我们的。至于他是怎么被抓到的，谁管那些呢？他参与了抢劫的事实就足以让我对他有所掌控了。这样一来，我们还能少操点心，也不会因为处理掉他而吃亏。

source: “Say it again, you wile, owdacious fellow!” said Mr. Bumble. “How dare you mention such a thing, sir? And how dare you encourage him, you insolent minx? Kiss her!” exclaimed Mr. Bumble, in strong indignation. “Faugh!”
domestication: "你再说一遍，你这个狡猾的家伙！”邦布尔先生怒喝道。“你怎么敢提这种事，先生？你凭什么支持他，你这个厚颜无耻的女人？亲她！”邦布尔先生愤怒地大喊。“呸！”
foreignization: “再说一次，你这个狡猾、厚颜无耻的家伙！”班布尔先生说。“你怎么敢提起这样的事情，先生？你又怎么敢鼓励他，你这个放肆的坏女人？亲她！”班布尔先生愤怒地大声喊道。“呸！”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Say it again, you wile, owdacious fellow!” said Mr. Bumble. “How dare you mention such a thing, sir? And how dare you encourage him, you insolent minx? Kiss her!” exclaimed Mr. Bumble, in strong indignation. “Faugh!”
"你再说一遍，你这个狡猾的家伙！”邦布尔先生怒喝道。“你怎么敢提这种事，先生？你凭什么支持他，你这个厚颜无耻的女人？亲她！”邦布尔先生愤怒地大喊。“呸！”

“Say it again, you wile, owdacious fellow!” said Mr. Bumble. “How dare you mention such a thing, sir? And how dare you encourage him, you insolent minx? Kiss her!” exclaimed Mr. Bumble, in strong indignation. “Faugh!”
“再说一次，你这个狡猾、厚颜无耻的家伙！”班布尔先生说。“你怎么敢提起这样的事情，先生？你又怎么敢鼓励他，你这个放肆的坏女人？亲她！”班布尔先生愤怒地大声喊道。“呸！”

source: The landlord reciprocated the old man’s laugh; and returned to his guests. The Jew was no sooner alone, than his countenance resumed its former expression of anxiety and thought. After a brief reflection, he called a hack-cabriolet, and bade the man drive towards Bethnal Green. He dismissed him within some quarter of a mile of Mr. Sikes’s residence, and performed the short remainder of the distance on foot.
domestication: 房东也跟着老头儿笑了几声，然后回到客人身边。犹太人一旦独自待着，脸上又恢复了刚才的紧张和沉思的表情。稍微思考了一会儿，他叫来一辆出租马车，告诉车夫往白教堂区方向走。他在离斯ай克斯先生住处约有四分之一英里的地方让车夫停下车，然后自己步行走完剩下的路。
foreignization: 房东回应老人的笑声，回到他的客人身边。犹太人一旦独自一人，他的表情就恢复了以前担心和思考的神情。经过短暂的思考后，他叫了一辆出租马车，让车夫朝着贝斯纳尔格林方向驶去。他在距离赛克斯先生的住所不到四分之一英里的地方打发了车夫，剩下的路程他步行走完。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

He dismissed him within some quarter of a mile of Mr. Sikes’s residence, and performed the short remainder of the distance on foot.

After a brief reflection, he called a hack-cabriolet, and bade the man drive towards Bethnal Green. 房东回应老人的笑声，回到他的客人身边。犹太人一旦独自一人，他的表情就恢复了以前担心和思考的神情。经过短暂的思考后，他叫了一辆出租马车，让车夫朝着贝斯纳尔格林方向驶去。他在距离赛克斯先生的住所不到四分之一英里的地方打发了车夫，剩下的路程他步行走完。

source: Oliver was precisely in this condition. He saw the Jew with his half-closed eyes; heard his low whistling; and recognised the sound of the spoon grating against the saucepan’s sides: and yet the self-same senses were mentally engaged, at the same time, in busy action with almost everybody he had ever known.
domestication: 奥利弗正处于这种状态。他看到了那个半闭着眼睛的犹太人，听到了他低声地吹口哨，还认出了勺子刮着锅边的声音。然而，他的感官却同时在忙碌地处理着几乎所有他曾经认识的人和事。他的脑海里一片混乱，各种记忆和印象纷纷涌现。
foreignization: 奥利弗正处于这种状态。他用几乎闭上的眼睛看到犹太人，听到他低声的口哨声，并且认出了勺子刮着平底锅侧面的声音：然而，同样的感官在同一时间也在忙碌地与他几乎所有认识的人进行精神交往。

source: “Always the way!” muttered the Jew to himself as he turned homeward. “The worst of these women is, that a very little thing serves to call up some long-forgotten feeling; and, the best of them is, that it never lasts. Ha! ha! The man against the child, for a bag of gold!”
domestication: 这就是事物的常规啊！那个犹太人自言自语地嘟囔着，转身回家了。他心里想，这些女人最糟糕的地方是，她们动不动就因为一些小事而旧情复燃，但她们最好的地方是，那种感觉永远也不会持续太久。唉，为了一个钱袋，成年人和孩子反目成仇，实在令人可笑！
foreignization: “老样子！”那个犹太人自言自语地转身回家。“这些女人最糟糕的地方在于，一点小事就能唤起她们某些被遗忘已久的感受；而她们最好的地方在于，这种感受永远不会持久。哈！哈！男人与孩子相对立，只为一袋黄金！”

source: It was but an instant, a glance, a flash, before his eyes; and they were gone. But they had recognised him, and he them; and their look was as firmly impressed upon his memory, as if it had been deeply carved in stone, and set before him from his birth. He stood transfixed for a moment; then, leaping from the window into the garden, called loudly for help.
domestication: 这只是一瞬间的印象，一瞥之间，一闪而过的景象就在他眼前消失了。但是他们彼此都已经相互认出了对方，而那一眼的交汇就像是在他的记忆上深深刻下了一道痕迹，就好像是一块石头从他出生开始就摆在他面前一样。他被定住了一会儿，然后一下子从窗户跳进了花园，大声呼救。
foreignization: 那只是一瞬间，一眼，闪光，在他面前；然后他们消失了。但是他们认出了他，他也认出了他们；他们的目光被深深地印在他的记忆中，就好像它被深深地刻在石头上，并且从他出生时就摆在他面前一样。他僵立了一会儿；然后，从窗户跳到花园里，大声呼救。

source: “Thank Heaven upon your knees, dear lady,” cried the girl, “that you had friends to care for and keep you in your childhood, and that you were never in the midst of cold and hunger, and riot and drunkenness, and—and—something worse than all—as I have been from my cradle. I may use the word, for the alley and the gutter were mine, as they will be my deathbed.”
domestication: "谢天谢地，你有亲人疼爱你，关心你，从小就让你过着幸福的日子，”那个女孩哭喊道，“而我从出生开始，就一直身处困苦之中，饥寒交迫，整日在暴力和醉酒中度日，还有比这些更糟糕的东西。这种生活环境就是我的摇篮，也终将成为我的葬身之地。”
foreignization: “亲爱的夫人，”那个女孩哭喊道，“感谢上苍，你有朋友在你的童年时期照顾你和保护你，你从来没有在寒冷和饥饿、喧闹和醉酒之中度日，和——和——比这一切都更糟糕的事情——就像我从摇篮里开始就一直经历的那样，我可以使用这个词，因为小巷和污水沟属于我，就像它们也将成为我的死亡之地一样。”

I may use the word, for the alley and the gutter were mine, as they will be my deathbed.”
"谢天谢地，你有亲人疼爱你，关心你，从小就让你过着幸福的日子，”那个女孩哭喊道，“而我从出生开始，就一直身处困苦之中，饥寒交迫，整日在暴力和醉酒中度日，还有比这些更糟糕的东西。这种生活环境就是我的摇篮，也终将成为我的葬身之地。”

source: “Bill had him on his back, and scudded like the wind. We stopped to take him between us; his head hung down, and he was cold. They were close upon our heels; every man for himself, and each from the gallows! We parted company, and left the youngster lying in a ditch. Alive or dead, that’s all I know about him.”
domestication: 比尔把他扛在背上，飞快地跑着，就像风一样。我们停下来，一起把他从比尔背上接过来，他的头耷拉着，一动不动，已经没了气息。追兵就快追上来了，我们只好各自逃命，否则就只有上断头台这一条路了！于是我们分开了，把那个年轻人丢在了一个沟渠里。他当时是死是活，我就一无所知了。
foreignization: 比尔背着他，像风一样疾驰，我们停下来让他挟在我们之间；他的头垂了下来，他很冷。他们紧随我们的脚后跟，每个人都为自己着想，每个人都为了逃避绞刑架！我们分道扬镳，让那年轻人躺在一个水沟里。不管是活的还是死的，这是我所知道的全部。

source: He found himself jostled among a crowd of people, chiefly women, who were huddled together in a dirty frowsy room, at the upper end of which was a raised platform railed off from the rest, with a dock for the prisoners on the left hand against the wall, a box for the witnesses in the middle, and a desk for the magistrates on the right; the awful locality last named, being screened off by a partition which concealed the bench from the common gaze, and left the vulgar to imagine (if they could) the full majesty of justice.
domestication: 他发现自己被挤在一群人中间，主要是些妇女，都挤在一起，拥挤在一个肮脏的房间里。房间的尽头有一个高台，周围有栏杆隔开，左边靠着墙壁有个囚犯席，中间有个证人席，右边有个审判官的办公桌。这块令人肃然起敬的地方，被一道屏风隔开，挡住了人们对审判席的视线，让那些普通百姓去想象（如果他们能想象的话）法律的庄严。整个场面让人觉得，正义高高在上，难以企及。
foreignization: 他发现自己被挤在一群人中，主要是妇女，她们挤在一起站在一个肮脏凌乱的房间里，在房间的远端有一个高台隔离了其他地方，靠左边墙壁的地方有罪犯的位置，中间有证人席，右边有法官席；最后提到的令人敬畏的地方，被一个隔板遮挡住了，法官席从普通人的视线中隐藏了，留下普通人去想象（如果他们能做到）正义的全部威严。

source: “Stop, stop!” said the old woman in a loud whisper. “Will she be buried tomorrow, or next day, or tonight? I laid her out; and I must walk, you know. Send me a large cloak: a good warm one: for it is bitter cold. We should have cake and wine, too, before we go! Never mind; send some bread—only a loaf of bread and a cup of water. Shall we have some bread, dear?” she said eagerly: catching at the undertaker’s coat, as he once more moved towards the door.
domestication: "慢着，慢着！”老太婆低声说，但声音很大，“是明天埋她，还是后天，或者今晚？我给她收拾停当了，我也得去送葬的，你知道。给我寄一件大衣，一件保暖的，外面冷得很！我们应该在出发前吃块糕点喝杯酒什么的！算了，随便送点面包来就行，一块面包和一杯水就成。我们能不能弄点面包啊，亲爱的？”她问道，急切地抓住殡仪馆的人的衣服，因为他又一次想要往门口走。
foreignization: “住手，住手！”老妇人大声耳语道。“她会在明天被埋葬掉，还是后天，或者今晚？我给她穿上了寿衣；而且我必须走路，你知道的。送给我一件大斗篷：一件温暖的好的斗篷：因为外面很冷。我们应该在出发前吃蛋糕和喝葡萄酒！没关系；只要送一些面包——仅仅一块面包和一杯水就行。我们会有面包吗，亲爱的？”她急切地说着，抓住了殡仪师的外套，当他再次向门口移动时。

source: Mr. Gamfield gave an arch look at the faces round the table, and, observing a smile on all of them, gradually broke into a smile himself. The bargain was made. Mr. Bumble, was at once instructed that Oliver Twist and his indentures were to be conveyed before the magistrate, for signature and approval, that very afternoon.
domestication: 甘姆菲尔德先生对着桌子周围的面孔挤了挤眼，发现每个人都带着笑容，于是自己也慢慢地咧开嘴笑了。成交了。班布尔先生当即接到指示，要在那天下午就将奥利弗·特威斯特和他的契约带到法官面前签字盖章。事情就这么敲定了。班布尔先生肯定乐得合不拢嘴，因为事情比他想象中顺利多了。这笔买卖做得真够快的，大家脸上都洋溢着满意的笑容。
foreignization: 甘姆菲尔德先生用一种傲慢的眼神环视桌子周围的面孔，发现每个人的脸上都浮现出笑容，于是他自己也逐渐露出了笑容。协议达成了。班布尔先生立刻被告知，必须在那天下午将奥利弗·特威斯特和他的学徒合同带到裁判官那里签字并批准。

messages_domestication.content:

Mr. Bumble, was at once instructed that Oliver Twist and his indentures were to be conveyed before the magistrate, for signature and approval, that very afternoon.

source: He closed the lid of the box with a loud crash; and, laying his hand on a bread knife which was on the table, started furiously up. He trembled very much though; for, even in his terror, Oliver could see that the knife quivered in the air.
domestication: 他猛地关上盒子盖，坐在桌边的面包刀引起了他的注意，他突然站起来，气势汹汹。然而，他的手却在颤抖，尽管他害怕得瑟瑟发抖，奥利弗还是能看到他手中的刀在空中剧烈颤动。
foreignization: 他伴随着一声巨响关上了箱子盖，接着，他把手放在桌子上的面包刀上，怒气冲冲地站了起来。他尽管非常颤抖；因为，即使在恐惧之中，奥利弗也能看到那把刀在空中颤动。

source: There was a long pause. Every member of the respectable coterie appeared plunged in his own reflections; not excepting the dog, who by a certain malicious licking of his lips seemed to be meditating an attack upon the legs of the first gentleman or lady he might encounter in the streets when he went out.
domestication: 场面突然变得十分尴尬，一阵长时间的沉默。每个有头有脸的人都愣在那里，陷入了自己的思绪中，连狗也一样，满脸凶相，舔着嘴唇，好像在打主意，等出去后就找第一个碰到的路人下口。
foreignization: 有一段长时间的沉默。受人尊敬的这个圈子里的每个成员似乎都陷入了自己的思考之中；连狗也不例外，它通过某种恶意的舔自己的嘴唇，似乎在思考着，当他出去后，对他遇到的第一个男人或女人采取攻击。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

There was a long pause. Every member of the respectable coterie appeared plunged in his own reflections; not excepting the dog, who by a certain malicious licking of his lips seemed to be meditating an attack upon the legs of the first gentleman or lady he might encounter in the streets when he went out.
场面突然变得十分尴尬，一阵长时间的沉默。每个有头有脸的人都愣在那里，陷入了自己的思绪中，连狗也一样，满脸凶相，舔着嘴唇，好像在打主意，等出去后就找第一个碰到的路人下口。

There was a long pause. Every member of the respectable coterie appeared plunged in his own reflections; not excepting the dog, who by a certain malicious licking of his lips seemed to be meditating an attack upon the legs of the first gentleman or lady he might encounter in the streets when he went out.
有一段长时间的沉默。受人尊敬的这个圈子里的每个成员似乎都陷入了自己的思考之中；连狗也不例外，它通过某种恶意的舔自己的嘴唇，似乎在思考着，当他出去后，对他遇到的第一个男人或女人采取攻击。

source: “But I also know,” pursued the old gentleman, “the misery, the slow torture, the protracted anguish of that ill-assorted union. I know how listlessly and wearily each of that wretched pair dragged on their heavy chain through a world that was poisoned to them both. I know how cold formalities were succeeded by open taunts; how indifference gave place to dislike, dislike to hate, and hate to loathing, until at last they wrenched the clanking bond asunder, and retiring a wide space apart, carried each a galling fragment, of which nothing but death could break the rivets, to hide it in new society beneath the gayest looks they could assume. Your mother succeeded; she forgot it soon. But it rusted and cankered at your father’s heart for years.”
domestication: 老先生继续说，“但我也知道，那对不般配的夫妻的痛苦、那缓慢的折磨和漫长的痛楚。他们拖着沉重的枷锁，在对他们两人来说都已变得荒凉的世界里，毫无生气地、疲惫地过着日子。我知道，他们之间的冷漠礼貌逐渐变成了公开的讽刺，冷漠变成了厌恶，厌恶变成了仇恨，而仇恨又变成了憎恶，直到最后，他们终于打破了那响当当的锁链，退到远处，各自带着一块残片，这块残片只有一种东西能打破它的铆钉——那就是死亡。他们将这块残片藏在新的社交圈中，戴上最灿烂的面具遮盖。你的母亲成功做到了这一点，她很快就忘记了。但是，对于你的父亲来说，那却像一根铁钉一样深深地扎进他的心中，长久地生锈和溃烂，历经多年。”
foreignization: “但我也知道，”老先生继续说，“那不相配的结合所带来的痛苦、缓慢的折磨和长期的痛楚。我知道那对可怜的夫妇中每一个人是如何无精打采而又疲惫地拖着沉重的锁链穿过对他们两人来说都是有毒的世界。我知道他们是如何由冷漠的礼貌转变为公开的嘲讽，如何从漠不关心转变为厌恶，再由厌恶转变为憎恨，直到最后他们将叮当作响的锁链撕裂，退到远处各自带着一段刺激的碎片（除了死之外，没有什么能打破它的铆钉），并在新社会下假装最开朗的面孔来隐藏它。你的母亲成功了，她很快就忘记了。但这却使你父亲的心在多年里生锈和溃烂。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“But I also know,” pursued the old gentleman, “the misery, the slow torture, the protracted anguish of that ill-assorted union. I know how listlessly and wearily each of that wretched pair dragged on their heavy chain through a world that was poisoned to them both. I know how cold formalities were succeeded by open taunts; how indifference gave place to dislike, dislike to hate, and hate to loathing, until at last they wrenched the clanking bond asunder, and retiring a wide space apart, carried each a galling fragment, of which nothing but death could break the rivets, to hide it in new society beneath the gayest looks they could assume. Your mother succeeded; she forgot it soon. But it rusted and cankered at your father’s heart for years.”

老先生继续说，“但我也知道，那对不般配的夫妻的痛苦、那缓慢的折磨和漫长的痛楚。他们拖着沉重的枷锁，在对他们两人来说都已变得荒凉的世界里，毫无生气地、疲惫地过着日子。我知道，他们之间的冷漠礼貌逐渐变成了公开的讽刺，冷漠变成了厌恶，厌恶变成了仇恨，而仇恨又变成了憎恶，直到最后，他们终于打破了那响当当的锁链，退到远处，各自带着一块残片，这块残片只有一种东西能打破它的铆钉——那就是死亡。他们将这块残片藏在新的社交圈中，戴上最灿烂的面具遮盖。你的母亲成功做到了这一点，她很快就忘记了。但是，对于你的父亲来说，那却像一根铁钉一样深深地扎进他的心中，长久地生锈和溃烂，历经多年。”

“但我也知道，”老先生继续说，“那不相配的结合所带来的痛苦、缓慢的折磨和长期的痛楚。我知道那对可怜的夫妇中每一个人是如何无精打采而又疲惫地拖着沉重的锁链穿过对他们两人来说都是有毒的世界。我知道他们是如何由冷漠的礼貌转变为公开的嘲讽，如何从漠不关心转变为厌恶，再由厌恶转变为憎恨，直到最后他们将叮当作响的锁链撕裂，退到远处各自带着一段刺激的碎片（除了死之外，没有什么能打破它的铆钉），并在新社会下假装最开朗的面孔来隐藏它。你的母亲成功了，她很快就忘记了。但这却使你父亲的心在多年里生锈和溃烂。”

source: “Ha! ha! my dear,” replied the Jew, “you must get up very early in the morning, to win against the Dodger.”
domestication: “哈哈，我的好朋友，”那个犹太人回应道，“你得比早起的鸟儿还要早才行，才能在与 Dodger 的较量中获胜。”

source: “Morning!” said Charley Bates; “you must put your boots on over-night, and have a telescope at each eye, and a opera-glass between your shoulders, if you want to come over him.”
domestication: “早上好！”查理·贝茨说，“要是你想抓住他，你就得晚上穿上靴子，在每只眼前架个望远镜，在肩膀之间架个戏剧观察镜。”大概这就是查理·贝茨的意思吧，不过话说回来，这家伙说的话也真够没逻辑的。简单说，他是叫人注意提防那家伙，否则就有可能被他给骗了。

source: “Thus. It is quite clear that we shall have extreme difficulty in getting to the bottom of this mystery, unless we can bring this man, Monks, upon his knees. That can only be done by stratagem, and by catching him when he is not surrounded by these people. For, suppose he were apprehended, we have no proof against him. He is not even (so far as we know, or as the facts appear to us) concerned with the gang in any of their robberies. If he were not discharged, it is very unlikely that he could receive any further punishment than being committed to prison as a rogue and vagabond; and of course ever afterwards his mouth would be so obstinately closed that he might as well, for our purposes, be deaf, dumb, blind, and an idiot.”
domestication: 这么看来，要想弄清这件蹊跷事的真相，除非我们能让这个叫曼克斯的人低头认错。不然的话，我们就是没法深入调查。要让他低头，只能靠计策，而且必须是在他没有被一堆人围着的时候抓住他。如果他被抓了，我们也没有证据能够指控他。至少就目前我们所知道的和案件情况来看，他似乎与匪帮的任何劫案都没有牵连。如果不放他，他顶多被关进监狱，算是治安犯。但即使这样，他的嘴巴也会被堵得严严实实，以后无论我们怎么审问，他都可能装哑巴，充聋作哑，跟傻子一样。
foreignization: “如此。很明显，我们将会非常困难地揭开这个谜底，除非我们能让这个人，蒙克斯，在我们面前跪下。这只能通过计策来实现，并且当他没有被这些人包围时抓住他。因为，如果他被捕，我们没有任何证据来指控他。他甚至（就我们所知，或事实似乎表明）并不参与帮派的任何抢劫活动。如果他不被释放，很不可能他会受到除了被判入狱为流氓和游民以外的任何进一步惩罚；当然，从那以后，他的嘴巴将会被这么顽固地封闭，以至于对于我们的目的来说，他也许更好是聋的、哑的、盲的和白痴。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Thus. It is quite clear that we shall have extreme difficulty in getting to the bottom of this mystery, unless we can bring this man, Monks, upon his knees. That can only be done by stratagem, and by catching him when he is not surrounded by these people. For, suppose he were apprehended, we have no proof against him. He is not even (so far as we know, or as the facts appear to us) concerned with the gang in any of their robberies. If he were not discharged, it is very unlikely that he could receive any further punishment than being committed to prison as a rogue and vagabond; and of course ever afterwards his mouth would be so obstinately closed that he might as well, for our purposes, be deaf, dumb, blind, and an idiot.”

这么看来，要想弄清这件蹊跷事的真相，除非我们能让这个叫曼克斯的人低头认错。不然的话，我们就是没法深入调查。要让他低头，只能靠计策，而且必须是在他没有被一堆人围着的时候抓住他。如果他被抓了，我们也没有证据能够指控他。至少就目前我们所知道的和案件情况来看，他似乎与匪帮的任何劫案都没有牵连。如果不放他，他顶多被关进监狱，算是治安犯。但即使这样，他的嘴巴也会被堵得严严实实，以后无论我们怎么审问，他都可能装哑巴，充聋作哑，跟傻子一样。
