“Thus. It is quite clear that we shall have extreme difficulty in getting to the bottom of this mystery, unless we can bring this man, Monks, upon his knees. That can only be done by stratagem, and by catching him when he is not surrounded by these people. For, suppose he were apprehended, we have no proof against him. He is not even (so far as we know, or as the facts appear to us) concerned with the gang in any of their robberies. If he were not discharged, it is very unlikely that he could receive any further punishment than being committed to prison as a rogue and vagabond; and of course ever afterwards his mouth would be so obstinately closed that he might as well, for our purposes, be deaf, dumb, blind, and an idiot.”

“如此。很明显，我们将会非常困难地揭开这个谜底，除非我们能让这个人，蒙克斯，在我们面前跪下。这只能通过计策来实现，并且当他没有被这些人包围时抓住他。因为，如果他被捕，我们没有任何证据来指控他。他甚至（就我们所知，或事实似乎表明）并不参与帮派的任何抢劫活动。如果他不被释放，很不可能他会受到除了被判入狱为流氓和游民以外的任何进一步惩罚；当然，从那以后，他的嘴巴将会被这么顽固地封闭，以至于对于我们的目的来说，他也许更好是聋的、哑的、盲的和白痴。”

source: Talking all the way, he followed Mr. Giles upstairs; and while he is going upstairs, the reader may be informed, that Mr. Losberne, a surgeon in the neighbourhood, known through a circuit of ten miles round as “the doctor,” had grown fat, more from good-humour than from good living: and was as kind and hearty, and withal as eccentric an old bachelor, as will be found in five times that space, by any explorer alive.
domestication: 一边聊天，一边跟着盖尔斯先生上了楼。在这期间，让我们顺便说一下，住在附近的外科医生洛斯伯恩先生，在方圆十里之内都被称为“大夫”。他虽然长得很肥胖，但并不是因为贪吃，而是因为他的性格非常好，心情总是很愉快。作为一个老光棍，他不仅非常热情和善良，而且还非常古怪。只要你四处打听一下，就会发现像他这样的人即使在更大的范围内也很难找到了。
foreignization: 一边说话，他跟着吉尔斯先生上了楼；当他上楼的时候，读者可以被告知，洛斯伯恩先生，一位住在附近的外科医生，以“医生”的名义在方圆十英里的范围内闻名：他由于好心情而比由于高尚的生活方式更发福：他是一位和善、热情、而且像任何活着的探险家在五倍的空间里找到的那样古怪的老光棍。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Talking all the way, he followed Mr. Giles upstairs; and while he is going upstairs, the reader may be informed, that Mr. Losberne, a surgeon in the neighbourhood, known through a circuit of ten miles round as “the doctor,” had grown fat, more from good-humour than from good living: and was as kind and hearty, and withal as eccentric an old bachelor, as will be found in five times that space, by any explorer alive.
一边聊天，一边跟着盖尔斯先生上了楼。在这期间，让我们顺便说一下，住在附近的外科医生洛斯伯恩先生，在方圆十里之内都被称为“大夫”。他虽然长得很肥胖，但并不是因为贪吃，而是因为他的性格非常好，心情总是很愉快。作为一个老光棍，他不仅非常热情和善良，而且还非常古怪。只要你四处打听一下，就会发现像他这样的人即使在更大的范围内也很难找到了。

source: “No evil wind at all, my dear, for evil winds blow nobody any good; and I’ve brought something good with me, that you’ll be glad to see. Dodger, my dear, open the bundle; and give Bill the little trifles that we spent all our money on, this morning.”
domestication: “没什么不吉利的事，亲爱的，因为不好的风对谁都没有好处。我们带来了些好东西，你们会很高兴的。快点儿，Dodger，打开包裹，把我们今天早上花了所有钱买的那些小玩意儿给比尔吧。”
foreignization: “一点儿坏风都没有，我的亲爱的，因为坏风不会吹给任何人带来好处；而且我带来了些好东西，你会很高兴看到的。杜贾，我的亲爱的，打开包裹；并把我们今天早上花了所有钱买的小玩意儿送给比尔。”

source: The animal came up from the very force of habit; but as Sikes stooped to attach the handkerchief to his throat, he uttered a low growl and started back.
domestication: 这畜生是出于习惯的驱使才凑了过来，但当斯ай克斯弯腰给他系围巾时，它却低低地咆哮了一声，突然往后退开。

source: “Of many things, Bill,” replied the girl, shivering, and as she did so, pressing her hands upon her eyes. “But, Lord! What odds in that?”

foreignization: "有很多事情， 比尔，” 回答的女孩，发抖， 当她这样做时，按压着她的眼睛。 “但是，主啊！有什么区别呢？”

source: “Oh ah! I’ll come on,” replied the Dodger, brushing his hat with the palm of his hand. “Ah! (to the Bench) it’s no use your looking frightened; I won’t show you no mercy, not a ha’porth of it. You’ll pay for this, my fine fellers. I wouldn’t be you for something! I wouldn’t go free, now, if you was to fall down on your knees and ask me. Here, carry me off to prison! Take me away!”
domestication: “哎呀，我走啦！”Dodger回答道，同时用手掌擦了擦帽子。“唉，你们（看着法官）不要吓得脸色发白，我不会对你们手下留情，一点儿也不。你们会为此付出代价，我的好朋友们。我才不想做你们呢，即使你们跪下来求我，我现在也不愿意被释放。来吧，带我去监狱！把我带走吧！”
foreignization: “噢，啊，我会来的，”小偷回答道，他用手掌擦着帽子。“啊！（对审判席说）你们不用害怕；我不会对你们仁慈，连一便士的价值都不会的。你会为此付出代价，我的好家伙们。我不会用什么东西来交换当你们的位置。我现在即使你们跪下求我，也不会选择自由。这里，带我去监狱吧！把我带走！”

source: Now, it so happened that Mr. Fang was at that moment perusing a leading article in a newspaper of the morning, adverting to some recent decision of his, and commending him, for the three hundred and fiftieth time, to the special and particular notice of the Secretary of State for the Home Department. He was out of temper; and he looked up with an angry scowl.
domestication: 那时，方先生刚好在看早上的报纸，里面有一篇社论正在讨论他最近做出的某个决定，并且已经是第三百五十次把他推荐给内政部的国务卿。方先生心情不好，看起来很不爽，抬头怒视着。
foreignization: 这时，方先生碰巧在看那天早上的报纸上的一篇社论，提到他最近的一个判决，并且是第 三百五十次向内政大臣推荐他。他心情很不好，带着愤怒的皱眉抬起头来。

Now, it so happened that Mr. Fang was at that moment perusing a leading article in a newspaper of the morning, adverting to some recent decision of his, and commending him, for the three hundred and fiftieth time, to the special and particular notice of the Secretary of State for the Home Department. He was out of temper; and he looked up with an angry scowl.
这时，方先生碰巧在看那天早上的报纸上的一篇社论，提到他最近的一个判决，并且是第 三百五十次向内政大臣推荐他。他心情很不好，带着愤怒的皱眉抬起头来。

source: After receiving an assurance from both, that she might safely do so, she proceeded in a voice so low that it was often difficult for the listener to discover even the purport of what she said, to describe, by name and situation, the public-house whence she had been followed that night. From the manner in which she occasionally paused, it appeared as if the gentleman were making some hasty notes of the information she communicated. When she had thoroughly explained the localities of the place, the best position from which to watch it without exciting observation, and the night and hour on which Monks was most in the habit of frequenting it, she seemed to consider for a few moments, for the purpose of recalling his features and appearances more forcibly to her recollection.
domestication: 她得到双方的保证后才放心，大声说话，但声音始终很低，听者常常难以听清她在说什么。她继续说了下去，提到了那家酒馆的名字和位置，那是在哪一晚跟踪她的地方。从她偶尔停顿的样子来看，似乎那位先生正在匆忙记下她提供的信息。当她详细解释完地点、最佳观察位置以及蒙克斯最常光顾的时间后，她似乎沉思了几秒钟，目的是更好地回忆他的长相和特征。

source: The mud lay thick upon the stones, and a black mist hung over the streets; the rain fell sluggishly down, and everything felt cold and clammy to the touch. It seemed just the night when it befitted such a being as the Jew to be abroad. As he glided stealthily along, creeping beneath the shelter of the walls and doorways, the hideous old man seemed like some loathsome reptile, engendered in the slime and darkness through which he moved: crawling forth, by night, in search of some rich offal for a meal.
domestication: 泥巴厚厚地覆盖在石头上，一团黑雾笼罩着街道；雨水无力地往下滴答，所有东西摸起来都冷冰冰、湿漉漉的。这真是个适合像犹太人这样的鬼鬼祟祟的人出门的夜晚。他鬼鬼祟祟地溜着，缩在墙壁和门道的掩护下，这个丑陋的老家伙活像一只令人恶心的爬虫，从他移动的烂泥和黑暗中诞生，到了晚上出来寻找一些肥美的残渣来吃。
foreignization: 泥土厚厚地覆盖在石头上，一团黑雾笼罩着街道；雨水缓慢地落下，所有东西都感觉摸起来又冷又潮湿。这似乎就是这样一个夜晚，适合像犹太人这样的生物出现在外面。当他悄悄地滑行时，潜伏在墙壁和门道的遮蔽下，这个丑陋的老人似乎像某种令人憎恶的爬行动物，在他移动的烂泥和黑暗中孕育而生：在夜间爬出来，寻找一些富有食物的残渣来吃。

source: “I shall surprise you very much, I have no doubt,” said Rose, naturally embarrassed; “but you once showed great benevolence and goodness to a very dear young friend of mine, and I am sure you will take an interest in hearing of him again.”
domestication: 罗斯说话时有些不好意思，他说：“我想我马上会让你大吃一惊，不过你曾经对我一个很要好的朋友非常好，我相信你还愿意知道他现在怎么样吧。”
foreignization: “我肯定会让你很惊讶，”罗斯带着自然的尴尬说，“但你曾经对一个我非常亲爱的年轻朋友表现出了极大的仁慈和善良，我相信你一定会对再次听到有关他的消息感兴趣。”

source: “I’ll swear I saw it!” replied Monks, trembling. “It was bending forward when I saw it first; and when I spoke, it darted away.”
domestication: 唉，我敢肯定我看到了！蒙克斯说话时全身发抖，吓得够呛。“当时它的身子是弯着的，一看到我就撒腿就跑了。”
foreignization: “我发誓我看到了它！”蒙克斯回答道，他在发抖。“我一开始看到它时，它正向前弯曲；当我说话时，它迅速跑开了。”

source: “Take heed, Oliver! take heed!” said the old man, shaking his right hand before him in a warning manner. “He’s a rough man, and thinks nothing of blood when his own is up. Whatever falls out, say nothing; and do what he bids you. Mind!” Placing a strong emphasis on the last word, he suffered his features gradually to resolve themselves into a ghastly grin, and, nodding his head, left the room.
domestication: "小心点，奥利弗！小心点！”老头儿说着，右手在他面前挥舞着，警告的意味很明显。“他是个粗鲁的人，一旦性情发作，杀人放火都不在话下。无论发生什么事，都别吱声，听他的话就行了，记住了吗？”他强调最后这句话，脸上慢慢浮现出一丝狰狞的笑容，然后点了点头，离开了房间。
foreignization: “当心，奥利弗！当心！”老人说，他在警告的姿势下在自己面前摇晃着他的右手。“他是一个粗鲁的人，当他的怒气上来了，对血腥毫不介意。不管发生什么事，都不要说话；照他说的去做。注意！”他对最后一个字有很强的重音，然后他让他的面部特征逐渐转变成一个骇人的笑容，并点头离开了房间。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Take heed, Oliver! take heed!” said the old man, shaking his right hand before him in a warning manner. “He’s a rough man, and thinks nothing of blood when his own is up. Whatever falls out, say nothing; and do what he bids you. Mind!” Placing a strong emphasis on the last word, he suffered his features gradually to resolve themselves into a ghastly grin, and, nodding his head, left the room.

source: “This,” said the man, stopping in a gloomy passage where a couple of workmen were making some preparations in profound silence—“this is the place he passes through. If you step this way, you can see the door he goes out at.”

source: “Not that infernal hole we were in before,” she could hear the man say as they went upstairs. Fagin laughed; and making some reply which did not reach her, seemed, by the creaking of the boards, to lead his companion to the second story.
domestication: 她能听到那人说“不会又回到我们之前那个破地方吧”，当他们上楼时。斯韦格尔哈哈大笑，接着说了些什么，但她没听清，只听得楼板吱嘎作响，那个人便跟着他上了二楼。
foreignization: “不，不是我们之前待的那个可怕的地方，”她能听到那个人说话，他们边上楼边说。法金笑了；并且做出了一些回应，这些回应没能传到她的耳朵里，似乎通过地板的吱嘎声，带领他的同伴到了二楼。

source: The old man bit his yellow fingers, and meditated for some seconds; his face working with agitation the while, as if he dreaded something, and feared to know the worst. At length he raised his head.
domestication: 老头子咬着他的黄指头，沉思了几秒钟，他的脸在这期间一直因激动而扭曲着，就像他害怕着什么，又担心知道最坏的消息一样。最后，他终于抬起了头。
foreignization: 这老头咬着他的黄手指，沉思了几秒钟；他脸上在此期间因激动而扭曲着，就好像他害怕某件事情，并且害怕知道最坏的结果。最后他抬起了头。

source: But Mr. Bumble was cunning enough; and he at once saw that an opportunity was opened, for the lucrative disposal of some secret in the possession of his better half. He well remembered the night of old Sally’s death, which the occurrences of that day had given him good reason to recollect, as the occasion on which he had proposed to Mrs. Corney; and although that lady had never confided to him the disclosure of which she had been the solitary witness, he had heard enough to know that it related to something that had occurred in the old woman’s attendance, as workhouse nurse, upon the young mother of Oliver Twist. Hastily calling this circumstance to mind, he informed the stranger, with an air of mystery, that one woman had been closeted with the old harridan shortly before she died; and that she could, as he had reason to believe, throw some light on the subject of his inquiry.
domestication: 班布尔先生可不是个傻瓜，他马上看出有机可乘，可以利用他老婆手中的某些秘密来大赚一笔。他清清楚楚地记得老萨莉去世的那晚，那天发生的事让他不禁回想起自己向科尼夫人求婚的往事。尽管科尼夫人从未透露她亲眼目睹过的秘密，但他还是从言谈举止中知道了些风声，了解到这件事与老太太在育婴堂做护士时有关，特别是与奥利弗·特威斯特的母亲有关。班布尔先生赶紧在脑子里搜索相关细节，然后神秘地告诉陌生人，有一个女人在老太太去世前不久曾经和她单独待在一起，而这个女人可能对他的询问有所了解。
foreignization: 但是，邦布尔先生足够狡猾；他一下子就看到了一个机会，可以用他妻子所拥有的某个秘密来获取利益。他清楚地记得老萨莉去世的那一夜，当天发生的事情让他有充分的理由回忆起那是他向科尼夫人求婚的场合；尽管科尼夫人从未向他透露过她亲眼目睹的那些事情，但他听到的足以知道那与老妇人作为救济院的护士照顾奥利弗·特威斯特的年轻母亲有关。当他迅速回想起这一情况后，他以一种神秘的态度告诉陌生人，一位女性曾在老妇人去世前不久与其密谈；他有理由相信，这位女性可以对他的询问主題提供一些线索。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

But Mr. Bumble was cunning enough; and he at once saw that an opportunity was opened, for the lucrative disposal of some secret in the possession of his better half. He well remembered the night of old Sally’s death, which the occurrences of that day had given him good reason to recollect, as the occasion on which he had proposed to Mrs. Corney; and although that lady had never confided to him the disclosure of which she had been the solitary witness, he had heard enough to know that it related to something that had occurred in the old woman’s attendance, as workhouse nurse, upon the young mother of Oliver Twist. Hastily calling this circumstance to mind, he informed the stranger, with an air of mystery, that one woman had been closeted with the old harridan shortly before she died; and that she could, as he had reason to believe, throw some light on the subject of his inquiry.

班布尔先生可不是个傻瓜，他马上看出有机可乘，可以利用他老婆手中的某些秘密来大赚一笔。他清清楚楚地记得老萨莉去世的那晚，那天发生的事让他不禁回想起自己向科尼夫人求婚的往事。尽管科尼夫人从未透露她亲眼目睹过的秘密，但他还是从言谈举止中知道了些风声，了解到这件事与老太太在育婴堂做护士时有关，特别是与奥利弗·特威斯特的母亲有关。班布尔先生赶紧在脑子里搜索相关细节，然后神秘地告诉陌生人，有一个女人在老太太去世前不久曾经和她单独待在一起，而这个女人可能对他的询问有所了解。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

But Mr. Bumble was cunning enough; and he at once saw that an opportunity was opened, for the lucrative disposal of some secret in the possession of his better half. He well remembered the night of old Sally’s death, which the occurrences of that day had given him good reason to recollect, as the occasion on which he had proposed to Mrs. Corney; and although that lady had never confided to him the disclosure of which she had been the solitary witness, he had heard enough to know that it related to something that had occurred in the old woman’s attendance, as workhouse nurse, upon the young mother of Oliver Twist. Hastily calling this circumstance to mind, he informed the stranger, with an air of mystery, that one woman had been closeted with the old harridan shortly before she died; and that she could, as he had reason to believe, throw some light on the subject of his inquiry.

但是，邦布尔先生足够狡猾；他一下子就看到了一个机会，可以用他妻子所拥有的某个秘密来获取利益。他清楚地记得老萨莉去世的那一夜，当天发生的事情让他有充分的理由回忆起那是他向科尼夫人求婚的场合；尽管科尼夫人从未向他透露过她亲眼目睹的那些事情，但他听到的足以知道那与老妇人作为救济院的护士照顾奥利弗·特威斯特的年轻母亲有关。当他迅速回想起这一情况后，他以一种神秘的态度告诉陌生人，一位女性曾在老妇人去世前不久与其密谈；他有理由相信，这位女性可以对他的询问主題提供一些线索。

source: This mad excitement over, there returned, with ten-fold force, the dreadful consciousness of his crime. He looked suspiciously about him, for the men were conversing in groups, and he feared to be the subject of their talk. The dog obeyed the significant beck of his finger, and they drew off, stealthily, together. He passed near an engine where some men were seated, and they called to him to share in their refreshment. He took some bread and meat; and as he drank a draught of beer, heard the firemen, who were from London, talking about the murder. “He has gone to Birmingham, they say,” said one: “but they’ll have him yet, for the scouts are out, and by tomorrow night there’ll be a cry all through the country.”
domestication: 那阵狂热过去后，他又感到自己的罪行带来的恐惧，这种感觉变得更加强烈。他怀疑地环顾四周，发现那些人都聚集在一起议论纷纷，他担心他们正在谈论自己。狗察觉到他手势的意思，于是他们一起偷偷地离开了。途经一台机车旁边，有些男人坐在那里，他们邀请他加入他们，分享点吃的。他拿了一些面包和肉，喝了一口啤酒，听到来自伦敦的消防员们在讨论谋杀案。“他们说凶手已经逃到了伯明翰，”其中一个人说，“但他们最终还是会抓到他的，因为警方已经开始搜索，到了明晚，整个国家都会知道这件事。”
foreignization: 这种疯狂的激动过去后，他的犯罪意识又以十倍的力量回来了。他怀疑地环顾四周，因为那些男人聚集在一起交谈，他担心自己会成为他们谈话的对象。狗服从了他手指的暗示，他们偷偷地一起离开。他走过一个发动机旁，里面有一些男人坐在那里，他们叫他来分享他们的饮食。他拿了一些面包和肉；当他喝了一口啤酒时，听到了来自伦敦的消防员们正在谈论谋杀。“人们说他已经去伯明翰了，”其中一个人说：“但他们最终还是会抓到他的，因为侦察员已经出发，到明晚整个国家就会发出呼喊。”

source: Although Oliver did as he was desired, at once; and passed the back of his unoccupied hand briskly across his eyes, he left a tear in them when he looked up at his conductor. As Mr. Bumble gazed sternly upon him, it rolled down his cheek. It was followed by another, and another. The child made a strong effort, but it was an unsuccessful one. Withdrawing his other hand from Mr. Bumble’s he covered his face with both; and wept until the tears sprung out from between his chin and bony fingers.
domestication: 奥利弗照做了，但当他抬头看着带他的那个人时，他的眼睛里还是掉下了一滴眼泪。当邦布尔先生严厉地盯着他时，那滴眼泪顺着他的脸颊流下来。接着又掉下了几滴。这个孩子努力想忍住，但没成功。他抽回另一只手捂住脸，眼泪从下巴和骨瘦如柴的手指缝里直往外流。
foreignization: 虽然奥利弗照做了；他用闲着的一只手在眼睛上快速擦了一下，当他抬头看着他的引路人时，他的眼中仍然含着泪水。 当班布尔先生严厉地注视着他时，眼泪顺着他的脸颊流下来。 又有一滴眼泪接着落下，又有一滴眼泪接着落下。 孩子尽了最大的努力，但没有成功。 他把另一只手从班布尔先生手中抽出来，用两只手捂住脸，哭泣着，眼泪从他的下巴和骨瘦如柴的手指缝里渗了出来。

source: “But, yer see,” observed Noah, “as she will be able to do a good deal, I should like to take something very light.”
domestication: “我觉得，”诺亚说，“既然她可以帮上大忙，那我就想找点轻活儿干吧。”
foreignization: “但是，你看”，诺亚观察到，“由于她能够做很多事情，我想找点轻活儿。”

messages_domestication.content:

“But, yer see,” observed Noah, “as she will be able to do a good deal, I should like to take something very light.”
“我觉得，”诺亚说，“既然她可以帮上大忙，那我就想找点轻活儿干吧。”

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“But, yer see,” observed Noah, “as she will be able to do a good deal, I should like to take something very light.”
“但是，你看”，诺亚观察到，“由于她能够做很多事情，我想找点轻活儿。”

source: As the other two were impressed with a similar presentiment; and as their blood, like his, had all gone down again; some speculation ensued upon the cause of this sudden change in their temperament.
domestication: 另外两个人也产生了同样的预感，他们的血气像他一样又都沉了下去。他们觉得很奇怪，这时候就开始纷纷议论起这突然变化的原因来了。
foreignization: 当另外两个人也同样被一种预感所震撼；并且他们的血液像他一样都回流了下去；关于这种突然变化在他们性情中的原因的一些推测随之而来。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

As the other two were impressed with a similar presentiment; and as their blood, like his, had all gone down again; some speculation ensued upon the cause of this sudden change in their temperament.
另外两个人也产生了同样的预感，他们的血气像他一样又都沉了下去。他们觉得很奇怪，这时候就开始纷纷议论起这突然变化的原因来了。

source: But his heart was heavy, notwithstanding; and he wished, as he crept into his narrow bed, that that were his coffin, and that he could be lain in a calm and lasting sleep in the churchyard ground, with the tall grass waving gently above his head, and the sound of the old deep bell to soothe him in his sleep.
domestication: 尽管如此，他的心还是沉重得很，当他钻进那张窄床时，他真希望那张床就是他的棺材，自己能被安葬在教堂的墓地里，在一片高高的草丛中安然长眠，草叶在头顶轻轻摇曳，老钟发出的低沉声音令他进入甜美的梦乡。这样也好，至少能解脱了这烦恼的生活。
foreignization: 但他的心依然很沉重；当他溜进狭窄的床时，他希望那就是他的棺材，并且他能够被放在教堂墓地里一个平静而持久的睡眠中，高高的草轻轻地在他头上摇摆，而老旧深沉的钟声能在他睡眠中安慰他。

但他的心依然很沉重；当他溜进狭窄的床时，他希望那就是他的棺材，并且他能够被放在教堂墓地里一个平静而持久的睡眠中，高高的草轻轻地在他头上摇摆，而老旧深沉的钟声能在他睡眠中安慰他。

source: Oliver soon recovering from the fainting-fit into which Mr. Brownlow’s abrupt exclamation had thrown him, the subject of the picture was carefully avoided, both by the old gentleman and Mrs. Bedwin, in the conversation that ensued: which indeed bore no reference to Oliver’s history or prospects, but was confined to such topics as might amuse without exciting him. He was still too weak to get up to breakfast; but, when he came down into the housekeeper’s room next day, his first act was to cast an eager glance at the wall, in the hope of again looking on the face of the beautiful lady. His expectations were disappointed, however, for the picture had been removed.
domestication: 奥利弗从晕倒中很快恢复过来，他听到了布朗洛先生的突然爆发，这让他感到很意外。在随后的交谈中，老先生和贝德温夫人都小心地避开了关于那幅画的讨论。他们的话题转移到了一些无关紧要的事情上，比如天气、新闻或者其他一些无伤大雅的事，完全不涉及奥利弗的过去或者未来的计划。由于身体还没有恢复，奥利弗仍然无法起床吃早餐。但是，当他第二天下楼到管家室的时候，他第一件事就是急切地朝墙壁看去，希望能够再次见到那位美丽女士的面容。然而，他失望了，因为那幅画已经被移走了。
foreignization: 奥利弗很快从昏厥中恢复过来，昏厥是布朗洛先生突然的惊呼所引起的，随后在随之而来的交谈中，老绅士和贝德温夫人都小心地避开了关于那幅画的讨论：这种讨论确实与奥利弗的历史或前景无关，而是局限于那些可能会逗乐他而不至于使他激动的话题。他仍然太虚弱了，无法起来吃早餐；但是，当他第二天下楼到管家房间时，他的第一反应就是急切地朝墙壁望去，希望再看一眼那个美丽女人的脸。然而，他的期望却落空了，因为那幅画已经被移走了。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Oliver soon recovering from the fainting-fit into which Mr. Brownlow’s abrupt exclamation had thrown him, the subject of the picture was carefully avoided, both by the old gentleman and Mrs. Bedwin, in the conversation that ensued: which indeed bore no reference to Oliver’s history or prospects, but was confined to such topics as might amuse without exciting him. He was still too weak to get up to breakfast; but, when he came down into the housekeeper’s room next day, his first act was to cast an eager glance at the wall, in the hope of again looking on the face of the beautiful lady. His expectations were disappointed, however, for the picture had been removed.

奥利弗从晕倒中很快恢复过来，他听到了布朗洛先生的突然爆发，这让他感到很意外。在随后的交谈中，老先生和贝德温夫人都小心地避开了关于那幅画的讨论。他们的话题转移到了一些无关紧要的事情上，比如天气、新闻或者其他一些无伤大雅的事，完全不涉及奥利弗的过去或者未来的计划。由于身体还没有恢复，奥利弗仍然无法起床吃早餐。但是，当他第二天下楼到管家室的时候，他第一件事就是急切地朝墙壁看去，希望能够再次见到那位美丽女士的面容。然而，他失望了，因为那幅画已经被移走了。

source: The girl drew closer to the table, and glancing at Monks with an air of careless levity, withdrew her eyes; but as he turned towards Fagin, she stole another look; so keen and searching, and full of purpose, that if there had been any bystander to observe the change, he could hardly have believed the two looks to have proceeded from the same person.
domestication: 那女孩走近桌子，漫不经心地朝蒙克斯瞟了一眼，然后移开视线。但当蒙克斯转向费金时，她又偷偷地看了他一眼，这一眼的神情如此敏锐、审视、充满目的性，如果有人旁观这一幕，简直很难相信前后两眼神是同一个人发出的。
foreignization: 这个女孩走到桌子旁，漫不经心地朝蒙克斯看了一眼，然后移开了视线；但当他转向费金时，她又偷偷地瞥了一眼；她的目光如此锐利、探寻和充满目的，那么如果有人在场观察到这种变化，他很难相信这两眼神是同一个人发出的。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The girl drew closer to the table, and glancing at Monks with an air of careless levity, withdrew her eyes; but as he turned towards Fagin, she stole another look; so keen and searching, and full of purpose, that if there had been any bystander to observe the change, he could hardly have believed the two looks to have proceeded from the same person.
那女孩走近桌子，漫不经心地朝蒙克斯瞟了一眼，然后移开视线。但当蒙克斯转向费金时，她又偷偷地看了他一眼，这一眼的神情如此敏锐、审视、充满目的性，如果有人旁观这一幕，简直很难相信前后两眼神是同一个人发出的。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The girl drew closer to the table, and glancing at Monks with an air of careless levity, withdrew her eyes; but as he turned towards Fagin, she stole another look; so keen and searching, and full of purpose, that if there had been any bystander to observe the change, he could hardly have believed the two looks to have proceeded from the same person.
这个女孩走到桌子旁，漫不经心地朝蒙克斯看了一眼，然后移开了视线；但当他转向费金时，她又偷偷地瞥了一眼；她的目光如此锐利、探寻和充满目的，那么如果有人在场观察到这种变化，他很难相信这两眼神是同一个人发出的。

source: Oliver, who could hardly stand, made a shift to raise himself on his feet, and was at once lugged along the streets by the jacket-collar, at a rapid pace. The gentleman walked on with them by the officer’s side; and as many of the crowd as could achieve the feat, got a little ahead, and stared back at Oliver from time to time. The boys shouted in triumph; and on they went.
domestication: 奥利弗勉强站起来，挣扎着抬起脚跟，被抓着衣领拖着在街上走。那个先生和那个警察一起走，旁边一大群人也跟着，能挤到前面的人就趁机回头盯着奥利弗看，那些小子们得意地喊叫着，一行人就这么继续往前走。
foreignization: 奥利弗勉强站了起来，立刻被抓住衣领拖着在街道上快速行走。先生和警官并肩走着，人群中能跟上的人试图往前赶几步，然后回头盯着奥利弗看。小男孩们得意地大声喊叫着，他们继续前进。

source: The man who had knocked at the door, was a stout personage of middle height, aged about fifty: with shiny black hair, cropped pretty close; half-whiskers, a round face, and sharp eyes. The other was a red-headed, bony man, in top-boots; with a rather ill-favoured countenance, and a turned-up sinister-looking nose.
domestication: 来敲门的那个人，长得膘肥体壮，中等身材，大约五十岁的样子，头发乌黑油亮，剃得短短的；胡须稀疏，脸蛋圆圆的，眼神却很犀利。而另一个人呢，却是个头发红得发紫的瘦高个儿，脚蹬长靴，长得一脸不讨人喜欢，鼻子还朝上挑着，看起来挺阴险的样子。
foreignization: 敲门的人是一个身材中等、体格健壮的人，大约五十岁左右；他有着光亮的黑发，剪得相当短；有半边胡须，圆脸和锐利的眼睛。另一个人是一个头发红色、骨瘦如柴的人，穿着高筒靴；他有一张相貌不好看的脸，鼻子向上翘，显得有些险恶。

source: The cry was repeated—a light appeared—a vision of two terrified half-dressed men at the top of the stairs swam before his eyes—a flash—a loud noise—a smoke—a crash somewhere, but where he knew not,—and he staggered back.
domestication: 那声惨叫又响起了，一道亮光闪过，他的眼前出现了两个半裸着身子的男人惊恐万分地站在楼梯顶上，接着是一道闪光，一声巨响，冒出了一股烟雾，还有东西坠落的声音，但他不知道发生在哪里，他踉跄着向后退去。
foreignization: 哭喊声再次响起——一道光亮出现——两个半裸着上身的惊恐男子站在楼梯顶端的景象在他眼前闪现——一道闪光——一声巨响——一缕烟雾——某个地方传来了一声撞击声，但他不知道具体在哪里——然后他踉跄着退了回去。

messages_domestication.content:

source: “Wait a minute!” said the girl: “I wouldn’t hurry by, if it was you that was coming out to be hung, the next time eight o’clock struck, Bill. I’d walk round and round the place till I dropped, if the snow was on the ground, and I hadn’t a shawl to cover me.”
domestication: “慢着！”姑娘说：“如果下次八点钟要你去受刑，我才不急着走呢， 빌！就算地上铺满雪，我身上也没一件披肩， 我也会在这地方转圈子，直到累倒为止。”
foreignization: “等一下！”那个女孩说：“如果下次八点钟响起时是你要被绞死，我不会匆忙离开。我会在这个地方走来走去，直到我倒下，即使地面上有雪，也没有一条披肩让我裹着。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Wait a minute!” said the girl: “I wouldn’t hurry by, if it was you that was coming out to be hung, the next time eight o’clock struck, Bill. I’d walk round and round the place till I dropped, if the snow was on the ground, and I hadn’t a shawl to cover me.”
“慢着！”姑娘说：“如果下次八点钟要你去受刑，我才不急着走呢， 빌！就算地上铺满雪，我身上也没一件披肩， 我也会在这地方转圈子，直到累倒为止。”

“Wait a minute!” said the girl: “I wouldn’t hurry by, if it was you that was coming out to be hung, the next time eight o’clock struck, Bill. I’d walk round and round the place till I dropped, if the snow was on the ground, and I hadn’t a shawl to cover me.”
“等一下！”那个女孩说：“如果下次八点钟响起时是你要被绞死，我不会匆忙离开。我会在这个地方走来走去，直到我倒下，即使地面上有雪，也没有一条披肩让我裹着。”

source: The terrified children cried bitterly; but the old woman, who had hitherto remained as quiet as if she had been wholly deaf to all that passed, menaced them into silence. Having unloosened the cravat of the man who still remained extended on the ground, she tottered towards the undertaker.
domestication: 孩子们吓得哭闹不已，但老太婆一直如同聋子般静坐不动，这时却突然开口警告他们别出声。老太婆解开了地上躺着的人的领带，然后蹒跚地走向殡仪师。
foreignization: 吓坏了的孩子们痛苦地哭泣；但是老妇人，迄今为止一直像完全聋了似的对发生的一切保持沉默，现在却用一种威胁的眼神让他们保持沉默。解开了仍然躺在地上的人的领结后，她踉跄地向殡葬承办者走去。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The terrified children cried bitterly; but the old woman, who had hitherto remained as quiet as if she had been wholly deaf to all that passed, menaced them into silence. Having unloosened the cravat of the man who still remained extended on the ground, she tottered towards the undertaker.
孩子们吓得哭闹不已，但老太婆一直如同聋子般静坐不动，这时却突然开口警告他们别出声。老太婆解开了地上躺着的人的领带，然后蹒跚地走向殡仪师。

吓坏了的孩子们痛苦地哭泣；但是老妇人，迄今为止一直像完全聋了似的对发生的一切保持沉默，现在却用一种威胁的眼神让他们保持沉默。解开了仍然躺在地上的人的领结后，她踉跄地向殡葬承办者走去。

source: And so Oliver remained all that day, and for the greater part of many subsequent days, seeing nobody, between early morning and midnight, and left during the long hours to commune with his own thoughts. Which, never failing to revert to his kind friends, and the opinion they must long ago have formed of him, were sad indeed.
domestication: 就这样，奥利弗整天都待在那里，而且在随后的很多天里，大部分时间都是如此，从早晨到半夜，他除了和自己的思绪打交道外，什么也没做。他老是会想到那些好心的朋友，并且想知道他们早就怎么看待他了，这些想法让他感到十分伤心。
foreignization: 于是，奥利弗在那一天以及随后的许多天的大部分时间里一直孤独一人，从清晨到午夜都见不到一个人，被留下来长时间与自己的思绪交谈。而他的想法总是会回到他的亲切的朋友们身上，以及他们很早以前就已经形成的关于他的看法，这些想法确实是很悲伤的。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

And so Oliver remained all that day, and for the greater part of many subsequent days, seeing nobody, between early morning and midnight, and left during the long hours to commune with his own thoughts. Which, never failing to revert to his kind friends, and the opinion they must long ago have formed of him, were sad indeed.
就这样，奥利弗整天都待在那里，而且在随后的很多天里，大部分时间都是如此，从早晨到半夜，他除了和自己的思绪打交道外，什么也没做。他老是会想到那些好心的朋友，并且想知道他们早就怎么看待他了，这些想法让他感到十分伤心。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

And so Oliver remained all that day, and for the greater part of many subsequent days, seeing nobody, between early morning and midnight, and left during the long hours to commune with his own thoughts. Which, never failing to revert to his kind friends, and the opinion they must long ago have formed of him, were sad indeed.
于是，奥利弗在那一天以及随后的许多天的大部分时间里一直孤独一人，从清晨到午夜都见不到一个人，被留下来长时间与自己的思绪交谈。而他的想法总是会回到他的亲切的朋友们身上，以及他们很早以前就已经形成的关于他的看法，这些想法确实是很悲伤的。

source: Although Mr. Crackit spoke in a scarcely audible whisper, and laughed without noise, Sikes imperiously commanded him to be silent, and to get to work. Toby complied, by first producing his lantern, and placing it on the ground; then by planting himself firmly with his head against the wall beneath the window, and his hands upon his knees, so as to make a step of his back. This was no sooner done, than Sikes, mounting upon him, put Oliver gently through the window with his feet first; and, without leaving hold of his collar, planted him safely on the floor inside.
domestication: 克拉基特先生说话时几乎听不到，他的笑声也没有任何声音，但赛克斯还是命令他保持安静，开始工作。托比照做了，首先拿出灯笼放在了地上，然后背靠着窗下的墙站稳，双手撑在膝盖上，把自己的背当成阶梯一样。刚刚摆好位置，赛克斯就爬上托比的背，轻轻地把奥利弗从窗口放进去，脚先进去。整个过程中，他都没松开对奥利弗衣领的抓握，直到奥利弗安全地站在里面地板上。
foreignization: 克雷基特先生虽然几乎是低声细语地说话，而且不出声地笑，赛克斯还是命令他保持安静并开始工作。 托比遵从了他的命令，首先拿出了他的灯笼并将其放在了地上；然后他把自己牢固地靠在窗户下的墙壁上，双手放在膝盖上，以便把他的背当作一个台阶。 托比一做好这些，赛克斯就踩着他的背，轻轻地把奥利弗从窗口推进去，脚先进去；同时没有放开奥利弗的衣领，将他安全地放在里面地板上。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Although Mr. Crackit spoke in a scarcely audible whisper, and laughed without noise, Sikes imperiously commanded him to be silent, and to get to work. Toby complied, by first producing his lantern, and placing it on the ground; then by planting himself firmly with his head against the wall beneath the window, and his hands upon his knees, so as to make a step of his back. This was no sooner done, than Sikes, mounting upon him, put Oliver gently through the window with his feet first; and, without leaving hold of his collar, planted him safely on the floor inside.

source: The surgeon deposited it in her arms. She imprinted her cold white lips passionately on its forehead; passed her hands over her face; gazed wildly round; shuddered; fell back—and died. They chafed her breast, hands, and temples; but the blood had stopped forever. They talked of hope and comfort. They had been strangers too long.
domestication: 外科医生轻轻地把孩子递到她怀里，她的冰冷的嘴唇热情地亲吻着孩子的额头，抚摸着自己的脸，狂野地四周张望，打了个寒战，然后倒回去……就一命呜呼。他们试图通过揉搓她的胸部、双手和太阳穴来激活她的血液循环，但已经没有任何反应了。有人试图安慰她说“别担心”，但都只是陌生人的劝慰话，他们之间早已形同陌路。
foreignization: 外科医生将婴儿放在她的怀里。她热情地用她冰冷的白唇亲吻它的额头；用手抚摸她的脸；疯狂地环顾四周；打了个寒战；向后倒下——然后死去了。他们揉搓着她的胸部，双手和太阳穴；但是血已经永远停止流动。他们谈论着希望和安慰。他们已经是陌生人太久了。

source: “The Bow Street officers, sir,” replied Brittles, taking up a candle; “me and Mr. Giles sent for ’em this morning.”

foreignization: “鲍街的警察，先生，”布里特尔回答道，他拿起一根蜡烛；“我和吉尔斯先生早上派人去叫他们来了。”

messages_foreignization.content:

“The Bow Street officers, sir,” replied Brittles, taking up a candle; “me and Mr. Giles sent for ’em this morning.”
“鲍街的警察，先生，”布里特尔回答道，他拿起一根蜡烛；“我和吉尔斯先生早上派人去叫他们来了。”

source: “When, indeed, ma’am!” rejoined Mr. Bumble. “Why here’s one man that, in consideration of his wife and large family, has a quartern loaf and a good pound of cheese, full weight. Is he grateful, ma’am? Is he grateful? Not a copper farthing’s worth of it! What does he do, ma’am, but ask for a few coals; if it’s only a pocket handkerchief full, he says! Coals! What would he do with coals? Toast his cheese with ’em and then come back for more. That’s the way with these people, ma’am; give ’em a apron full of coals today, and they’ll come back for another, the day after tomorrow, as brazen as alabaster.”
domestication: “的确，夫人！”邦布尔先生接口说，“比如说有个男人，因为有妻子和一大群孩子，竟然能得到四分之一个面包和一磅重的奶酪。难道他感激吗？他根本不领情！他到底做了什么呢，夫人？他居然要一些煤，哪怕只是一块手帕那么一点儿。他要煤干嘛？难道是想拿来烤奶酪，然后再来要更多？这些人就是这样，夫人，你今天给他们一条围裙那么多煤，他们后天就会回来要更多，而且脸皮还比石头都厚。”

“When, indeed, ma’am!” rejoined Mr. Bumble. “Why here’s one man that, in consideration of his wife and large family, has a quartern loaf and a good pound of cheese, full weight. Is he grateful, ma’am? Is he grateful? Not a copper farthing’s worth of it! What does he do, ma’am, but ask for a few coals; if it’s only a pocket handkerchief full, he says! Coals! What would he do with coals? Toast his cheese with ’em and then come back for more. That’s the way with these people, ma’am; give ’em a apron full of coals today, and they’ll come back for another, the day after tomorrow, as brazen as alabaster.”

source: This was a very strong expression of approbation, an uncommonly hearty welcome, from a person of Mr. Sikes’ temperament. Nancy, appearing much gratified thereby, saluted him cordially.
domestication: 斯派克先生这样一个脾气暴躁的人竟然对她表示如此热情的赞许，确实让娜西感到非常高兴。于是，她也十分热情地和他打了招呼。
foreignization: 这是一种非常强烈的赞同表达，对于像赛克斯先生这样性情的人来说，这是十分罕见的热情欢迎。南希似乎因此而感到非常高兴，热情地向他问候。

source: “Only think,” said Fagin, shrugging his shoulders, and stretching out his hands; “only consider. You’ve done what’s a very pretty thing, and what I love you for doing; but what at the same time would put the cravat round your throat, that’s so very easily tied and so very difficult to unloose—in plain English, the halter!”
domestication: “想想吧，” 费金耸了耸肩膀，伸出双手说，“好好想想。你做了一件很漂亮的事，我也很喜欢你这么做，但同时，这件事也可能会让你戴上绞索，那个被套在脖子上的东西，一旦套上就很难松开——说白了，就是绞刑架！
foreignization: “只要想想，”费金耸了耸肩膀，伸出双手说，“只需要考虑一下。你做了一件非常漂亮的事情，这件事情我很喜欢你这么做；但是与此同时会让绞索套在你的脖子上，这个绞索如此容易系上却如此难以解开——用通俗的英语来说，就是绞索！

“Only think,” said Fagin, shrugging his shoulders, and stretching out his hands; “only consider. You’ve done what’s a very pretty thing, and what I love you for doing; but what at the same time would put the cravat round your throat, that’s so very easily tied and so very difficult to unloose—in plain English, the halter!”
“只要想想，”费金耸了耸肩膀，伸出双手说，“只需要考虑一下。你做了一件非常漂亮的事情，这件事情我很喜欢你这么做；但是与此同时会让绞索套在你的脖子上，这个绞索如此容易系上却如此难以解开——用通俗的英语来说，就是绞索！

source: “I did not,” replied Mr. Brownlow, rising too; “but within the last fortnight I have learnt it all. You have a brother; you know it, and him. There was a will, which your mother destroyed, leaving the secret and the gain to you at her own death. It contained a reference to some child likely to be the result of this sad connection, which child was born, and accidentally encountered by you, when your suspicions were first awakened by his resemblance to your father. You repaired to the place of his birth. There existed proofs—proofs long suppressed—of his birth and parentage. Those proofs were destroyed by you, and now, in your own words to your accomplice the Jew, ‘the only proofs of the boy’s identity lie at the bottom of the river, and the old hag that received them from the mother is rotting in her coffin.’ Unworthy son, coward, liar,—you, who hold your councils with thieves and murderers in dark rooms at night,—you, whose plots and wiles have brought a violent death upon the head of one worth millions such as you,—you, who from your cradle were gall and bitterness to your own father’s heart, and in whom all evil passions, vice, and profligacy, festered, till they found a vent in a hideous disease which had made your face an index even to your mind—you, Edward Leeford, do you still brave me!”
domestication: “没错，”布朗洛先生站起来说，“但在过去两周里，我已经全部搞清楚了。你有一个弟弟，你知道的，而且你也认识他。你妈妈销毁了一份遗嘱，把这个秘密和利益留给了你，等她死了就归你所有。这份遗嘱里提到可能会有一个孩子出生，这个孩子确实出生了，当你第一次看到他长得像你父亲的时候，你的疑心就被激发了。你跑到那个孩子出生的地方去查找证据。那里确实有一些被压抑很久的证据，证明了孩子的出身和血统。这些证据被你销毁了，现在，用你自己的话来说，你对你的同伙那個猶太人說：“孩子身份的唯一證據現在沉在河底，而接收過証據的老太婆已经在她的棺材里腐烂了。” 不配做儿子的东西，懦夫，骗子——你经常在夜深人静的时候和小偷、凶手们在黑屋子里商量——你，你的阴谋诡计给一个富可敌国的人带来了残酷的死亡——你，从你出生开始就是你父亲心中的苦涩，你所有的坏情绪、恶习和放荡在你身上溃烂，直到它们变成了一种丑陋的疾病，这种疾病甚至使你的面貌成为你心灵的写照——你，爱德华·利福德，你还敢跟我叫板吗？
foreignization: “我没有，”布朗洛先生也站起来回答说，“但是在过去的两个星期里我都已经知道了这一切。你有一个哥哥，你知道这个人，也知道他。有一份遗嘱，你母亲毁掉了它，把这个秘密和利益留给你在她去世的时候。这份遗嘱提到了一个可能是这段悲伤关系的结果的孩子，这个孩子出生了，并且你偶然遇到了他，当时你的怀疑是由他的长相与你父亲的相似而引发的。你去了他出生的地方，那里有——被长期压制的——他出身和父母的证据。这些证据被你销毁了，现在，按照你自己的话，告诉你的同伙那个犹太人，“男孩身份的唯一证据现在在河底，而从他母亲那里接收它们的老巫婆正在她的棺材里腐烂”。不配当儿子的家伙，懦夫，骗子——你，和小偷以及凶手在夜晚黑暗的房间里开会的人——你，你的阴谋诡计给价值百万富翁的头上带来了暴力死亡——你，从你出生以来就是你父亲心中的胆汁和苦涩，在你身上所有邪恶的激情，恶习，放荡，疥疮，直到它们找到了一个可怕的疾病的发泄口，这个疾病甚至使你的脸成为你思想的指标——你，爱德华·利福德，你还在勇敢地对抗我吗？

You repaired to the place of his birth. There existed proofs—proofs long suppressed—of his birth and parentage. Those proofs were destroyed by you, and now, in your own words to your accomplice the Jew, ‘the only proofs of the boy’s identity lie at the bottom of the river, and the old hag that received them from the mother is rotting in her coffin.’ Unworthy son, coward, liar,—you, who hold your councils with thieves and murderers in dark rooms at night,—you, whose plots and wiles have brought a violent death upon the head of one worth millions such as you,—you, who from your cradle were gall and bitterness to your own father’s heart, and in whom all evil passions, vice, and profligacy, festered, till they found a vent in a hideous disease which had made your face an index even to your mind—you, Edward Leeford, do you still brave me!”

source: “Rot you!” murmured the man, in a horrible passion; between his clenched teeth; “if I had only had the courage to say the word, I might have been free of you in a night. Curses on your head, and black death on your heart, you imp! What are you doing here?”
domestication: 这人气得咬牙切齿，低声咒骂道：“该死的！要是我当时有胆量说出那句话，就一夜之间摆脱了你。你的头上遭雷劈，你的心被黑暗吞噬，去死吧，小鬼！你来这里干什么？”
foreignization: “枯萎吧你！”那个男人低声咒骂道，极度愤怒；他的话语从紧咬的牙齿之间挤出；“如果我当时有勇气说出那句话，也许一夜之间我就能摆脱你。诅咒降临到你的头上，你这个小鬼，黑暗死亡降临到你的心上，你在这里做什么？”

“Rot you!” murmured the man, in a horrible passion; between his clenched teeth; “if I had only had the courage to say the word, I might have been free of you in a night. Curses on your head, and black death on your heart, you imp! What are you doing here?”
“枯萎吧你！”那个男人低声咒骂道，极度愤怒；他的话语从紧咬的牙齿之间挤出；“如果我当时有勇气说出那句话，也许一夜之间我就能摆脱你。诅咒降临到你的头上，你这个小鬼，黑暗死亡降临到你的心上，你在这里做什么？”

source: Stretching forth her trembling fingers as she spoke, the old creature shook them exultingly before her face, and fumbling in her pocket, brought out an old time-discoloured tin snuff-box, from which she shook a few grains into the outstretched palm of her companion, and a few more into her own. While they were thus employed, the matron, who had been impatiently watching until the dying woman should awaken from her stupor, joined them by the fire, and sharply asked how long she was to wait?
domestication: 她说话时，颤抖着手指，伸出手去，兴奋地在自己面前摇晃着手指。接着，她翻找口袋，掏出一个旧的、因年久失色的锡制鼻烟盒，从中倒出几粒鼻烟，分别放在同伴和自己手掌心上。当他们这样做的时候，那位一直在焦急地等待垂死女人从恍惚状态醒来的女主人，加入了他们在火堆旁边的人群，严厉地问道，他们还要等多久？

source: “Heaven bless you for your goodness!” rejoined the girl. “If you knew what I am sometimes, you would pity me, indeed. But I have stolen away from those who would surely murder me, if they knew I had been here, to tell you what I have overheard. Do you know a man named Monks?”
domestication: “上帝保佑你的好心！”那个女孩回答道。“如果你知道我有时候是什么样子的，你肯定会可怜我。但是我偷偷地从那些人身边溜出来告诉你我听到的秘密，如果他们知道我来过这里，一定会杀了我。你认识一个叫蒙克斯的男人吗？”
foreignization: “上帝保佑你因为你的仁慈！”那个女孩回答道。“如果你知道我有时候会变成什么样，你确实会可怜我。但是我溜走了，因为那些人如果知道我来过这里，肯定会杀死我，我是来告诉你我无意中听到的事情的。你认识一个叫蒙克斯的人吗？”

source: The old man turned to close the door, as he made this reflection; the noise thus occasioned, roused the girl. She eyed his crafty face narrowly, as she inquired to his recital of Toby Crackit’s story. When it was concluded, she sank into her former attitude, but spoke not a word. She pushed the candle impatiently away; and once or twice as she feverishly changed her position, shuffled her feet upon the ground; but this was all.
domestication: 老头转身去关门，就在这时，他心里闪过一个念头。关门的声音把女孩惊醒了。她睁大眼睛，紧紧地盯着老头的狡猾面孔，听他讲托比·克拉基特的故事。等老头讲完，她又恢复了原来的姿势，但是一言不发。她不耐烦地把蜡烛推开，几次发热似的换了个姿势，还不停地在地上跺脚，但也就仅此而已。
foreignization: 那老人转身去关门，边走边想着；所发出的声音惊醒了那个女孩。她在聆听他讲述托比·克拉基特的故事时，目不转睛地盯着他的狡猾面容。当他讲完之后，她又恢复了她之前的姿势，但一句话也没说。她焦躁地把蜡烛推开；当她发热地改变姿势时，一两次摩擦脚在地面上；但就仅此而已。

source: The man went to the door, and returned to announce that all was quiet. The two robbers issued forth with Oliver between them. Barney, having made all fast, rolled himself up as before, and was soon asleep again.
domestication: 那人走到门口，回来报告说一切平安无事。两个强盗带着奥利弗从中间走了出来。巴尼把所有东西都收拾妥当后，又像之前一样蜷缩起来，不一会儿又睡着了。
foreignization: 那个人走到门口，又回来宣布一切都很安静。两名强盗带着奥利弗从他们中间走出来。巴尼一切都搞定后，又像以前一样把自己裹起来，很快又睡着了。

source: The tone of forced gaiety in which the last words were spoken, seemed to produce a deeper impression on Sikes than the wild and rigid look which had preceded them.
domestication: 最后几句话说得勉强欢快，但这似乎在赛克斯心中留下了更深的印象，而之前那疯狂僵硬的神情反而逊色了。
foreignization: 最后几句话带有强迫的欢快语气，这似乎在萨イクス身上产生了比之前那疯狂而僵硬的目光更深刻的印象。

source: The apothecary’s apprentice, having completed the manufacture of the toothpick, planted himself in front of the fire and made good use of it for ten minutes or so: when apparently growing rather dull, he wished Mrs. Corney joy of her job, and took himself off on tiptoe.
domestication: 那位药铺学徒做完牙签，蹲在火炉旁边，暖和了十来分钟。这小子慢慢的就没了兴致，对科尼太太说了句“祝你高兴”，然后脚尖着地溜走了。
foreignization: 学徒药剂师在完成牙签的制作之后，站在火炉前边，十分钟左右，他似乎有点无聊，祝贺科尼夫人工作顺利，然后他蹑手蹑脚地离开了。

source: As she uttered these words, and indicated, with her hand, the direction in which she wished them to proceed, the countryman looked round, and roughly asking what they took up the whole pavement for, passed on.
domestication: 她刚说完这些话，并且用手指了指她希望他们前往的方向，一旁的乡下人转过头来，粗鲁地问他们为什么要占据整个路面，然后继续往前走。
foreignization: 当她说出这些话，并用手示意他们应该走的方向时，那个乡下人四周张望了一下，粗鲁地问他们为什么占据了整个人行道，然后继续往前走。

source: The young gentleman smiled, as if to intimate that the latter fragments of discourse were playfully ironical; and finished the beer as he did so.
domestication: 这年轻人笑了起来，好像在暗示刚才那些话都是开玩笑的，嘴里嘟囔着什么，同时也把剩下的啤酒喝完了。
foreignization: 这位年轻的绅士微笑，仿佛要暗示刚才那些话是开玩笑的讽刺；并且他一边这样说，一边喝完了啤酒。

source: “No,” replied Fagin, “not very. He was charged with attempting to pick a pocket, and they found a silver snuff-box on him,—his own, my dear, his own, for he took snuff himself, and was very fond of it. They remanded him till today, for they thought they knew the owner. Ah! he was worth fifty boxes, and I’d give the price of as many to have him back. You should have known the Dodger, my dear; you should have known the Dodger.”
domestication: “不嘛，”费金回答说，“没什么大不了的。他只是被指控想要偷个口袋，结果在他身上搜出一只银质鼻烟盒——那是他的，亲爱的，是他的，因为他自己就喜欢嗅鼻烟，而且非常喜欢。他们暂时关了他，直到今天，因为他们以为知道盒子主人的身份。唉，如果能让他回来，我连五十个这样的盒子都愿意付钱。你们应该认识这个滑头鬼，亲爱的，你们真应该认识这个滑头鬼。”
foreignization: “不”，费金回答说，“不很。他被指控企图扒窃，人们在他身上发现了一个银色的鼻烟盒，——他的，亲爱的，是他的，因为他自己吸鼻烟，并且非常喜欢。而他们以可能找到了鼻烟盒的主人为由将他拘留至今日。啊！他值五十个这样的盒子，我会出价买下这么多个的价格来让他回来。你应该认识到达尔杰，我的亲爱的；你应该认识到达尔杰。”

“No,” replied Fagin, “not very. He was charged with attempting to pick a pocket, and they found a silver snuff-box on him,—his own, my dear, his own, for he took snuff himself, and was very fond of it. They remanded him till today, for they thought they knew the owner. Ah! he was worth fifty boxes, and I’d give the price of as many to have him back. You should have known the Dodger, my dear; you should have known the Dodger.”

“No,” replied Fagin, “not very. He was charged with attempting to pick a pocket, and they found a silver snuff-box on him,—his own, my dear, his own, for he took snuff himself, and was very fond of it. They remanded him till today, for they thought they knew the owner. Ah! he was worth fifty boxes, and I’d give the price of as many to have him back. You should have known the Dodger, my dear; you should have known the Dodger.”

source: All this, Mr. Brownlow, although himself somewhat of an impetuous gentleman: knowing his friend’s peculiarities, bore with great good humour; as Mr. Grimwig, at tea, was graciously pleased to express his entire approval of the muffins, matters went on very smoothly; and Oliver, who made one of the party, began to feel more at his ease than he had yet done in the fierce old gentleman’s presence.
domestication: 布朗洛先生尽管自己也有点性急，但他知道格林维格先生的怪癖，所以总是很有耐心地容忍着。尤其当他们坐下来喝茶时，格林维格先生竟然很高兴地夸赞了手指饼，气氛一下子变得很融洽。奥利弗作为座上客，开始在这老绅士面前感到轻松一些。
foreignization: 所有这一切，布朗洛先生，他自己也算是一个性情急躁的绅士：了解朋友的怪癖，用非常好的幽默感容忍着；当格里姆威格先生在喝茶时，很有礼貌地表示他完全赞同这些松饼，事情进行得非常顺利；奥利弗，他是聚会的一员，开始感到比他以前在这位凶猛的老先生面前更轻松。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

All this, Mr. Brownlow, although himself somewhat of an impetuous gentleman: knowing his friend’s peculiarities, bore with great good humour; as Mr. Grimwig, at tea, was graciously pleased to express his entire approval of the muffins, matters went on very smoothly; and Oliver, who made one of the party, began to feel more at his ease than he had yet done in the fierce old gentleman’s presence.
布朗洛先生尽管自己也有点性急，但他知道格林维格先生的怪癖，所以总是很有耐心地容忍着。尤其当他们坐下来喝茶时，格林维格先生竟然很高兴地夸赞了手指饼，气氛一下子变得很融洽。奥利弗作为座上客，开始在这老绅士面前感到轻松一些。

source: “Do you hear his worship ask if you’ve anything to say?” inquired the jailer, nudging the silent Dodger with his elbow.
domestication: “嘿，大师问你还有什么话要说吗？”狱卒用肘部轻推一言不发的道格尔，问道。
foreignization: “你听见他问你有没有什么可说的吗？”典狱长问道，同时用他的肘部轻推沉默的道格尔。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Do you hear his worship ask if you’ve anything to say?” inquired the jailer, nudging the silent Dodger with his elbow.
“嘿，大师问你还有什么话要说吗？”狱卒用肘部轻推一言不发的道格尔，问道。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Do you hear his worship ask if you’ve anything to say?” inquired the jailer, nudging the silent Dodger with his elbow.
“你听见他问你有没有什么可说的吗？”典狱长问道，同时用他的肘部轻推沉默的道格尔。

source: “—That Monks,” pursued the girl, “had seen him accidently with two of our boys on the day we first lost him, and had known him directly to be the same child that he was watching for, though I couldn’t make out why. A bargain was struck with Fagin, that if Oliver was got back he should have a certain sum; and he was to have more for making him a thief, which this Monks wanted for some purpose of his own.”
domestication: 那小偷见过他一次，是跟我们两个孩子在一起，当时我们正好丢了他，他一眼就认出这就是他一直在等的那孩子，不过我不知道为什么。然后他和老板.Fagin谈妥，如果能把奥利弗弄回来，就给他一定的钱，如果能把他教成小偷，还能得到更多，这个人有他的目的。
foreignization: “那个蒙克斯，”那女孩继续说，“碰巧在我们第一次丢失他那天看见过他和我们的两个孩子在一起，于是马上就知道他就是他一直在寻找的那孩子，尽管我不清楚为什么。与费金达成协议，如果奥利弗被带回来，他将获得一笔钱；如果费金能把奥利弗培养成小偷，他还能得到更多，因为蒙克斯出于某种私利想要这样做。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“—That Monks,” pursued the girl, “had seen him accidently with two of our boys on the day we first lost him, and had known him directly to be the same child that he was watching for, though I couldn’t make out why. A bargain was struck with Fagin, that if Oliver was got back he should have a certain sum; and he was to have more for making him a thief, which this Monks wanted for some purpose of his own.”
那小偷见过他一次，是跟我们两个孩子在一起，当时我们正好丢了他，他一眼就认出这就是他一直在等的那孩子，不过我不知道为什么。然后他和老板.Fagin谈妥，如果能把奥利弗弄回来，就给他一定的钱，如果能把他教成小偷，还能得到更多，这个人有他的目的。

A bargain was struck with Fagin, that if Oliver was got back he should have a certain sum; and he was to have more for making him a thief, which this Monks wanted for some purpose of his own.”
“那个蒙克斯，”那女孩继续说，“碰巧在我们第一次丢失他那天看见过他和我们的两个孩子在一起，于是马上就知道他就是他一直在寻找的那孩子，尽管我不清楚为什么。与费金达成协议，如果奥利弗被带回来，他将获得一笔钱；如果费金能把奥利弗培养成小偷，他还能得到更多，因为蒙克斯出于某种私利想要这样做。”

source: “Stay there,” whispered Fagin. He signed to Barney, who withdrew. In an instant, the lad entered the room adjoining, and, under pretence of snuffing the candle, moved it in the required position, and, speaking to the girl, caused her to raise her face.
domestication: 法金低声说：“待着别动。”他朝巴尼使了个眼神，巴尼退了下去。一会儿，年轻小伙子进了旁边的房间，假装闻蜡烛味，顺便把蜡烛移到了需要的位置，同时跟小姑娘说了几句话，让她抬起了头来。
foreignization: “呆在那里，” 费金低声说。他向巴尼示意，巴尼退了回去。一瞬间，小伙子进入了隔壁房间，并借口闻蜡烛的味道，将其移动到所需的位置，并对女孩说话，使她扬起了脸。

source: The jailer took the disengaged hand of Oliver; and, whispering him not to be alarmed, looked on without speaking.
domestication: 典狱长握住奥利弗的一只手，低声告诉他别害怕，然后就站在一旁一言不发地看着。
foreignization: 典狱长握住奥利弗松开的一只手；并在他耳边低声说不要害怕，自己则默默地看着。

messages_domestication.content:

source: “I have been a liar, and among liars from a little child,” said the girl after another interval of silence, “but I will take your words.”
domestication: 这姑娘沉默了一会儿后接着说：“我从小就学会了说谎，也和一群喜欢撒谎的人混在一起，但我会相信你的话。”
foreignization: “我一直是个骗子，从小就和骗子混在一起，”姑娘在另一个沉默的间隔之后说，“但我会接受你的话。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“I have been a liar, and among liars from a little child,” said the girl after another interval of silence, “but I will take your words.”
这姑娘沉默了一会儿后接着说：“我从小就学会了说谎，也和一群喜欢撒谎的人混在一起，但我会相信你的话。”

messages_foreignization.content:

“我一直是个骗子，从小就和骗子混在一起，”姑娘在另一个沉默的间隔之后说，“但我会接受你的话。”

source: The spirit of contradiction was strong in Mr. Grimwig’s breast, at the moment; and it was rendered stronger by his friend’s confident smile.
domestication: 格林威先生心里本来就有反对的倾向，此刻更加强烈了，尤其是看到朋友那副信心满满的笑容，更加激发了他的逆反心理。他越发地不愿意同意对方的看法，而且越想越觉得应该跟对方辩个明白。
foreignization: 格林威先生的内心此刻充满了矛盾的精神；而他朋友自信的微笑使得这种精神更加强烈。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The spirit of contradiction was strong in Mr. Grimwig’s breast, at the moment; and it was rendered stronger by his friend’s confident smile.
格林威先生心里本来就有反对的倾向，此刻更加强烈了，尤其是看到朋友那副信心满满的笑容，更加激发了他的逆反心理。他越发地不愿意同意对方的看法，而且越想越觉得应该跟对方辩个明白。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The spirit of contradiction was strong in Mr. Grimwig’s breast, at the moment; and it was rendered stronger by his friend’s confident smile.
格林威先生的内心此刻充满了矛盾的精神；而他朋友自信的微笑使得这种精神更加强烈。

source: At this moment, there walked into the room: supporting himself by a thick stick: a stout old gentleman, rather lame in one leg, who was dressed in a blue coat, striped waistcoat, nankeen breeches and gaiters, and a broad-brimmed white hat, with the sides turned up with green. A very small-plaited shirt frill stuck out from his waistcoat; and a very long steel watch-chain, with nothing but a key at the end, dangled loosely below it. The ends of his white neckerchief were twisted into a ball about the size of an orange; the variety of shapes into which his countenance was twisted, defy description. He had a manner of screwing his head on one side when he spoke; and of looking out of the corners of his eyes at the same time: which irresistibly reminded the beholder of a parrot. In this attitude, he fixed himself, the moment he made his appearance; and, holding out a small piece of orange-peel at arm’s length, exclaimed, in a growling, discontented voice,
domestication: 就在这时，一个胖老头走进了房间。他拄着一根粗大的手杖，因腿部有点瘸，身穿蓝色外套，条纹背心，南京绒裤和马靴，还有一顶宽檐白帽，帽檐边缘翘起，带有绿色的装饰。一件小得不能再小的衬衫领子从他的背心里露出来；而一条长长的钢制怀表链在下面晃悠，链子末端只挂着一把钥匙。他的白色围巾两端被扭成一个像橙子大小的球体，他的脸扭曲成各种形状，实在难以描述。他说话时有个习惯，就是把头歪向一边，同时用眼角往外看，这让人忍不住想起一只鹦鹉。他一出现，就摆出了这个姿势，伸出一块橙皮，双臂直直地伸出去，然后用一种抱怨的、不满的声音叫道：
foreignization: 此刻，一个男人拄着一根粗大的手杖走进了房间：他是个有点跛的老绅士，穿着一件蓝色的外套、条纹背心、南京布裤和马靴，头上戴着一顶宽檐白帽，帽边用绿色物料折起来。 一条很细的花边从他的背心里露出来； 一条很长的钢制怀表链子在下面松松地荡来荡去，链子末端只挂着一把钥匙。 他白色围巾的两端被扭成一个约莫像橙子大小的球体； 他脸上的表情变化多端，难以描述。 当他说话时，他有种习惯，就是把头歪到一边； 同时，他会从眼角瞥人： 这种样子让人忍不住想起一只鹦鹉。 他一出现，就把自己定格在这种姿势； 他举起一小块橙皮，伸直手臂，然后用一种抱怨的、不满的语调大声说，

source: “Drive on!” he cried, “hard, fast, full gallop! Nothing short of flying will keep pace with me, today.”
domestication: “快走！”他大声喊道，“使劲儿，快点儿，一路飞奔！今天除了飞起来什么也追不上我！”
foreignization: “继续驱赶！”他大叫，“使劲，快点，全力奔跑！除了飞起来，什么也不能跟上我今天的步伐。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Drive on!” he cried, “hard, fast, full gallop! Nothing short of flying will keep pace with me, today.”
“快走！”他大声喊道，“使劲儿，快点儿，一路飞奔！今天除了飞起来什么也追不上我！”

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Drive on!” he cried, “hard, fast, full gallop! Nothing short of flying will keep pace with me, today.”
“继续驱赶！”他大叫，“使劲，快点，全力奔跑！除了飞起来，什么也不能跟上我今天的步伐。”

source: As he uttered these words, the girl herself appeared and resumed her former seat. Her eyes were swollen and red; she rocked herself to and fro; tossed her head; and, after a little time, burst out laughing.
domestication: 说完这些话，那女孩自己出现了，回到她原来的座位上。她满脸泪痕，眼睛红肿不堪，不停地摇晃着身子，甩着头，过了一会儿，突然大笑起来。
foreignization: 当他说完这些话时，女孩自己出现了，重新坐回她的座位上。她的眼睛肿胀着、发红；她来回摇晃着自己；抛了抛头，然后，在过了一会儿之后，突然大笑起来。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

As he uttered these words, the girl herself appeared and resumed her former seat. Her eyes were swollen and red; she rocked herself to and fro; tossed her head; and, after a little time, burst out laughing.
说完这些话，那女孩自己出现了，回到她原来的座位上。她满脸泪痕，眼睛红肿不堪，不停地摇晃着身子，甩着头，过了一会儿，突然大笑起来。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

As he uttered these words, the girl herself appeared and resumed her former seat. Her eyes were swollen and red; she rocked herself to and fro; tossed her head; and, after a little time, burst out laughing.
当他说完这些话时，女孩自己出现了，重新坐回她的座位上。她的眼睛肿胀着、发红；她来回摇晃着自己；抛了抛头，然后，在过了一会儿之后，突然大笑起来。

source: It was a little lattice window, about five feet and a half above the ground, at the back of the house: which belonged to a scullery, or small brewing-place, at the end of the passage. The aperture was so small, that the inmates had probably not thought it worth while to defend it more securely; but it was large enough to admit a boy of Oliver’s size, nevertheless. A very brief exercise of Mr. Sike’s art, sufficed to overcome the fastening of the lattice; and it soon stood wide open also.
domestication: 那是一个小巧的格子窗，大约离地五尺半的高度，在屋子的后面。这扇窗是通往走廊尽头的小厨房或说是酿酒间的。窗户开口很小，里面的人可能认为没必要加固防护；但对于奥利弗这样大小的男孩来说，却足够大的了。赛克斯先生施展了他一贯的“绝技”，很快就破解了格子窗的锁；然后窗户也大大地敞开了。
foreignization: 那是一個小格子窗，離地大約五英尺半，在房子的後面：這是屬於通道盡頭一個小廚房或釀酒的地方。開口很小，以至於屋裡的人可能認為不值得更牢固地保護它；但是對於像奧利弗這個年齡的男孩來說，還是足夠大的。錫克斯先生技藝的一點兒短暫運用，就足以克服格子窗的扣合；於是格子窗也打開了。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

It was a little lattice window, about five feet and a half above the ground, at the back of the house: which belonged to a scullery, or small brewing-place, at the end of the passage. The aperture was so small, that the inmates had probably not thought it worth while to defend it more securely; but it was large enough to admit a boy of Oliver’s size, nevertheless. A very brief exercise of Mr. Sike’s art, sufficed to overcome the fastening of the lattice; and it soon stood wide open also.
那是一个小巧的格子窗，大约离地五尺半的高度，在屋子的后面。这扇窗是通往走廊尽头的小厨房或说是酿酒间的。窗户开口很小，里面的人可能认为没必要加固防护；但对于奥利弗这样大小的男孩来说，却足够大的了。赛克斯先生施展了他一贯的“绝技”，很快就破解了格子窗的锁；然后窗户也大大地敞开了。

A very brief exercise of Mr. Sike’s art, sufficed to overcome the fastening of the lattice; and it soon stood wide open also.

source: “He would come back—I knew he would,” said the old lady, holding him in her arms. “How well he looks, and how like a gentleman’s son he is dressed again! Where have you been, this long, long while? Ah! the same sweet face, but not so pale; the same soft eye, but not so sad. I have never forgotten them or his quiet smile, but have seen them every day, side by side with those of my own dear children, dead and gone since I was a lightsome young creature.” Running on thus, and now holding Oliver from her to mark how he had grown, now clasping him to her and passing her fingers fondly through his hair, the good soul laughed and wept upon his neck by turns.
domestication: “他一定会回来的，我早就知道了，”老太太一边说，一边紧紧抱着他。“看看他现在有多精神，穿的像个富家少爷一样！你这一走就是这么久，这是怎么了？啊，还是那张可爱的脸，但不再那么苍白；还是那双温柔的眼睛，但不再那么悲伤。我一直没忘记它们，也没忘记他的安静的微笑，每天都在我脑海中浮现，和我自己的孩子们并肩而立，他们都已经去世了，从我还是个年轻人时开始。”老太太絮絮叨叨地说着，时而把奥利弗推开看他长高了多少，时而又把他抱紧，爱抚地把手指插进他的头发里，高兴得笑，伤感得哭，在他的脖子上笑着哭着，反复几次。
foreignization: “他会回来的——我知道他会回来，”老太太抱着他说。“他看起来有多好啊，又像个有身份的孩子一样打扮！你这些很长、很长的时间都去哪儿了？ 啊！同样的可爱面容，但不那么苍白；同样的柔软的眼睛，但不那么悲伤。我从来没有忘记他们，也没有忘记他宁静的微笑，我每天都看到他们，与我自己的亲爱的孩子们并排出现，他们在我还是个年轻女孩时就已经死去了。” 就这样继续着，她现在把奥利弗推开一些距离，看他长高了多少，现在又把他搂近并把手指亲切地穿过他的头发，这位好心肠的人在他的脖子上边哭边笑。

source: “Look at his togs, Fagin!” said Charley, putting the light so close to his new jacket as nearly to set him on fire. “Look at his togs! Superfine cloth, and the heavy swell cut! Oh, my eye, what a game! And his books, too! Nothing but a gentleman, Fagin!”
domestication: “快看他的穿戴，费金！”查理说着，把灯光照得离他新夹克非常近，差点把他烧着。“看看他的穿戴！精细布料，剪裁得像个大亨！我的天哪，这小子真有两下子！还有他的书呢，什么都像是绅士的样子，费金！”
foreignization: “看看他的衣服，费金！”查理说着，把灯光照到他新夹克上，几乎要把他点着火。“看看他的衣服！精细的布料，华丽的款式！哦，我的天哪，这是什么样的生活！还有他的书籍！一点也不像普通人，费金！”

source: Fagin nodded to him to take no further notice just then; and, in a few minutes, the girl subsided into her accustomed demeanour. Whispering Sikes that there was no fear of her relapsing, Fagin took up his hat and bade him good-night. He paused when he reached the room-door, and looking round, asked if somebody would light him down the dark stairs.
domestication: 法金朝他点了点头，示意他暂时别理会；一会儿工夫，那女孩又恢复了平常的样子。法金悄悄地对赛克斯说没事了，不用担心她会再发作，然后拿起帽子，跟他告别。走到门口，法金停下脚步，四周环顾了一下，请问有人能帮他在黑暗的楼梯上照亮一下。
foreignization: 法金点头示意他暂时不必理会；过了几分钟，姑娘恢复到了她通常的神态。法金在小声嘱咐赛克斯别担心她会旧病复发之后，拿起帽子向他道了晚安。他走到房间门口，停下来回头问有谁能帮他点灯照亮黑暗的楼梯。

source: “Not for the world,” replied Fagin. “Are you mad, my dear, stark mad, that you’d walk into the very place where—No, Charley, no. One is enough to lose at a time.”
domestication: “打死也不，”费金回答道，“你怎么了，亲爱的，你是不是疯了，竟然想走进那个地方？不行，查理，不行。一次失去一个已经够惨的了。”
foreignization: “不为全世界，”费金回答道。“你疯了吗，亲爱的，你难道是发疯了才会走进正是——不，查理，不。一次丢一个就足够了。”

source: “Yes, I am,” replied the man, who seemed a little the worse—or better, as the case might be—for drinking; “and not slow about it neither. My horse hasn’t got a load behind him going back, as he had coming up in the mornin’; and he won’t be long a-doing of it. Here’s luck to him. Ecod! he’s a good ’un!”
domestication: “是啊，我就是，”那个男人回答道，他看起来好像喝了点酒，但脸上却带着一副乐呵呵的表情。“我绝不会慢悠悠的，我的马回去的时候没那么多负担，不像早上来的时候那么辛苦，它很快就会休息好了。祝它好运，老天爷保佑！这个家伙真是好马一匹！”
foreignization: “是的，我是，”那个男人回答道，他似乎有点儿像喝了酒后，或许情况如此；“而且速度也不慢。我马背上回去时没有负载，就像早晨上来时一样；他不会花太长时间。祝他好运，天哪！他是个好马！”

source: “Not always those,” said the old gentleman, patting Oliver on the head, and smiling as he did so; “there are other equally heavy ones, though of a much smaller size. How should you like to grow up a clever man, and write books, eh?”

foreignization: “并不总是那些，”那位老先生说着，在奥利弗头上轻拍了一下，并且这么做时脸上露出微笑；“还有其他同样沉重的，但大小要小得多的东西。你将来想成为一个聪明人，写书吗？”

“Not always those,” said the old gentleman, patting Oliver on the head, and smiling as he did so; “there are other equally heavy ones, though of a much smaller size. How should you like to grow up a clever man, and write books, eh?”
这位老先生摸了摸奥利弗的头，面带微笑地说：“并不是这样的，孩子。还有其他同样重要的差事，只是体力消耗相对较小罢了。你要是能长大了成为个博学的男人，为世人著书立说，那该多好啊？”

source: “In that way of business,” rejoined Fagin; “and so are the people of the house. You’ve hit the right nail upon the head, and are as safe here as you could be. There is not a safer place in all this town than is the Cripples; that is, when I like to make it so. And I have taken a fancy to you and the young woman; so I’ve said the word, and you may make your minds easy.”
domestication: "在这行里，" 费金回答说，“房子里的主人也一样。你说对了，我们这里很安全，就像你在自己家一样。整个镇上没有比这里更安全的地方了，当然，这得看我高兴不高兴。而我现在挺喜欢你们俩，所以我已经吩咐过了，你们可以放心了。"
foreignization: “在这种生意中，”法金回答道；“这所房子里的那些人也是如此。你说得对，这里你是安全的。你不可能在这个镇上找到比克里普尔斯更安全的地方，至少当我想让它变得安全的时候是这样的。而且我已经喜欢上了你和那个年轻女人，所以我已经说了话，你们可以放心了。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“In that way of business,” rejoined Fagin; “and so are the people of the house. You’ve hit the right nail upon the head, and are as safe here as you could be. There is not a safer place in all this town than is the Cripples; that is, when I like to make it so. And I have taken a fancy to you and the young woman; so I’ve said the word, and you may make your minds easy.”
"在这行里，" 费金回答说，“房子里的主人也一样。你说对了，我们这里很安全，就像你在自己家一样。整个镇上没有比这里更安全的地方了，当然，这得看我高兴不高兴。而我现在挺喜欢你们俩，所以我已经吩咐过了，你们可以放心了。"

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“In that way of business,” rejoined Fagin; “and so are the people of the house. You’ve hit the right nail upon the head, and are as safe here as you could be. There is not a safer place in all this town than is the Cripples; that is, when I like to make it so. And I have taken a fancy to you and the young woman; so I’ve said the word, and you may make your minds easy.”
“在这种生意中，”法金回答道；“这所房子里的那些人也是如此。你说得对，这里你是安全的。你不可能在这个镇上找到比克里普尔斯更安全的地方，至少当我想让它变得安全的时候是这样的。而且我已经喜欢上了你和那个年轻女人，所以我已经说了话，你们可以放心了。”

source: Apparently, the dog had been somewhat deceived by Mr. Fagin’s outer garment; for as the Jew unbuttoned it, and threw it over the back of a chair, he retired to the corner from which he had risen: wagging his tail as he went, to show that he was as well satisfied as it was in his nature to be.
domestication: 看来，这只狗被费金先生的外套骗了；因为当这位犹太人解开扣子，把外套扔到椅背上时，狗退回到他站起来的角落：离开时，它摇着尾巴，表明它已经非常满意了。其实，这只狗只是看到那件外套就以为是主人，没想到里面竟然是个坏蛋！
foreignization: 显然，这只狗被芬根先生的外套骗了一些；因为当犹太人解开外套的纽扣，并将它披在一把椅子的背上时，他退回到他站起来的那个角落：边走边摇着尾巴，以表明他已经完全满意，正如他的天性所能做到的那样。

source: Oliver looked at Sikes, in mute and timid wonder; and drawing a stool to the fire, sat with his aching head upon his hands, scarcely knowing where he was, or what was passing around him.
domestication: 奥利弗瞪大眼睛惊讶地看着赛克斯，神情既好奇又害怕。他拖着沉重的步子搬来一张凳子，坐在火炉旁边，把疼痛的头埋在手里。他已经迷糊得不知道自己身在何处，也不知道周围发生了什么事。就好像做了一场大梦，恍恍惚惚的。他茫然地坐在那里，脑子里一片混乱。
foreignization: 奥利弗用默默的、胆怯的惊奇目光看着赛克斯，拖了一张凳子到火边，坐下后把疼痛的头靠在手上，他几乎不知道自己在哪里，也不知道周围发生了什么事。

source: “Then I’ll whop yer when I get in,” said the voice; “you just see if I don’t, that’s all, my work’us brat!” and having made this obliging promise, the voice began to whistle.
domestication: 那声音又说，“等我进来的时候就揍你，看我是否做不到，没出息的东西！”说完之后，那声音开始吹口哨了。
foreignization: "然后我进去后就会揍你，”那个声音说，“你等着瞧吧，如果我不揍你才怪呢，你这个穷小子！”说完这句好听的话后，声音开始吹口哨。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Then I’ll whop yer when I get in,” said the voice; “you just see if I don’t, that’s all, my work’us brat!” and having made this obliging promise, the voice began to whistle.
那声音又说，“等我进来的时候就揍你，看我是否做不到，没出息的东西！”说完之后，那声音开始吹口哨了。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Then I’ll whop yer when I get in,” said the voice; “you just see if I don’t, that’s all, my work’us brat!” and having made this obliging promise, the voice began to whistle.
"然后我进去后就会揍你，”那个声音说，“你等着瞧吧，如果我不揍你才怪呢，你这个穷小子！”说完这句好听的话后，声音开始吹口哨。

source: Weak with recent illness; stupified by the blows and the suddenness of the attack; terrified by the fierce growling of the dog, and the brutality of the man; overpowered by the conviction of the bystanders that he really was the hardened little wretch he was described to be; what could one poor child do! Darkness had set in; it was a low neighbourhood; no help was near; resistance was useless. In another moment he was dragged into a labyrinth of dark narrow courts, and was forced along them at a pace which rendered the few cries he dared to give utterance to, unintelligible. It was of little moment, indeed, whether they were intelligible or no; for there was nobody to care for them, had they been ever so plain.
domestication: 这孩子最近生病刚好，挨了打又遭突袭，脑子都糊涂了。那个狗叫得那么凶猛，人也那么蛮横，吓得他浑身发抖。周围的人都说他是个顽劣的孩子，他自己也信了，觉得无从辩解。这时天已经黑了，所在的是一个贫穷肮脏的街区，四周一个人影都没有，毫无援助可寻，反抗也没有用。就这样，他被拖进了一片漆黑狭窄的小巷，走得飞快，他敢发出几声微弱的呼救，也被淹没在脚步声中。就算他的哭喊再清楚，也没有人会理睬他。
foreignization: 他因最近的疾病而虚弱；被打击和突然袭击吓得发呆；被狗的凶猛咆哮和男子的残暴吓得恐惧；被旁观者认为他确实是被描述的那种顽劣的小流氓的想法所压倒；一个贫穷的孩子能做什么呢？ 黑暗已经降临；这是一个低矮的社区；附近没有帮助；抵抗是无用的。 在另一刻，他被拖入一个黑暗狭窄的小巷的迷宫中，以一种使得他敢于发出几声哭喊的速度强迫着他穿过小巷。 这本身无关紧要，究竟他们是否可以理解； 因为即使他们再明白，也没有人会关心。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Weak with recent illness; stupified by the blows and the suddenness of the attack; terrified by the fierce growling of the dog, and the brutality of the man; overpowered by the conviction of the bystanders that he really was the hardened little wretch he was described to be; what could one poor child do! Darkness had set in; it was a low neighbourhood; no help was near; resistance was useless. In another moment he was dragged into a labyrinth of dark narrow courts, and was forced along them at a pace which rendered the few cries he dared to give utterance to, unintelligible. It was of little moment, indeed, whether they were intelligible or no; for there was nobody to care for them, had they been ever so plain.
这孩子最近生病刚好，挨了打又遭突袭，脑子都糊涂了。那个狗叫得那么凶猛，人也那么蛮横，吓得他浑身发抖。周围的人都说他是个顽劣的孩子，他自己也信了，觉得无从辩解。这时天已经黑了，所在的是一个贫穷肮脏的街区，四周一个人影都没有，毫无援助可寻，反抗也没有用。就这样，他被拖进了一片漆黑狭窄的小巷，走得飞快，他敢发出几声微弱的呼救，也被淹没在脚步声中。就算他的哭喊再清楚，也没有人会理睬他。

Weak with recent illness; stupified by the blows and the suddenness of the attack; terrified by the fierce growling of the dog, and the brutality of the man; overpowered by the conviction of the bystanders that he really was the hardened little wretch he was described to be; what could one poor child do! Darkness had set in; it was a low neighbourhood; no help was near; resistance was useless. In another moment he was dragged into a labyrinth of dark narrow courts, and was forced along them at a pace which rendered the few cries he dared to give utterance to, unintelligible. It was of little moment, indeed, whether they were intelligible or no; for there was nobody to care for them, had they been ever so plain.

他因最近的疾病而虚弱；被打击和突然袭击吓得发呆；被狗的凶猛咆哮和男子的残暴吓得恐惧；被旁观者认为他确实是被描述的那种顽劣的小流氓的想法所压倒；一个贫穷的孩子能做什么呢？ 黑暗已经降临；这是一个低矮的社区；附近没有帮助；抵抗是无用的。 在另一刻，他被拖入一个黑暗狭窄的小巷的迷宫中，以一种使得他敢于发出几声哭喊的速度强迫着他穿过小巷。 这本身无关紧要，究竟他们是否可以理解； 因为即使他们再明白，也没有人会关心。

source: The room was illuminated by two gas-lights; the glare of which was prevented by the barred shutters, and closely-drawn curtains of faded red, from being visible outside. The ceiling was blackened, to prevent its colour from being injured by the flaring of the lamps; and the place was so full of dense tobacco smoke, that at first it was scarcely possible to discern anything more. By degrees, however, as some of it cleared away through the open door, an assemblage of heads, as confused as the noises that greeted the ear, might be made out; and as the eye grew more accustomed to the scene, the spectator gradually became aware of the presence of a numerous company, male and female, crowded round a long table: at the upper end of which, sat a chairman with a hammer of office in his hand; while a professional gentleman with a bluish nose, and his face tied up for the benefit of a toothache, presided at a jingling piano in a remote corner.
domestication: 屋里亮着两盏煤气灯，但由于百叶窗和深红色的厚窗帘紧密地遮挡着，灯光从外面是看不见的。天花板也被弄黑了，以免煤气灯的强光伤害它；整个房间里充满了浓厚的烟雾，刚开始几乎什么都看不见。等到一些烟雾通过敞开的门散去后，一片杂乱的头发映入眼帘，房间里的喧闹声也让人感到混乱不堪。但是随着视力逐渐适应，人群中渐渐出现了一张张模糊的面孔。很快，你就能发现一大群男女围坐在一张长桌旁，在桌子的上方，一位手持法槌的主席坐在那里，而在房间的远角，一位戴着口罩、患有牙痛的专业音乐家正在钢琴旁演奏，发出叮当作响的声音。
foreignization: 这间房间被两盏煤气灯照亮；由于有铁栏杆窗户和紧闭的褪色红色窗帘，灯光没有从外面透出来。天花板是黑色的，这样可以防止煤气灯闪烁时损伤天花板的颜色；这地方充满了浓厚的烟雾，刚开始几乎看不清其他东西。但是，随着一些烟雾通过敞开的门散去，一堆头像迎接耳朵的声音一样混乱地出现；当眼睛更加习惯了这个场景时，观察者逐渐意识到一大群男女围绕在一张长桌子周围：在桌子的上端，一位带着手中法槌的主席就座；而一位职业人士，他的鼻子呈蓝色，脸因为牙痛而打着绷带，在远处一个角落里的一架叮当作响的钢琴前主持着。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The room was illuminated by two gas-lights; the glare of which was prevented by the barred shutters, and closely-drawn curtains of faded red, from being visible outside. The ceiling was blackened, to prevent its colour from being injured by the flaring of the lamps; and the place was so full of dense tobacco smoke, that at first it was scarcely possible to discern anything more. By degrees, however, as some of it cleared away through the open door, an assemblage of heads, as confused as the noises that greeted the ear, might be made out; and as the eye grew more accustomed to the scene, the spectator gradually became aware of the presence of a numerous company, male and female, crowded round a long table: at the upper end of which, sat a chairman with a hammer of office in his hand; while a professional gentleman with a bluish nose, and his face tied up for the benefit of a toothache, presided at a jingling piano in a remote corner.

屋里亮着两盏煤气灯，但由于百叶窗和深红色的厚窗帘紧密地遮挡着，灯光从外面是看不见的。天花板也被弄黑了，以免煤气灯的强光伤害它；整个房间里充满了浓厚的烟雾，刚开始几乎什么都看不见。等到一些烟雾通过敞开的门散去后，一片杂乱的头发映入眼帘，房间里的喧闹声也让人感到混乱不堪。但是随着视力逐渐适应，人群中渐渐出现了一张张模糊的面孔。很快，你就能发现一大群男女围坐在一张长桌旁，在桌子的上方，一位手持法槌的主席坐在那里，而在房间的远角，一位戴着口罩、患有牙痛的专业音乐家正在钢琴旁演奏，发出叮当作响的声音。

source: “When ladies as young, and good, and beautiful as you are,” replied the girl steadily, “give away your hearts, love will carry you all lengths—even such as you, who have home, friends, other admirers, everything, to fill them. When such as I, who have no certain roof but the coffinlid, and no friend in sickness or death but the hospital nurse, set our rotten hearts on any man, and let him fill the place that has been a blank through all our wretched lives, who can hope to cure us? Pity us, lady—pity us for having only one feeling of the woman left, and for having that turned, by a heavy judgment, from a comfort and a pride, into a new means of violence and suffering.”
domestication: “像你这样年轻、善良、美丽的女孩，”那个姑娘镇定地回答道，“当你们把心交给别人时，爱情会带你们走向任何地方——即使是像你们这样，有家有朋友，有众多仰慕者，生活充满了幸福的人。当像我这样的人，没有确定的归宿，只有棺材板，没有任何朋友可以在病痛和死亡时陪伴，只有医院的护士；当我们这些不幸的人，把已经空白了整个一生的位置，填上某个男人，并让他成为我们唯一的依靠；谁还能指望改变我们呢？可怜我们吧，女士——可怜我们只剩下了一点女性的感受，却被残酷的命运扭曲成了新的折磨和痛苦。”
foreignization: 当像您一样年轻、善良、美丽的女士们，”那个女孩稳定地回答说，“把你们的心交给别人时，爱情会带领你们走向任何地方——即使是你们拥有家园、朋友、其他仰慕者、以及一切可以填满你们生命的东西。当像我这样的人，没有确定的屋顶只有棺材盖，没有在生病或死亡时的朋友只有医院护士，我们将我们腐烂的心放在某个男人身上，并让他填补我们悲惨生活中一直空白的地方，谁能希望治愈我们？可怜我们，夫人——可怜我们仅剩下一种女性的感觉，并且因为一个沉重的判决，而将这种感觉从安慰和自豪转变为新的暴力和痛苦的手段。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“When ladies as young, and good, and beautiful as you are,” replied the girl steadily, “give away your hearts, love will carry you all lengths—even such as you, who have home, friends, other admirers, everything, to fill them. When such as I, who have no certain roof but the coffinlid, and no friend in sickness or death but the hospital nurse, set our rotten hearts on any man, and let him fill the place that has been a blank through all our wretched lives, who can hope to cure us? Pity us, lady—pity us for having only one feeling of the woman left, and for having that turned, by a heavy judgment, from a comfort and a pride, into a new means of violence and suffering.”

source: Jingling and clattering, till distance rendered its noise inaudible, and its rapid progress only perceptible to the eye, the vehicle wound its way along the road, almost hidden in a cloud of dust: now wholly disappearing, and now becoming visible again, as intervening objects, or the intricacies of the way, permitted. It was not until even the dusty cloud was no longer to be seen, that the gazers dispersed.
domestication: 叮当作响、铿锵有声，声音越来越远，渐渐听不见了，只有车子飞速行驶的模样还能让人看得一清二楚。这辆马车卷起一片尘土，沿着路蜿蜒前行，有时被树木或房屋遮挡住，看不见了；有时又出现眼前，依然裹在漫天灰尘中。直到最后连那片尘云也消失得无影无踪，围观的人才逐渐散去。
foreignization: 叮当作响和乒乓直响，直到距离使得它的噪音听不见了，它的快速行进只能被眼睛所感知，这辆车沿着路蜿蜒而行，几乎被一团尘云遮挡：现在完全消失，然后当中间的物体，或道路的曲折之处允许时，又变得可见。直到甚至那团尘云也看不到了，围观的人才分散开来。

It was not until even the dusty cloud was no longer to be seen, that the gazers dispersed.

source: “Yes, yes, I will, to say good-b’ye to you,” replied Oliver. “I shall see you again, Dick. I know I shall! You will be well and happy!”
domestication: “好吧，好吧，我走了，先跟你说声再见吧，”奥利弗回答道。“我一定会再见到你的，迪克。我坚信这一点！你会过得很好，生活会很幸福！”

source: “We are very glad to see you, Oliver, very,” said the Jew. “Dodger, take off the sausages; and draw a tub near the fire for Oliver. Ah, you’re a-staring at the pocket-handkerchiefs! eh, my dear? There are a good many of ’em, ain’t there? We’ve just looked ’em out, ready for the wash; that’s all, Oliver; that’s all. Ha! ha! ha!”
domestication: “奥利弗，很高兴见到你，非常高兴，”那个犹太人说。“道格尔，把香肠拿开；给奥利弗在火边放个脸盆。啊，你在看那些手帕吧？哎呀，有不少吧？我们刚刚整理出来，准备洗的，这就是全部，奥利弗，只是这样而已。哈哈哈！”
foreignization: “我们非常高兴见到你，奥利弗，非常高兴，”犹太人说。“多奇尔，拿下香肠，并且在火边为奥利弗拉开一桶热水。啊，你在盯着口袋手帕，嗯，我的亲爱的？有很多，对吧？我们刚刚把它们找出来，准备洗涤，这就是全部了，奥利弗，只是这样。哈！哈！哈！

source: “Why, it’s what I’m obliged to keep a little of in the house, to put into the blessed infants’ Daffy, when they ain’t well, Mr. Bumble,” replied Mrs. Mann as she opened a corner cupboard, and took down a bottle and glass. “It’s gin. I’ll not deceive you, Mr. B. It’s gin.”
domestication: 唉，这东西我得在家里备点儿，以备那些可怜的小家伙病了的时候，加点儿在他们的达菲药水里，，曼夫人边说边打开角落里的一个小柜子，拿出一瓶酒和一个酒杯。“这可是威士忌呢，我不会骗你的，班布尔先生。这东西就是威士忌。”
foreignization: “这是我不得不在家里存放一点儿的东西，用于给可怜的小婴儿的达菲药水，当他们生病时，班布尔先生，”曼夫人回答道，同时打开了角落里的一个橱柜，拿下了一瓶酒和一只玻璃杯。“这是一种杜松子酒。我不会骗你，B先生。这是杜松子酒。”

source: Oliver’s ailings were neither slight nor few. In addition to the pain and delay attendant on a broken limb, his exposure to the wet and cold had brought on fever and ague: which hung about him for many weeks, and reduced him sadly. But, at length, he began, by slow degrees, to get better, and to be able to say sometimes, in a few tearful words, how deeply he felt the goodness of the two sweet ladies, and how ardently he hoped that when he grew strong and well again, he could do something to show his gratitude; only something, which would let them see the love and duty with which his breast was full; something, however slight, which would prove to them that their gentle kindness had not been cast away; but that the poor boy whom their charity had rescued from misery, or death, was eager to serve them with his whole heart and soul.
domestication: 奥利弗的病痛不轻也不少。他不仅因骨折而遭受疼痛和延误，而且由于湿冷，引发了高烧和发作性寒战，这些症状一直困扰他好几周，让他虚弱得可怜。但是，终于，他开始慢慢地好转，有时候他含着眼泪说出几个字，表达他对那两位好心女士的感激之情，以及当他恢复健康后，他希望能做点什么来报答她们的恩情。无论是什么，只要能让她们看到他满怀的爱和责任感；只要能向她们证明，她们的仁慈并没有白费，而被她们从苦难或死亡中拯救出来的穷孩子，急切地希望以全部的心和灵魂来侍奉她们。
foreignization: 奥利弗的病痛不轻也不少。他不仅遭受了骨折带来的疼痛和延误，还因为暴露在风雨中而患上了发烧和打颤：这使他数周无法痊愈，并使他非常痛苦。但是，终于，他开始慢慢地好转，有时能说出几句话，含泪表达他对两位好心女士的感激之情，当他恢复健康时，他希望能够做些什么来表示他的感激；只要有一点点事情，就能让他们看到他满怀的爱和责任；只要有一点点事情，就能证明他们的仁慈没有白费；那个他们慈善救了的可怜男孩，从苦难或死亡中拯救出来的人，渴望用他全部的心灵和灵魂来侍奉他们。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Oliver’s ailings were neither slight nor few. In addition to the pain and delay attendant on a broken limb, his exposure to the wet and cold had brought on fever and ague: which hung about him for many weeks, and reduced him sadly. But, at length, he began, by slow degrees, to get better, and to be able to say sometimes, in a few tearful words, how deeply he felt the goodness of the two sweet ladies, and how ardently he hoped that when he grew strong and well again, he could do something to show his gratitude; only something, which would let them see the love and duty with which his breast was full; something, however slight, which would prove to them that their gentle kindness had not been cast away; but that the poor boy whom their charity had rescued from misery, or death, was eager to serve them with his whole heart and soul.

奥利弗的病痛不轻也不少。他不仅因骨折而遭受疼痛和延误，而且由于湿冷，引发了高烧和发作性寒战，这些症状一直困扰他好几周，让他虚弱得可怜。但是，终于，他开始慢慢地好转，有时候他含着眼泪说出几个字，表达他对那两位好心女士的感激之情，以及当他恢复健康后，他希望能做点什么来报答她们的恩情。无论是什么，只要能让她们看到他满怀的爱和责任感；只要能向她们证明，她们的仁慈并没有白费，而被她们从苦难或死亡中拯救出来的穷孩子，急切地希望以全部的心和灵魂来侍奉她们。

Translate the following text to Chinese.

Oliver’s ailings were neither slight nor few. In addition to the pain and delay attendant on a broken limb, his exposure to the wet and cold had brought on fever and ague: which hung about him for many weeks, and reduced him sadly. But, at length, he began, by slow degrees, to get better, and to be able to say sometimes, in a few tearful words, how deeply he felt the goodness of the two sweet ladies, and how ardently he hoped that when he grew strong and well again, he could do something to show his gratitude; only something, which would let them see the love and duty with which his breast was full; something, however slight, which would prove to them that their gentle kindness had not been cast away; but that the poor boy whom their charity had rescued from misery, or death, was eager to serve them with his whole heart and soul.

奥利弗的病痛不轻也不少。他不仅遭受了骨折带来的疼痛和延误，还因为暴露在风雨中而患上了发烧和打颤：这使他数周无法痊愈，并使他非常痛苦。但是，终于，他开始慢慢地好转，有时能说出几句话，含泪表达他对两位好心女士的感激之情，当他恢复健康时，他希望能够做些什么来表示他的感激；只要有一点点事情，就能让他们看到他满怀的爱和责任；只要有一点点事情，就能证明他们的仁慈没有白费；那个他们慈善救了的可怜男孩，从苦难或死亡中拯救出来的人，渴望用他全部的心灵和灵魂来侍奉他们。

source: The broad sky seemed on fire. Rising into the air with showers of sparks, and rolling one above the other, were sheets of flame, lighting the atmosphere for miles round, and driving clouds of smoke in the direction where he stood. The shouts grew louder as new voices swelled the roar, and he could hear the cry of Fire! mingled with the ringing of an alarm-bell, the fall of heavy bodies, and the crackling of flames as they twined round some new obstacle, and shot aloft as though refreshed by food. The noise increased as he looked. There were people there—men and women—light, bustle. It was like new life to him. He darted onward—straight, headlong—dashing through brier and brake, and leaping gate and fence as madly as his dog, who careered with loud and sounding bark before him.
domestication: 天空仿佛着了火，一片片火焰升腾到空中，带着一串串火花，像滚雪球似的一个接着一个，照亮了四周好几里的地面，将浓烟推向他站立的方向。喊叫声越来越大，更多的声音加入进来，他能听到“着火了”的呼喊声混杂着警钟的铃声，沉重物体的倒塌声，以及火焰缠绕在新的障碍物上发出啪啪的响声，好像获得了新生一样直冲云霄。声音越来越大，当他望去时。他看到了人——男人和女人，到处都是灯光、忙碌的身影。这对他来说就像注入了新生命。他猛地向前冲去，头也不回，像他的狗一样疯狂地朝前奔跑，狗在前面满口汪汪叫着。
foreignization: 宽阔的天空似乎着了火。带着火花飞溅而起，一个又一个的火焰卷翻滚上去，照亮了四周数英里的空气，并且驱赶着烟雾朝他站立的方向飘去。喊声变得更大，因为新的声音加入到了震耳欲聋的混乱中，他可以听到“着火了！”的呼喊声与警报钟声交织在一起，还有重物倒地的撞击声和火焰环绕在新的障碍物上时的噼啪声，好像被食物所滋养，冲天而起。噪音在他注视的同时越来越大，有人在那里——男人和女人——光明，熙熙攘攘，对他来说就像新生一般。他飞速向前冲去——笔直，头也不回——疯狂地穿过荆棘丛和矮树丛，像他的狗一样跳过门和篱笆，他的狗在他面前横冲直撞，吠叫声响亮而有节奏。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The broad sky seemed on fire. Rising into the air with showers of sparks, and rolling one above the other, were sheets of flame, lighting the atmosphere for miles round, and driving clouds of smoke in the direction where he stood. The shouts grew louder as new voices swelled the roar, and he could hear the cry of Fire! mingled with the ringing of an alarm-bell, the fall of heavy bodies, and the crackling of flames as they twined round some new obstacle, and shot aloft as though refreshed by food. The noise increased as he looked. There were people there—men and women—light, bustle. It was like new life to him. He darted onward—straight, headlong—dashing through brier and brake, and leaping gate and fence as madly as his dog, who careered with loud and sounding bark before him.

source: “You think women never can keep secrets, I suppose?” said the matron, interposing, and returning, as she spoke, the searching look of Monks.
domestication: 这位女看护插话道：“你是觉得女人嘴巴不牢吧？”她边说边把目光投向蒙克斯，脸上满是质疑的神色。

source: The dog, though. If any description of him were out, it would not be forgotten that the dog was missing, and had probably gone with him. This might lead to his apprehension as he passed along the streets. He resolved to drown him, and walked on, looking about for a pond: picking up a heavy stone and tying it to his handkerchief as he went.
domestication: 这狗可就是另一回事了。要是有人发表他的描述，大家肯定会注意到少了这条狗，并且可能会猜测狗跟他在一起。这样的话，他一旦走到街上，就有可能被抓住。于是，他下定决心要把狗弄死，就边走边找水塘。他边走边捡起一块大石头，边用手帕包起来准备使用。
foreignization: 然而，那只狗怎么办。如果有人描述他，人们不会忘记那只狗也失踪了，很可能是跟着他离开的。这可能会导致他被人认出，当他走在街上时。他决定把狗淹死，然后继续走着，寻找一个池塘边：一边走，一边捡起一块重石头并把它绑在他的手帕上。

source: “I had serious thoughts of eating my head tonight,” said Mr. Grimwig, “for I began to think I should get nothing else. I’ll take the liberty, if you’ll allow me, of saluting the bride that is to be.”
domestication: 格林威格先生说：“今天晚上我真是想一头撞死，我都快以为今晚会吃不到东西了。请允许我行个礼吧，向即将结婚的新娘表示敬意。”
foreignization: “我今晚曾经认真地想过要吃掉自己的头，”格林威格先生说，“因为我开始以为我什么也得不到。我想，如果你允许的话，我要冒昧地向即将成为新娘的人表示敬意。”

source: Many of the shops were already closing in the back lanes and avenues through which she tracked her way, in making from Spitalfields towards the West-End of London. The clock struck ten, increasing her impatience. She tore along the narrow pavement: elbowing the passengers from side to side; and darting almost under the horses’ heads, crossed crowded streets, where clusters of persons were eagerly watching their opportunity to do the like.
domestication: 她穿过一条条后街和小巷，朝伦敦西区走去。当时，很多店铺已经开始打烊了。钟声敲响十点，她的不耐烦越来越强烈。她在狭窄的人行道上快速前进，一边走一边撞开行人，几乎贴着马头底下飞快地穿过拥挤的街道，路上一群群的人正急切地等待着过马路的机会。
foreignization: 她穿过的后街和通往伦敦西区的道路上，很多商店已经开始打烊了。她越发感到焦躁。钟声敲响十点，十点的钟声又增加了她的焦急。她飞快地沿着狭窄的人行道走着，用肘部把路人从一边撞到另一边；几乎贴近马头，横穿拥挤的街道，那里聚集了一群人，他们正热切地等待着过马路的机会。

source: Fagin laid his hand upon the bundle, and locking it in the cupboard, sat down again without speaking. But he did not take his eyes off the robber, for an instant, during this action; and now that they sat over against each other, face to face, he looked fixedly at him, with his lips quivering so violently, and his face so altered by the emotions which had mastered him, that the housebreaker involuntarily drew back his chair, and surveyed him with a look of real affright.
domestication: 法金把手放在那个包裹上，将它锁进柜子，然后又坐了回去，一句话也没说。但是，他的眼睛却一直没有离开那个小偷，即使在他做这些动作的时候也是如此。现在，他们面对面地坐在那里，他紧紧地盯着小偷，嘴唇因为激动得颤抖不已，脸色也变得十分难看，被情绪所支配。小偷不禁往后退了退椅子，带着真正的恐惧看着他。
foreignization: 法金把他的手放在了那个包上，并且锁在了橱柜里，然后坐了回去，却没有说话。但是他一瞬间都没有将视线从小偷身上移开。在他进行这些动作的时候，他现在正和对方面对面地坐在那里，他用颤抖的嘴唇，脸上也被他所遭受的情绪改变了这样一个表情来凝视着对方，小偷不由自主地往后拉开了他的椅子，用真正害怕的眼神打量着他。

source: “Now, young ’un!” said Sikes, looking up at the clock of St. Andrew’s Church, “hard upon seven! you must step out. Come, don’t lag behind already, Lazy-legs!”
domestication: “好了，小子！”赛克斯抬头看了看圣安德鲁教堂的钟楼，“快到七点了，你该走了。快点，别磨蹭，懒骨头！”

source: The servant did not know; but would go and inquire. She presently returned, and said, that Mr. Brownlow had sold off his goods, and gone to the West Indies, six weeks before. Oliver clasped his hands, and sank feebly backward.
domestication: 佣人不知道，但她愿意去打听一下。过了一会儿，她回来了，说布朗洛先生六周前已经卖掉了所有的财物，然后去了西印度群岛。奥利弗听到这个消息后，两手紧握在一起，身子虚弱地向后倒去。
foreignization: 那女佣不知道，但她说她会去打听。 一会儿后，她回来了，说布朗洛先生六周前已经卖掉了他的商品，去了西印度群岛。奥利弗合起双手，虚弱地倒在后面。

foreignization: “你可以离开你的朋友，”奥利弗带着半笑说，“让他们为你所做的事情受到惩罚。”
