"让我看看；他最多二十分钟就会回来，”布朗洛先生说着，掏出他的怀表并把它放在桌子上。“到那时天就黑了。”

source: Morning drew on apace. The air became more sharp and piercing, as its first dull hue—the death of night, rather than the birth of day—glimmered faintly in the sky. The objects which had looked dim and terrible in the darkness, grew more and more defined, and gradually resolved into their familiar shapes. The rain came down, thick and fast, and pattered noisily among the leafless bushes. But, Oliver felt it not, as it beat against him; for he still lay stretched, helpless and unconscious, on his bed of clay.
domestication: 天色渐渐亮了起来，空气变得更加清爽和刺骨。夜幕的黑暗慢慢消散，一丝微弱的光线从天边透出，仿佛是白天的黎明正在缓缓破晓。那些在黑暗中看起来模糊可怕的物体，现在逐渐变得清晰，恢复了它们熟悉的形状。雨下得很大，密密麻麻的雨滴敲打着光秃秃的灌木丛，发出响亮的滴答声。但是，奥利弗却感觉不到，因为他仍然躺在地上，一动不动，毫无知觉，就像被冻住了一样。他已经失去了所有的意识，任凭雨水打在身上，却毫无反应。
foreignization: 早晨迅速地到来了。空气变得更加刺骨和刺耳，随着夜晚的死——而非新的一天的诞生——首先在天空中出现了微弱的苍白色。那些在黑暗中看起来昏暗且可怕的物体，变得越来越清晰，并逐渐恢复到了它们熟悉的形状。雨水倾盆而下，厚重而迅速，发出巨大的声响，打在光秃秃的灌木丛中。但是，奥利弗却没有感觉到它，当雨水猛烈地打在他身上；因为他仍然躺着，毫无反应和意识，躺在他的泥土床上。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Morning drew on apace. The air became more sharp and piercing, as its first dull hue—the death of night, rather than the birth of day—glimmered faintly in the sky. The objects which had looked dim and terrible in the darkness, grew more and more defined, and gradually resolved into their familiar shapes. The rain came down, thick and fast, and pattered noisily among the leafless bushes. But, Oliver felt it not, as it beat against him; for he still lay stretched, helpless and unconscious, on his bed of clay.
天色渐渐亮了起来，空气变得更加清爽和刺骨。夜幕的黑暗慢慢消散，一丝微弱的光线从天边透出，仿佛是白天的黎明正在缓缓破晓。那些在黑暗中看起来模糊可怕的物体，现在逐渐变得清晰，恢复了它们熟悉的形状。雨下得很大，密密麻麻的雨滴敲打着光秃秃的灌木丛，发出响亮的滴答声。但是，奥利弗却感觉不到，因为他仍然躺在地上，一动不动，毫无知觉，就像被冻住了一样。他已经失去了所有的意识，任凭雨水打在身上，却毫无反应。

source: “You wouldn’t mind it again, Tom, would you,” asked the Dodger, winking upon Charley and the Jew, “if Bet was all right?”
domestication: “你不会介意再帮我们一次吧，汤姆，”Dodger眨了眨眼对着查理和那位犹太人说，“只要贝特没事的话。”

source: “And who can wonder if it be so, mother?” rejoined the young man; “or why should I say, if?—It is—it is—you know it, mother—you must know it!”
domestication: “儿子这么说有什么奇怪的呢？”年轻人反驳道，“我干嘛要说‘如果’呢？——事情就是这样，你也清楚，妈妈——你必须清楚才对！”
foreignization: “谁会觉得奇怪呢，母亲？”年轻人回答道，“或者我为什么要说如果呢？——事实就是如此——它确实如此——你知道的，母亲——你必须知道它！”

source: In fact, Mr. Fagin had so well humoured his young friend’s eccentric disposition, that Master Bates, who had at first been disposed to consider the imprisoned Dodger rather in the light of a victim, now looked upon him as the chief actor in a scene of most uncommon and exquisite humour, and felt quite impatient for the arrival of the time when his old companion should have so favourable an opportunity of displaying his abilities.
domestication: 事实上，费金先生已经非常了解他年轻朋友的古怪脾气，所以贝茨少爷一开始还以为被囚禁的多奇尔只是一个受害者，现在却觉得他是整个闹剧的主角，真是令人捧腹和好笑。现在，贝茨少爷已经很期待他的老伙伴能够找到机会展示他的本事。
foreignization: 事实上，费金先生已经很好地迎合了他年轻朋友的古怪性格，所以贝茨先生最初准备把被囚禁的道奇看作受害者，现在却认为道奇是最不寻常和最优美幽默场景中的主要演员，并且他对老搭档将有如此有利的机会展示自己的才能感到非常着急。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

In fact, Mr. Fagin had so well humoured his young friend’s eccentric disposition, that Master Bates, who had at first been disposed to consider the imprisoned Dodger rather in the light of a victim, now looked upon him as the chief actor in a scene of most uncommon and exquisite humour, and felt quite impatient for the arrival of the time when his old companion should have so favourable an opportunity of displaying his abilities.

source: “Well!” said Mr. Bumble, raising his hands and eyes with most impressive solemnity. “Well! of all the artful and designing orphans that ever I see, Oliver, you are one of the most bare-facedest.”
domestication: “天啊！”班布尔先生双手和眼睛都举得高高的，带着最令人肃然起敬的严肃说：“我见过很多狡猾又有心计的小弃儿，但你奥利弗可是最厚颜无耻的了。”
foreignization: “好啊！”邦布尔先生说着，双手和眼睛带着最令人印象深刻的庄严举起来，“好啊！我所见过的所有狡猾而又精心设计的孤儿中，奥利弗，你是最厚颜无耻的一个。”

source: “Save us!” said the old lady, with tears in her eyes. “What a grateful little dear it is. Pretty creetur! What would his mother feel if she had sat by him as I have, and could see him now!”
domestication: 老太太哭着求道：“救救我们吧！”她边说边流泪，嘴里喃喃地说：“这孩子真可爱，真是个小乖乖！要是他妈妈像我一样在他身边照顾他，现在见到他这副样子，得有多伤心啊！”
foreignization: “救救我们！”那个老妇人含着眼泪说。“多么感激的小东西啊，真可爱的东西！如果他的母亲能像我一样坐在他旁边，看见他现在这个样子，她会有什么感受呢！”

messages_domestication.content:

source: The dog no doubt heard; because Mr. Sikes spoke in the very harshest key of a very harsh voice; but, appearing to entertain some unaccountable objection to having his throat cut, he remained where he was, and growled more fiercely than before: at the same time grasping the end of the poker between his teeth, and biting at it like a wild beast.
domestication: 这条狗肯定听见了，因为斯威克斯先生说话的声音很大，语气也很凶狠。但是，它似乎对被割喉有莫名的反感，所以仍然保持着原来的姿势，只是开始更加猛烈地咆哮。同时，它用嘴巴紧紧咬住了火钳的末端，就像一只野兽一样死死咬住不放。
foreignization: 这只狗无疑听到了，因为赛克斯先生用他那很刺耳的声音以最刺耳的调子说话。但是，好像由于他无法解释地反对割自己的喉咙，他留在那里，他的叫声比之前更凶猛，此时他咬住了火钳的一端，用牙齿咬住它，像一只野兽一样咬。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The dog no doubt heard; because Mr. Sikes spoke in the very harshest key of a very harsh voice; but, appearing to entertain some unaccountable objection to having his throat cut, he remained where he was, and growled more fiercely than before: at the same time grasping the end of the poker between his teeth, and biting at it like a wild beast.

source: Oliver was awakened in the morning, by a loud kicking at the outside of the shop-door: which, before he could huddle on his clothes, was repeated, in an angry and impetuous manner, about twenty-five times. When he began to undo the chain, the legs desisted, and a voice began.
domestication: 奥利弗早上被一阵猛烈的敲门声吵醒了，仿佛有人在店门外踢门，声音震耳欲聋。在他还没来得及穿好衣服之前，这种打门声就重复了大约二十五次，简直让人无法忍受。就在他开始解开门链的时候，那些腿终于停下来不动了，取而代之的是一个响亮的声音。
foreignization: 奥利弗在早晨被一阵猛烈地踢店门的动静吵醒了：在他还没来得及急忙穿好衣服之前，这种动静以一种生气而又激动的方式大约重复了二十五次。当他开始解开铁链时，腿不再踢门了，一个声音开始了。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Oliver was awakened in the morning, by a loud kicking at the outside of the shop-door: which, before he could huddle on his clothes, was repeated, in an angry and impetuous manner, about twenty-five times. When he began to undo the chain, the legs desisted, and a voice began.

source: Oliver stirred the fire. Drawing her chair close to it, she sat there, for a little time, without speaking; but at length she raised her head, and looked round.
domestication: 奥利弗搅动了火焰，艾米莉把椅子挪到火边，默默地坐了一会儿，良久，她才抬起头来，环顾四周。
foreignization: 奥利弗搅动着火，奥利弗将她的椅子挪到火边，坐在那里有一段时间都没有说话，但过了一会儿，她抬起头四周张望。

source: “Then,” said the doctor impetuously, “I put it to you again, whether you think it reasonable that this promise to the girl should be considered binding; a promise made with the best and kindest intentions, but really—”
domestication: 于是，医生又说，“我再问你一次，你觉得这个对女孩的承诺应该被认为是有约束力的吗？这个承诺是出于善良和亲切的想法，但是——”
foreignization: "那么，”医生冲动地说道，“我再次问你，你是否认为对女孩的承诺应该被认为是有约束力的；一个带着最好的和最亲切的意图所作出的承诺，但是——”

source: Mr. Fang was a lean, long-backed, stiff-necked, middle-sized man, with no great quantity of hair, and what he had, growing on the back and sides of his head. His face was stern, and much flushed. If he were really not in the habit of drinking rather more than was exactly good for him, he might have brought action against his countenance for libel, and have recovered heavy damages.
domestication: 方先生身材偏瘦，背有点驼，脖子也挺僵硬，算是一个中等身材的人。头发不多，而且只剩下后面和两边还有一些杂乱的头发。他的脸色很严肃，而且脸颊泛红，好像喝多了酒似的。如果他真的不是因为常喝酒才导致脸红，那他都可以控告自己的脸在诽谤他，要求赔偿一大笔钱。
foreignization: 方先生是一个身体瘦长、背部凸出、脖子僵硬、身材中等的人，他头上没有多少头发，而且头发只长在头的后面和两侧。他的脸很严肃，且很红润。如果他真的不是经常喝得比对自己有益的量稍微多一点，那么他可能会因为诽谤而对自己的容貌提出起诉，并获得巨额赔偿。

source: “You that keep this house,” said Sikes, turning his face to Crackit, “do you mean to sell me, or to let me lie here till this hunt is over?”
domestication: “喂，你是这家店的老板吧？” 赛克斯转过头对克拉基特说：“你到底是想卖我还是让我躲在这里等这场风暴过去？”
foreignization: “你这家里的看守者，”赛克斯转过脸对克拉基特说，“你是想出卖我，还是让我躺在这里直到这场追捕结束？”

source: The old gentleman with the spectacles gradually dozed off, over the little bit of parchment; and there was a short pause, after Oliver had been stationed by Mr. Bumble in front of the desk.
domestication: 那位戴着眼镜的老先生慢慢地靠在椅背上，渐渐地睡着了，头歪在那张羊皮纸上。奥利弗被邦布尔先生安排站在桌前，大家都沉默不语，气氛有点尴尬，一时间鸦雀无声。

source: “She goes abroad tonight,” said Fagin, “and on the right errand, I’m sure; for she has been alone all day, and the man she is afraid of will not be back much before daybreak. Come with me. Quick!”
domestication: “她今晚出国，” 法金说，“而且我相信她的任务很对，因为她整天都一个人，而她害怕的那个人直到天亮前都不会回来。跟我来吧，快点！”
foreignization: “她今晚出国，”法金说，“去办正事，我敢肯定；因为她整天都独自一人，她害怕的那个人在黎明前不会回来。跟我来，快点！

messages_domestication.content:

domestication: 这老东西在干什么呢？难道又在欺负那些孩子吧，你这个贪得无厌、贪婪无耻的老奸商！那个男人慢条斯理地坐下，阴阳怪气地说。你说实话，真奇怪他们没把你给宰了！我要是他们，我早就下手了。如果我是你的学徒，我也早就那么干了，但是……不，我做了之后也不能把你卖掉，因为你除了被当成怪物装在玻璃瓶子里供人参观之外，什么正经用都没有。估计玻璃瓶也造不了有多大的，你就是这样一个怪物。

source: Nancy’s appearance gave a new turn to the conversation; for the boys, receiving a sly wink from the wary old Jew, began to ply her with liquor: of which, however, she took very sparingly; while Fagin, assuming an unusual flow of spirits, gradually brought Mr. Sikes into a better temper, by affecting to regard his threats as a little pleasant banter; and, moreover, by laughing very heartily at one or two rough jokes, which, after repeated applications to the spirit-bottle, he condescended to make.
domestication: 南希的到来让话题发生了转折，因为那些男孩接到了犹太老头投来的调皮眼神，开始给她倒酒。不过南希只是象征性地喝了一点。而法金则装出一副十分高兴的样子，逐渐把斯ай克斯的情绪哄好，他假装认为对方的威胁只是开玩笑，还在对方几次劝酒之后，居然屈尊讲了几个粗俗的笑话，并且大声哈哈笑了起来。
foreignization: 南希的到来给谈话带来了新的转折；因为男孩们接受了狡猾的老犹太人的暗示，开始劝她喝酒：然而，她却非常有节制地喝着；而费金假装出一副高兴的样子，逐渐让斯凯克斯先生的情绪变好，假装认为他的威胁只是开玩笑，而且，还对他在反复劝酒后才说出的一个或两个粗俗笑话大笑不止；

source: Noah Claypole, or Morris Bolter as the reader pleases, punctually followed the directions he had received, which—Master Bates being pretty well acquainted with the locality—were so exact that he was enabled to gain the magisterial presence without asking any question, or meeting with any interruption by the way.
domestication: 诺亚·克莱波尔，或说莫里斯·博尔特吧，读者喜欢叫他什么就叫什么，他很守时，按照他收到的指示出发。由于班特斯对这个地方相当熟悉，所以给他的指示非常详细，他完全没遇到任何问题，根本不用问任何人，就径直来到了法官面前。
foreignization: 诺亚·克莱波尔，或莫里斯·博尔特，正如读者所愿，他准时按照他收到的指示进行了行动，这些指示——由于贝茨先生对当地地理环境相当熟悉——非常详细，这样一来他能够在没有询问任何问题或在途中遇到任何障碍的情况下接近法官的办公室。

messages_domestication.content:

source: “Why, that she couldn’t very easily get out of doors unless he knew where she was going to,” said Noah; “and so the first time she went to see the lady, she—ha! ha! ha! it made me laugh when she said it, that it did—she gave him a drink of laudanum.”
domestication: “唉，原来是这样的，”诺亚说，“她如果想出门，就必须让他知道去哪儿；所以第一次去看那位女士时，她干了一件很有意思的事——哈哈哈！我当时听了就忍不住笑！她居然给他来了一剂可卡因，让他睡着了！”
foreignization: “为什么，因为她不能轻易地出门除非他知道她要去哪里，”诺亚说，“因此第一次她去看那位女士时，她——哈！哈！哈！当她说的时候，它让我笑了，就是这样——她给他一口鸦片酒。”

messages_domestication.content:

“Why, that she couldn’t very easily get out of doors unless he knew where she was going to,” said Noah; “and so the first time she went to see the lady, she—ha! ha! ha! it made me laugh when she said it, that it did—she gave him a drink of laudanum.”
“唉，原来是这样的，”诺亚说，“她如果想出门，就必须让他知道去哪儿；所以第一次去看那位女士时，她干了一件很有意思的事——哈哈哈！我当时听了就忍不住笑！她居然给他来了一剂可卡因，让他睡着了！”

“Why, that she couldn’t very easily get out of doors unless he knew where she was going to,” said Noah; “and so the first time she went to see the lady, she—ha! ha! ha! it made me laugh when she said it, that it did—she gave him a drink of laudanum.”
“为什么，因为她不能轻易地出门除非他知道她要去哪里，”诺亚说，“因此第一次她去看那位女士时，她——哈！哈！哈！当她说的时候，它让我笑了，就是这样——她给他一口鸦片酒。”

source: “Why, for what,” said the gentleman in a kinder tone, “for what purpose can you have brought us to this strange place? Why not have let me speak to you, above there, where it is light, and there is something stirring, instead of bringing us to this dark and dismal hole?”
domestication: 这位先生语气缓和了些，问道：“你把我们带到这个奇怪的地方来干什么？为什么不让我在上面跟你说话呢？那里光线充足，热闹非凡，我们何必来到这个黑暗而又阴森的所在？”他问得有理，带我们来到这里到底有什么目的呢？
foreignization: “为什么，为了什么，”那位先生用一种更友善的语气说，“为了什么目的你才把我们带到这个奇怪的地方？为什么不让我在上面跟你说话，那里有光亮，还有一些活动，而不是把我们带到这个黑暗而令人沮丧的洞穴？”

messages_domestication.content:

“Why, for what,” said the gentleman in a kinder tone, “for what purpose can you have brought us to this strange place? Why not have let me speak to you, above there, where it is light, and there is something stirring, instead of bringing us to this dark and dismal hole?”

“为什么，为了什么，”那位先生用一种更友善的语气说，“为了什么目的你才把我们带到这个奇怪的地方？为什么不让我在上面跟你说话，那里有光亮，还有一些活动，而不是把我们带到这个黑暗而令人沮丧的洞穴？”

source: Having listened at the keyhole, to assure himself that nobody was approaching the chamber, Mr. Bumble, beginning at the bottom, proceeded to make himself acquainted with the contents of the three long drawers: which, being filled with various garments of good fashion and texture, carefully preserved between two layers of old newspapers, speckled with dried lavender: seemed to yield him exceeding satisfaction. Arriving, in course of time, at the right-hand corner drawer (in which was the key), and beholding therein a small padlocked box, which, being shaken, gave forth a pleasant sound, as of the chinking of coin, Mr. Bumble returned with a stately walk to the fireplace; and, resuming his old attitude, said, with a grave and determined air, “I’ll do it!” He followed up this remarkable declaration, by shaking his head in a waggish manner for ten minutes, as though he were remonstrating with himself for being such a pleasant dog; and then, he took a view of his legs in profile, with much seeming pleasure and interest.
domestication: 班布尔先生在门口偷听了一会儿，确定没人靠近，他才走进房间，开始从下往上检查三個很大的抽屉。这些抽屉里装满了时髦的衣服，都仔细地夹在两层旧报纸之间，还洒上了干薰衣草。看起来，这些东西让他非常满意。最后，他打开右边抽屉，发现了一把钥匙和一个小铁盒，摇一摇铁盒，里面传出钱币碰撞的响声，听得他眉开眼笑。班布尔先生又恢复了他原来的姿势，说道：“我要这么办！”说完后，他又摇了十分钟头，好像在训斥自己是个坏蛋。之后，他对着镜子左看看右看看自己的腿，好像很欣赏自己。
foreignization: 巴恩布尔先生在门口偷听了一会儿，确认没有人靠近房间后，从下往上开始检查这三只长抽屉的内容：抽屉里装着各种时髦的衣服，这些衣服保养得很好，被放在两层旧报纸之间，还撒了些干薰衣草；这一切似乎让他非常满意。经过一番搜索，他终于来到了右边抽屉（里面有钥匙），发现一个小的带锁的盒子，摇晃盒子时，里面传出了像硬币碰撞的愉快声音。于是，巴恩布尔先生昂首阔步地走回壁炉旁，恢复了原来的姿势，带着严肃而坚定的表情说：“我要这么做！”随后，他摇了十分钟的头，好像在责备自己是一个令人愉快的小狗；然后，他带着极大的喜悦和兴趣地从侧面打量自己的腿。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Having listened at the keyhole, to assure himself that nobody was approaching the chamber, Mr. Bumble, beginning at the bottom, proceeded to make himself acquainted with the contents of the three long drawers: which, being filled with various garments of good fashion and texture, carefully preserved between two layers of old newspapers, speckled with dried lavender: seemed to yield him exceeding satisfaction. Arriving, in course of time, at the right-hand corner drawer (in which was the key), and beholding therein a small padlocked box, which, being shaken, gave forth a pleasant sound, as of the chinking of coin, Mr. Bumble returned with a stately walk to the fireplace; and, resuming his old attitude, said, with a grave and determined air, “I’ll do it!” He followed up this remarkable declaration, by shaking his head in a waggish manner for ten minutes, as though he were remonstrating with himself for being such a pleasant dog; and then, he took a view of his legs in profile, with much seeming pleasure and interest.

source: “Cheap!” cried a shrill voice in Mr. Bumble’s ear: “you would have been dear at any price; and dear enough I paid for you, Lord above knows that!”

source: “I hope I may have good cause to do so,” replied Mr. Losberne; “though I confess I don’t think I shall. But yesterday morning you had made up your mind, in a great hurry, to stay here, and to accompany your mother, like a dutiful son, to the sea-side. Before noon, you announce that you are going to do me the honour of accompanying me as far as I go, on your road to London. And at night, you urge me, with great mystery, to start before the ladies are stirring; the consequence of which is, that young Oliver here is pinned down to his breakfast when he ought to be ranging the meadows after botanical phenomena of all kinds. Too bad, isn’t it, Oliver?”
domestication: “希望我有正当的理由这么做，”洛斯伯恩先生回答说，“虽然我承认我不这么认为。就在昨天早上，你匆忙决定留在这里，像个孝顺的儿子一样陪伴你母亲去海边。上午还没过，你就宣布你将荣幸地陪我走一段路，一起前往伦敦。到了晚上，你又神秘地催促我在女士们醒来之前出发，结果可怜的奥利弗被困在了早餐桌上，而他本应该在草原上探索各种植物奇观。太遗憾了，对吧，小奥利弗？”
foreignization: “我希望我有充分的理由这么做，”洛斯伯恩先生回答说，“虽然我承认我并不这么认为。但是就在昨天早上，你匆忙地决定留下来，并像一个孝顺的儿子一样陪伴你的母亲去海边。到了中午，你宣布你将荣幸地与我同行，直到你在去伦敦的路上。晚上，你以极大的神秘性催促我，在女士们醒来之前出发，其结果就是，小奥利弗被困在了他的早餐时，而他本应该是在荒野中探索各类植物现象。不是很糟糕吗，奥利弗？”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“I hope I may have good cause to do so,” replied Mr. Losberne; “though I confess I don’t think I shall. But yesterday morning you had made up your mind, in a great hurry, to stay here, and to accompany your mother, like a dutiful son, to the sea-side. Before noon, you announce that you are going to do me the honour of accompanying me as far as I go, on your road to London. And at night, you urge me, with great mystery, to start before the ladies are stirring; the consequence of which is, that young Oliver here is pinned down to his breakfast when he ought to be ranging the meadows after botanical phenomena of all kinds. Too bad, isn’t it, Oliver?”
“希望我有正当的理由这么做，”洛斯伯恩先生回答说，“虽然我承认我不这么认为。就在昨天早上，你匆忙决定留在这里，像个孝顺的儿子一样陪伴你母亲去海边。上午还没过，你就宣布你将荣幸地陪我走一段路，一起前往伦敦。到了晚上，你又神秘地催促我在女士们醒来之前出发，结果可怜的奥利弗被困在了早餐桌上，而他本应该在草原上探索各种植物奇观。太遗憾了，对吧，小奥利弗？”

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“I hope I may have good cause to do so,” replied Mr. Losberne; “though I confess I don’t think I shall. But yesterday morning you had made up your mind, in a great hurry, to stay here, and to accompany your mother, like a dutiful son, to the sea-side. Before noon, you announce that you are going to do me the honour of accompanying me as far as I go, on your road to London. And at night, you urge me, with great mystery, to start before the ladies are stirring; the consequence of which is, that young Oliver here is pinned down to his breakfast when he ought to be ranging the meadows after botanical phenomena of all kinds. Too bad, isn’t it, Oliver?”
“我希望我有充分的理由这么做，”洛斯伯恩先生回答说，“虽然我承认我并不这么认为。但是就在昨天早上，你匆忙地决定留下来，并像一个孝顺的儿子一样陪伴你的母亲去海边。到了中午，你宣布你将荣幸地与我同行，直到你在去伦敦的路上。晚上，你以极大的神秘性催促我，在女士们醒来之前出发，其结果就是，小奥利弗被困在了他的早餐时，而他本应该是在荒野中探索各类植物现象。不是很糟糕吗，奥利弗？”

source: With these last words, the Dodger suffered himself to be led off by the collar; threatening, till he got into the yard, to make a parliamentary business of it; and then grinning in the officer’s face, with great glee and self-approval.
domestication: 就在说完这些话后，流氓让别人抓着他的衣领把他带走了，他一直在威胁说要把这件事闹到国会去。直到他被拖进院子里，他还对警察咧着嘴笑，显得非常得意，非常佩服自己。
foreignization: 在说完这些话后，Dodger 允许别人抓着他的衣领把他带走；一路上，他一直在威胁说，要把这件事提交到议会；然后，当他们到了院子里时，他就对警官咧嘴笑，脸上充满了极大的喜悦和自我认可。

With these last words, the Dodger suffered himself to be led off by the collar; threatening, till he got into the yard, to make a parliamentary business of it; and then grinning in the officer’s face, with great glee and self-approval.
就在说完这些话后，流氓让别人抓着他的衣领把他带走了，他一直在威胁说要把这件事闹到国会去。直到他被拖进院子里，他还对警察咧着嘴笑，显得非常得意，非常佩服自己。

messages_foreignization.content:

With these last words, the Dodger suffered himself to be led off by the collar; threatening, till he got into the yard, to make a parliamentary business of it; and then grinning in the officer’s face, with great glee and self-approval.
在说完这些话后，Dodger 允许别人抓着他的衣领把他带走；一路上，他一直在威胁说，要把这件事提交到议会；然后，当他们到了院子里时，他就对警官咧嘴笑，脸上充满了极大的喜悦和自我认可。

source: He went on doggedly; but as he left the town behind him, and plunged into the solitude and darkness of the road, he felt a dread and awe creeping upon him which shook him to the core. Every object before him, substance or shadow, still or moving, took the semblance of some fearful thing; but these fears were nothing compared to the sense that haunted him of that morning’s ghastly figure following at his heels. He could trace its shadow in the gloom, supply the smallest item of the outline, and note how stiff and solemn it seemed to stalk along. He could hear its garments rustling in the leaves, and every breath of wind came laden with that last low cry. If he stopped it did the same. If he ran, it followed—not running too: that would have been a relief: but like a corpse endowed with the mere machinery of life, and borne on one slow melancholy wind that never rose or fell.
domestication: 他继续走着，但当他离开城镇，步入黑暗的道路时，一种恐惧和敬畏感开始悄悄袭来，让他心惊胆战。前方的每个物体，无论是实体还是影子，静止不动还是移动，都让他觉得像某种可怕的东西。但这些恐惧与他内心挥之不去的感觉相比，简直不值一提——那就是早晨那副骇人的模样跟在他身后。他可以在黑暗中看到它的影子，填补轮廓的每一个细节，并注意到它似乎以一种僵硬而肃穆的方式行走。他可以听到它的衣服沙沙作响于树叶中，每一阵风都带来了最后那低沉的哭声。如果他停下来，它也会停下。如果他跑起来，它也会跟上——但不是奔跑，如果那样就好了，而是像一个只具备最基本生命机能的尸体，被一股永不起伏的悲伤风吹着前进。
foreignization: 他坚定地继续前行；但当他离开城市，陷入道路的孤独和黑暗之中时，他感到一股恐惧和敬畏感悄悄袭来，这让他浑身发抖。 他面前的每个物体，无论是实体还是影子，静止的还是移动的，都变成了某种可怕的事物；但这些恐惧与他感受到的被那天早上的骇人形象跟随在他身后这一感觉相比简直不值一提。 他可以在黑暗中追踪它的影子，补充最小的轮廓细节，并注意到它似乎如何僵硬地、庄严地走着。 他可以听到它的衣服在树叶中沙沙作响，每一阵风都带来了最后那低沉的哭声。 如果他停下来，它也会停止。如果他跑起来，它就会跟随——而且不是跟着跑：那样的话倒是件解脱的事：但就像一个被赋予了仅仅维持生命的机械装置的尸体，被一股永远不会升起或降落的缓慢忧郁的风托着。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

He went on doggedly; but as he left the town behind him, and plunged into the solitude and darkness of the road, he felt a dread and awe creeping upon him which shook him to the core. Every object before him, substance or shadow, still or moving, took the semblance of some fearful thing; but these fears were nothing compared to the sense that haunted him of that morning’s ghastly figure following at his heels. He could trace its shadow in the gloom, supply the smallest item of the outline, and note how stiff and solemn it seemed to stalk along. He could hear its garments rustling in the leaves, and every breath of wind came laden with that last low cry. If he stopped it did the same. If he ran, it followed—not running too: that would have been a relief: but like a corpse endowed with the mere machinery of life, and borne on one slow melancholy wind that never rose or fell.

source: The room remained deserted for a quarter of an hour or more; the girl glided back with the same unearthly tread; and, immediately afterwards, the two men were heard descending. Monks went at once into the street; and the Jew crawled upstairs again for the money. When he returned, the girl was adjusting her shawl and bonnet, as if preparing to be gone.
domestication: 房间里静悄悄的，足足有二十分钟没人动静。那个女孩又回来了，她走路的样子还是那么鬼鬼祟祟，跟刚才一样。一会儿工夫，那两个男人就下来了。蒙克斯立刻就出门去了，而那个犹太人却又爬上楼去取钱。等他回来时，那女孩已经开始整理她的头巾和帽子，似乎是准备离开了。
foreignization: 房间至少在一刻钟之内仍然没有人；那个女孩用同样的非自然的脚步回来了；而紧接着，两个人被听到正在下楼。蒙克斯马上走到街上去了；犹太人又爬上楼去拿钱。当他回来时，那个女孩正在整理她的披肩和帽子，好像准备离开。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The room remained deserted for a quarter of an hour or more; the girl glided back with the same unearthly tread; and, immediately afterwards, the two men were heard descending. Monks went at once into the street; and the Jew crawled upstairs again for the money. When he returned, the girl was adjusting her shawl and bonnet, as if preparing to be gone.

Monks went at once into the street; and the Jew crawled upstairs again for the money. When he returned, the girl was adjusting her shawl and bonnet, as if preparing to be gone.
房间至少在一刻钟之内仍然没有人；那个女孩用同样的非自然的脚步回来了；而紧接着，两个人被听到正在下楼。蒙克斯马上走到街上去了；犹太人又爬上楼去拿钱。当他回来时，那个女孩正在整理她的披肩和帽子，好像准备离开。

source: “God Almighty help me, I am!” cried the girl passionately; “and I wish I had been struck dead in the street, or had changed places with them we passed so near tonight, before I had lent a hand in bringing him here. He’s a thief, a liar, a devil, all that’s bad, from this night forth. Isn’t that enough for the old wretch, without blows?”
domestication: 天哪！姑娘情绪激动地喊道：“我真的是！我希望我在街上被雷劈死，或是今晚和我们路过的那些人交换一下位置，这样我就不会参与把他带到这里来了。他从今以后就是个十恶不赦的小偷、骗子和魔鬼，不打他一顿，老家伙还不满足吗？”
foreignization: "全能的上帝帮助我，我就是！”那个女孩充满激情地哭喊道；“我希望我当时在街上被击中而死，或者今晚与我们路过的那些人交换位置，而不是在把他带到这里之前伸出援手。他是一个小偷，一个骗子，从今晚开始，一个魔鬼，什么都不好。这对于老家伙来说还不够吗，还需要打人吗？”

He’s a thief, a liar, a devil, all that’s bad, from this night forth. Isn’t that enough for the old wretch, without blows?”

source: “Yes, my dear, yes,” rejoined the Jew; his eyes glistening, and every muscle in his face working, with the excitement that the inquiry had awakened.
domestication: “是的，是的，我的亲爱的”，那个犹太人回答道，他的眼睛闪着光，每一条面纹都在动，显现出问题引发的激动。这个犹太人的表情就像被点燃了一样，全身都散发着兴奋的气息。他的眼睛里闪着光芒，好像揭开了一个秘密，或者说，终于等到了他想要的东西。
foreignization: “是的，我的亲爱的，是的，”那个犹太人回答说；他的眼睛闪着光，每一个面部的肌肉因询问而引起的激动都在活动着。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Yes, my dear, yes,” rejoined the Jew; his eyes glistening, and every muscle in his face working, with the excitement that the inquiry had awakened.
“是的，是的，我的亲爱的”，那个犹太人回答道，他的眼睛闪着光，每一条面纹都在动，显现出问题引发的激动。这个犹太人的表情就像被点燃了一样，全身都散发着兴奋的气息。他的眼睛里闪着光芒，好像揭开了一个秘密，或者说，终于等到了他想要的东西。

“Yes, my dear, yes,” rejoined the Jew; his eyes glistening, and every muscle in his face working, with the excitement that the inquiry had awakened.
“是的，我的亲爱的，是的，”那个犹太人回答说；他的眼睛闪着光，每一个面部的肌肉因询问而引起的激动都在活动着。

source: The Jew seemed much vexed by Oliver’s not expressing any greater curiosity on the subject; but the truth is, that, although Oliver felt very anxious, he was too much confused by the earnest cunning of Fagin’s looks, and his own speculations, to make any further inquiries just then. He had no other opportunity: for the Jew remained very surly and silent till night: when he prepared to go abroad.
domestication: 那个犹太人似乎对奥利弗没有表现出更强的好奇心感到非常不满。但是，实际上，尽管奥利弗感到非常焦急，他被费金严肃狡猾的眼神和自己的猜测弄得糊里糊涂的，以至于当时无法再提出更多的问题。他也没有其他机会，因为那犹太人一直保持着阴郁的沉默，直到夜晚才准备出门。可以说，那犹太人的表情让奥利弗摸不着头脑，一时间他也没法子再问什么。
foreignization: 那个犹太人似乎对奥利弗没有表现出更大的好奇心感到非常恼怒；但是事实是，尽管奥利弗感到很焦急，但是他被费金严肃的狡猾眼神和自己的猜测弄得太混乱了，以至于当时无法进一步询问。他没有其他机会，因为犹太人一直保持着阴郁和沉默，直到夜晚，当他准备出去的时候。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The Jew seemed much vexed by Oliver’s not expressing any greater curiosity on the subject; but the truth is, that, although Oliver felt very anxious, he was too much confused by the earnest cunning of Fagin’s looks, and his own speculations, to make any further inquiries just then. He had no other opportunity: for the Jew remained very surly and silent till night: when he prepared to go abroad.

source: This was said in jest; but if the speaker could have seen the evil leer with which the Jew bit his pale lip as he turned round to the cupboard, he might have thought the caution not wholly unnecessary, or the wish (at all events) to improve upon the distiller’s ingenuity not very far from the old gentleman’s merry heart.
domestication: 这话是开玩笑说的，但要是说这话的人看到那个犹太人转身面对柜子的时候脸上闪过的邪恶的笑容，他就会觉得这个提醒并非完全多余。或者他会觉得那个老头的心里并不是没有想要改进酒精制造商智慧的想法，毕竟酒后吐真言嘛。
foreignization: 这是开玩笑说的话；但如果说话者能看到那时犹太人转过身去打开柜子的动作时，脸上露出邪恶的冷笑，他咬着自己苍白的嘴唇，可能会认为这种提醒并非完全没有必要，或者（无论如何）渴望改进蒸馏器的智慧离这位老先生快乐的心并不太远。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

This was said in jest; but if the speaker could have seen the evil leer with which the Jew bit his pale lip as he turned round to the cupboard, he might have thought the caution not wholly unnecessary, or the wish (at all events) to improve upon the distiller’s ingenuity not very far from the old gentleman’s merry heart.
这话是开玩笑说的，但要是说这话的人看到那个犹太人转身面对柜子的时候脸上闪过的邪恶的笑容，他就会觉得这个提醒并非完全多余。或者他会觉得那个老头的心里并不是没有想要改进酒精制造商智慧的想法，毕竟酒后吐真言嘛。

messages_foreignization.content:

This was said in jest; but if the speaker could have seen the evil leer with which the Jew bit his pale lip as he turned round to the cupboard, he might have thought the caution not wholly unnecessary, or the wish (at all events) to improve upon the distiller’s ingenuity not very far from the old gentleman’s merry heart.
这是开玩笑说的话；但如果说话者能看到那时犹太人转过身去打开柜子的动作时，脸上露出邪恶的冷笑，他咬着自己苍白的嘴唇，可能会认为这种提醒并非完全没有必要，或者（无论如何）渴望改进蒸馏器的智慧离这位老先生快乐的心并不太远。

source: They were happy days, those of Oliver’s recovery. Everything was so quiet, and neat, and orderly; everybody so kind and gentle; that after the noise and turbulence in the midst of which he had always lived, it seemed like Heaven itself. He was no sooner strong enough to put his clothes on, properly, than Mr. Brownlow caused a complete new suit, and a new cap, and a new pair of shoes, to be provided for him. As Oliver was told that he might do what he liked with the old clothes, he gave them to a servant who had been very kind to him, and asked her to sell them to a Jew, and keep the money for herself. This she very readily did; and, as Oliver looked out of the parlour window, and saw the Jew roll them up in his bag and walk away, he felt quite delighted to think that they were safely gone, and that there was now no possible danger of his ever being able to wear them again. They were sad rags, to tell the truth; and Oliver had never had a new suit before.
domestication: 那真是一段快乐的日子，奥利弗恢复得很快。一切都那么安静，整洁，有条不紊；每个人都那么亲切，温柔。在经历了长期的混乱和骚动之后，这种生活让他感到仿佛身处天堂。等他稍微恢复了一些体力，能够穿衣的时候，布朗洛先生马上为他置办了一身全新的衣服，一顶新帽子和一双新鞋。由于奥利弗被告知可以随意处理旧衣服，他便将它们送给一直对他很友好的一个女佣，请她把这些旧衣卖给犹太人，然后把钱留给自己。那个女佣很乐意帮忙，当奥利弗从客厅的窗户望出去，看见犹太人把那些旧衣服卷起来放进包里然后走开时，他感觉非常高兴，觉得那些旧东西终于没了，自己再也不会穿那些破烂的衣服了。说实话，那些衣服确实很破旧，奥利弗以前从来没有穿过崭新的衣服。
foreignization: 那是奥利弗康复的幸福日子。 一切都这么安静，整洁，有序； 每个人都这么善良，温和； 在他一直居住的喧嚣和动荡之中，这就像天堂一样。 当他足够强壮，可以正确地穿上他的衣服时，布朗洛先生安排了一套全新的西装，一顶新帽子和一双新鞋，为他提供。 当奥利弗被告知他可以随意处置旧衣服时，他把它们送给一个一直很亲切的仆人，并要求她把它们卖给一个犹太人，并为自己保留这笔钱。 她非常乐意地这样做了； 当奥利弗从客厅窗口望出去，看见犹太人把它们卷起来放进袋子里并走开时，他感到非常高兴，认为它们安全地离开了，现在没有任何可能再穿它们的危险。 说实话，那些破烂的衣服很可悲； 奥利弗以前从未有过新衣服。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

They were happy days, those of Oliver’s recovery. Everything was so quiet, and neat, and orderly; everybody so kind and gentle; that after the noise and turbulence in the midst of which he had always lived, it seemed like Heaven itself. He was no sooner strong enough to put his clothes on, properly, than Mr. Brownlow caused a complete new suit, and a new cap, and a new pair of shoes, to be provided for him. As Oliver was told that he might do what he liked with the old clothes, he gave them to a servant who had been very kind to him, and asked her to sell them to a Jew, and keep the money for herself. This she very readily did; and, as Oliver looked out of the parlour window, and saw the Jew roll them up in his bag and walk away, he felt quite delighted to think that they were safely gone, and that there was now no possible danger of his ever being able to wear them again. They were sad rags, to tell the truth; and Oliver had never had a new suit before.

source: The cloth was laid for supper; the table was covered with bread and butter, plates and glasses; a porter-pot and a wine-bottle. At the upper end of the table, Mr. Noah Claypole lolled negligently in an easy-chair, with his legs thrown over one of the arms: an open clasp-knife in one hand, and a mass of buttered bread in the other. Close beside him stood Charlotte, opening oysters from a barrel: which Mr. Claypole condescended to swallow, with remarkable avidity. A more than ordinary redness in the region of the young gentleman’s nose, and a kind of fixed wink in his right eye, denoted that he was in a slight degree intoxicated; these symptoms were confirmed by the intense relish with which he took his oysters, for which nothing but a strong appreciation of their cooling properties, in cases of internal fever, could have sufficiently accounted.
domestication: 晚饭的桌子已经摆好了，面包、黄油、盘子和玻璃杯什么都有，还有一大锅啤酒和一瓶葡萄酒。桌子的上首，诺亚·克莱波尔懒散地躺在一张软椅上，腿搭在扶手上，一只手里拿着打开的折刀，另一只手里捏着一个抹了黄油的面包。他旁边站着夏洛特，从一桶里剥生蚝，而克莱波尔则以很大的热情吞下这些生蚝。年轻人的鼻子比平常红得多，右眼也眨得不停，说明他有点喝醉了。他吃生蚝时的津津有味，也证实了这一点，因为只有在体内发烧的情况下，才能这么享受生蚝的凉爽口感。
foreignization: 晚餐用的桌布已经铺好，桌上摆着面包和黄油、盘子和玻璃杯，还有一只水壶和一瓶葡萄酒。在桌子的上首，诺亚·克莱波尔先生懒散地躺在一张扶手椅里，双腿搭在扶手上，一只手握着打开的折刀，另一只手拿着涂满了黄油的面包。他旁边站着夏洛特，她正在从一个桶里剥开牡蛎，克莱波尔先生居然屈尊俯就地把牡蛎吞了下去，并且带着异常的热情。在这位年轻绅士的鼻子周围有一种不寻常的红晕，他的右眼里似乎有一种固定不变的眨眼，这些迹象表明他有些微醺；这些症状通过他吃牡蛎时表现出的强烈的享受得到证实，因为除了对牡蛎的冷却作用有很强的赞赏之外，其他什么也不能充分地解释他为什么这么享受牡蛎。

Translate the following text to Chinese.

Close beside him stood Charlotte, opening oysters from a barrel: which Mr. Claypole condescended to swallow, with remarkable avidity. A more than ordinary redness in the region of the young gentleman’s nose, and a kind of fixed wink in his right eye, denoted that he was in a slight degree intoxicated; these symptoms were confirmed by the intense relish with which he took his oysters, for which nothing but a strong appreciation of their cooling properties, in cases of internal fever, could have sufficiently accounted.

The cloth was laid for supper; the table was covered with bread and butter, plates and glasses; a porter-pot and a wine-bottle. At the upper end of the table, Mr. Noah Claypole lolled negligently in an easy-chair, with his legs thrown over one of the arms: an open clasp-knife in one hand, and a mass of buttered bread in the other. Close beside him stood Charlotte, opening oysters from a barrel: which Mr. Claypole condescended to swallow, with remarkable avidity. A more than ordinary redness in the region of the young gentleman’s nose, and a kind of fixed wink in his right eye, denoted that he was in a slight degree intoxicated; these symptoms were confirmed by the intense relish with which he took his oysters, for which nothing but a strong appreciation of their cooling properties, in cases of internal fever, could have sufficiently accounted.

晚餐用的桌布已经铺好，桌上摆着面包和黄油、盘子和玻璃杯，还有一只水壶和一瓶葡萄酒。在桌子的上首，诺亚·克莱波尔先生懒散地躺在一张扶手椅里，双腿搭在扶手上，一只手握着打开的折刀，另一只手拿着涂满了黄油的面包。他旁边站着夏洛特，她正在从一个桶里剥开牡蛎，克莱波尔先生居然屈尊俯就地把牡蛎吞了下去，并且带着异常的热情。在这位年轻绅士的鼻子周围有一种不寻常的红晕，他的右眼里似乎有一种固定不变的眨眼，这些迹象表明他有些微醺；这些症状通过他吃牡蛎时表现出的强烈的享受得到证实，因为除了对牡蛎的冷却作用有很强的赞赏之外，其他什么也不能充分地解释他为什么这么享受牡蛎。

source: “There is something in that boy’s face,” said the old gentleman to himself as he walked slowly away, tapping his chin with the cover of the book, in a thoughtful manner; “something that touches and interests me. Can he be innocent? He looked like— Bye the bye,” exclaimed the old gentleman, halting very abruptly, and staring up into the sky, “Bless my soul!—where have I seen something like that look before?”
domestication: 老先生一边慢慢地走开，一边自言自语道：“这小子脸上有种东西让我觉得很熟悉，也很感兴趣。”他一边说，一边用书皮轻敲着下巴，显得很沉思。“会不会他真的清白无辜呢？” 他想，“那小子长得真……唉呀！”老先生突然停住脚步，抬头望向天空，惊呼道：“我的天啊！我到底在哪里见过这么个表情呢？”
foreignization: “那个男孩的脸上有一些东西，”老绅士边走边对自己说，边慢慢地走开，用书皮敲着下巴，神情沉思；“一些东西打动了我并引起了我的兴趣，他会是无辜的吗？他看起来像——顺便说一下”，老绅士惊叫道，突然停下脚步，抬头盯着天空，“天哪！——我以前在哪里见过类似的表情？”

source: This cell was in shape and size something like an area cellar, only not so light. It was most intolerably dirty; for it was Monday morning; and it had been tenanted by six drunken people, who had been locked up, elsewhere, since Saturday night. But this is little. In our station-houses, men and women are every night confined on the most trivial charges—the word is worth noting—in dungeons, compared with which, those in Newgate, occupied by the most atrocious felons, tried, found guilty, and under sentence of death, are palaces. Let any one who doubts this, compare the two.
domestication: 这间牢房的形状和大小跟地窖差不多，就是光线不好些。这里脏得实在无法忍受，因为今天是星期一的早晨；从星期六夜里起，就有六个醉汉被关在这里。其实这也还算不了什么。在我们的警察局里，每晚都会因为一些微不足道的小事就把男人和女人关进牢房，这种牢房跟新门监狱里的牢房比起来，就像皇宫一样。新门监狱里的那些牢房，是为那些最可恶的重罪犯设计的，他们经过审判，被判有罪，并且等待行刑。而警察局里的那些牢房，却随便把人关进去。要是有人不相信这话，就让他去比较一下这两种牢房吧。
foreignization: 这种牢房的形状和大小就像是一个地窖，只不过没有那么亮。那里非常肮脏；因为现在是星期一早晨， 而这里自从星期六晚上就被六个醉酒的人占据了。但这还算是小事。在我们的警务室里，每晚都会有男人和女人因最微不足道的罪名——这个词值得注意——而被关在与新门监狱相比像宫殿一样的牢房里， 新门监狱里关押着最凶恶的重犯，他们已经受审，罪名成立，并且被判处死刑。让任何怀疑的人去比较这两者。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

This cell was in shape and size something like an area cellar, only not so light. It was most intolerably dirty; for it was Monday morning; and it had been tenanted by six drunken people, who had been locked up, elsewhere, since Saturday night. But this is little. In our station-houses, men and women are every night confined on the most trivial charges—the word is worth noting—in dungeons, compared with which, those in Newgate, occupied by the most atrocious felons, tried, found guilty, and under sentence of death, are palaces. Let any one who doubts this, compare the two.

source: “Delighted to see you looking so well, my dear,” said the Jew, bowing with mock humility. “The Artful shall give you another suit, my dear, for fear you should spoil that Sunday one. Why didn’t you write, my dear, and say you were coming? We’d have got something warm for supper.”
domestication: 这位犹太人装出一副虚假的谦恭模样，对着我的同伴鞠了一躬，满脸堆着笑容。“啊，你看起来气色真不错，我的好朋友！我会再给你弄一套新衣服，以免你把星期日那身打扮弄脏了。你怎么不早写信通知我们一声，你要来我们这里？我们早就准备好了热腾腾的晚饭等你呢。”
foreignization: “很高兴看到你这么有精神，我的亲爱的，”犹太人说着，带着假装的谦恭鞠躬。“狡猾鬼会给你再做一套衣服，我的亲爱的，免得你弄坏那身星期日的衣服。你为什么不写信，我的亲爱的，说你要来呢？我们本可以准备一些温热的晚餐。”

Sikes, snatching it from him, hastily opened it; and proceeded to count the sovereigns it contained.
domestication: “好吧，好吧， 빌，给我时间，给我时间，”那个犹太人柔声回应道。“这里有！一切都安全！”说着，他从胸口掏出一块旧的棉手帕，从角落解开一个结，拿出一个小的棕色纸包。 赛克斯从他手中夺过它，急忙打开，开始数里面的金币。

Sikes, snatching it from him, hastily opened it; and proceeded to count the sovereigns it contained.

“Yes, yes, Bill; give me time, give me time,” replied the Jew, soothingly. “Here it is! source: Good Heaven! what was that, which sent the blood tingling to his heart, and deprived him of his voice, and of power to move! There—there—at the window—close before him—so close, that he could have almost touched him before he started back: with his eyes peering into the room, and meeting his: there stood the Jew! And beside him, white with rage or fear, or both, were the scowling features of the man who had accosted him in the inn-yard.
domestication: 天哪！到底发生了什么事？他只觉得一股寒意从心底升起，浑身的血液都快要凝固了。他说不出话来，也动弹不得。就在那里，就在窗前，离他这么近，近到他几乎可以碰到对方，结果对方突然退了一步。那个人的两眼死死地盯着屋里，正好与他的目光对上了：站在那里的人竟然是那个犹太人！而在犹太人旁边，那个曾经在客栈院子里拦住他的人，此刻脸色煞白，满脸怒容，估计是既害怕又愤怒。
foreignization: 上帝啊！是什么东西让他的血液因恐惧而加速流动，剥夺了他的声音和移动的力量？就在那里——就在窗户那里——就在他面前——如此之近，以至于他几乎可以在对方后退之前触碰到他：用他那双眼睛窥视着房间，并与他的眼睛对视：站在那里的是那个犹太人！就在他旁边，脸色苍白因愤怒或害怕，或者两者兼有，是那个在客栈院子里拦住他的男人那一副满脸皱眉的面孔。

messages_domestication.content:

source: The tea was made, and handed in silence. Mr. Bumble, having spread a handkerchief over his knees to prevent the crumbs from sullying the splendour of his shorts, began to eat and drink; varying these amusements, occasionally, by fetching a deep sigh; which, however, had no injurious effect upon his appetite, but, on the contrary, rather seemed to facilitate his operations in the tea and toast department.
domestication: 茶泡好了，默默地端了上来。班布尔先生在膝盖上铺了一块手帕，免得面包屑沾到他漂亮的短裤上，然后开始吃喝，时不时深深地叹一口气。其实，这叹息不但没影响他的食欲，反而似乎还助长了他在品茶和吃吐司方面的劲头儿。他吃喝之间，时不时来一声深沉的叹息，倒也让人觉得这是一件很舒服的事。
foreignization: 茶已经泡好了，默默地端了上来。班布尔先生在膝盖上铺了一块手帕，以免面包屑玷污他短裤的光彩，然后开始吃喝；偶尔，他会发出一声深深的叹息；然而，这并没有对他的食欲产生不良影响，相反，似乎反而促进了他吃茶和吐司的动作。

source: “It!” replied the woman, laying her hand over the other’s mouth. “The only thing she had. She wanted clothes to keep her warm, and food to eat; but she had kept it safe, and had it in her bosom. It was gold, I tell you! Rich gold, that might have saved her life!”
domestication: "就是它！”女人把手捂在另一个人的嘴上，“她唯一拥有的东西。她需要衣物来保暖，需要食物来吃，但她一直保管着它，把它放在她的怀里。这是金子，我告诉你！能救命的贵重黄金！”
foreignization: “它！”女人回答道，将她的手放在另一个人的嘴上。“她唯一拥有的东西。她想要衣服来保暖，想要食物来吃；但是她一直将它安全地保管着，并且它就放在她的胸口。它是黄金，我告诉你！能救她一命的珍贵黄金！”

source: The two women-servants ran upstairs to carry the intelligence that Mr. Giles had captured a robber; and the tinker busied himself in endeavouring to restore Oliver, lest he should die before he could be hanged. In the midst of all this noise and commotion, there was heard a sweet female voice, which quelled it in an instant.
domestication: 两名女佣人匆忙跑上楼去报信，说盖尔斯先生抓到了一个小偷；同时，铁匠也忙着给奥利弗恢复体力，以免他在被绞死之前就不行了。在这混乱的场面中，突然传来一阵柔美的女声，顿时安静了一切。
foreignization: 两个女佣人跑上楼去报告说，盖尔斯先生抓到一个小偷了；锅匠则忙着想办法让奥利弗苏醒过来，免得他在被绞死之前死去。在这片混乱和喧闹中，传来一阵柔美的女声，立刻使所有声音都安静下来。

source: “The magistrates are not called upon to pronounce any opinion on the matter,” said the second old gentleman sharply. “Take the boy back to the workhouse, and treat him kindly. He seems to want it.”

source: “Where can he have come from!” exclaimed Toby. “He’s been to the other kens of course, and finding them filled with strangers come on here, where he’s been many a time and often. But where can he have come from first, and how comes he here alone without the other!”
domestication: 托比惊呼道：“他到底从哪里冒出来的啊！当然，他肯定去过其他地方，但发现那些地方满是陌生人，就来了这里，因为他以前来过很多次。但问题是，他最初到底从哪里来的呢？为什么单独一个来到这里，没有其他人陪伴？”
foreignization: “他到底从哪里来的！”托比惊呼道。“他当然去过其他的避难所，并且发现那里充满了陌生人，所以才会来到这里，他曾经多次来过这里。但是他最初到底从哪里来的呢？为什么没有其他人陪伴他一个人来到这里？”

source: Noah Claypole ran along the streets at his swiftest pace, and paused not once for breath, until he reached the workhouse-gate. Having rested here, for a minute or so, to collect a good burst of sobs and an imposing show of tears and terror, he knocked loudly at the wicket; and presented such a rueful face to the aged pauper who opened it, that even he, who saw nothing but rueful faces about him at the best of times, started back in astonishment.
domestication: 诺亚·克莱波尔沿着街道飞奔，最快的速度也没停下来喘口气，直到他跑到济贫院的大门前。歇了一会儿，他才收拾好一场号啕大哭和吓得瑟瑟发抖的样子，然后大声地敲门，当他出现在开门的老贫民面前时，那张哭相是如此可怜可悲，即使在平时只见到穷困潦倒的人的老头，也被吓了一跳。
foreignization: 诺亚·克莱波尔以最快的速度沿着街道奔跑，一口气没停一下，直到他到达了济贫院的大门。在这里休息了一两分钟，收集了大量的抽泣和一副令人敬畏的眼泪和恐惧后，他大声地敲门；并向开门的老穷人露出了一张如此悲伤的面容，以至于即使是他，这个在最好的日子里也只看到悲伤的脸的人，也会惊讶地退缩回来。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Noah Claypole ran along the streets at his swiftest pace, and paused not once for breath, until he reached the workhouse-gate. Having rested here, for a minute or so, to collect a good burst of sobs and an imposing show of tears and terror, he knocked loudly at the wicket; and presented such a rueful face to the aged pauper who opened it, that even he, who saw nothing but rueful faces about him at the best of times, started back in astonishment.
诺亚·克莱波尔沿着街道飞奔，最快的速度也没停下来喘口气，直到他跑到济贫院的大门前。歇了一会儿，他才收拾好一场号啕大哭和吓得瑟瑟发抖的样子，然后大声地敲门，当他出现在开门的老贫民面前时，那张哭相是如此可怜可悲，即使在平时只见到穷困潦倒的人的老头，也被吓了一跳。

source: “Here he is!” bawled Giles, calling in a state of great excitement, up the staircase; “here’s one of the thieves, ma’am! Here’s a thief, miss! Wounded, miss! I shot him, miss; and Brittles held the light.”
domestication: “他在这里！”吉尔斯一边大声喊叫，一边兴奋地在楼梯上跑上去，“诸位太太，找到了其中一个小偷！小姐，这里有个贼啊，受伤了！是我开枪打的，还是布里特尔斯拿着灯给我们照亮。”
foreignization: "他在这里！”吉尔斯大声喊道，他非常激动，朝楼梯上喊道，“这里有一个小偷，夫人！这里有一个小偷，小姐！受伤了，小姐！我开枪打中了他，小姐；布里特尔斯拿着灯。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Here he is!” bawled Giles, calling in a state of great excitement, up the staircase; “here’s one of the thieves, ma’am! Here’s a thief, miss! Wounded, miss! I shot him, miss; and Brittles held the light.”
“他在这里！”吉尔斯一边大声喊叫，一边兴奋地在楼梯上跑上去，“诸位太太，找到了其中一个小偷！小姐，这里有个贼啊，受伤了！是我开枪打的，还是布里特尔斯拿着灯给我们照亮。”

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Here he is!” bawled Giles, calling in a state of great excitement, up the staircase; “here’s one of the thieves, ma’am! Here’s a thief, miss! Wounded, miss! I shot him, miss; and Brittles held the light.”
"他在这里！”吉尔斯大声喊道，他非常激动，朝楼梯上喊道，“这里有一个小偷，夫人！这里有一个小偷，小姐！受伤了，小姐！我开枪打中了他，小姐；布里特尔斯拿着灯。”

source: “That crack down in the back lane at Edmonton, Blathers,” said Mr. Duff, assisting his colleague’s memory.
domestication: “埃德蒙顿后巷的那件砸玻璃案，布莱瑟斯，你还记得吧？”达夫先生帮同事回忆道。
foreignization: “埃德蒙顿后巷里的那起事件，布拉瑟斯，”达夫先生说，同时帮助他的同事回忆。

source: The old housekeeper answered the summons with all dispatch; and dropping a curtsey at the door, waited for orders.
domestication: 老妈子接到呼唤后立即应答，走到门口行了个屈膝礼，等候吩咐。

source: The girl’s life had been squandered in the streets, and among the most noisome of the stews and dens of London, but there was something of the woman’s original nature left in her still; and when she heard a light step approaching the door opposite to that by which she had entered, and thought of the wide contrast which the small room would in another moment contain, she felt burdened with the sense of her own deep shame, and shrunk as though she could scarcely bear the presence of her with whom she had sought this interview.
domestication: 这女孩的青春岁月都浪费在了街头和伦敦最污秽的巷子里，但她身上仍然残留着一些当初的纯真。当她听到对面门口传来轻微的脚步声，想起自己马上就要与另一个世界的人相遇时，她感到深深的羞耻感压得自己喘不过气来。她仿佛不敢面对自己求见的那个人，畏缩得像一只被雨淋湿的小猫。
foreignization: 这个女孩的人生已经在街头和伦敦最肮脏的窑子和巢穴中浪费了，但她身上仍然保留了一些女性的原始天性；当她听到门口有轻微的脚步声靠近，靠近的是她进来时对面的那扇门，她想到了小房间很快会包含的巨大差异时，她感到深深的羞耻感压迫着自己，她畏缩了，好像她几乎无法承受与她寻求这次交谈的那个女人同处一室。

Translate the following text to Chinese.

这女孩的青春岁月都浪费在了街头和伦敦最污秽的巷子里，但她身上仍然残留着一些当初的纯真。当她听到对面门口传来轻微的脚步声，想起自己马上就要与另一个世界的人相遇时，她感到深深的羞耻感压得自己喘不过气来。她仿佛不敢面对自己求见的那个人，畏缩得像一只被雨淋湿的小猫。

source: The three spectators seemed quite stupefied. They offered no interference, and the boy and man rolled on the ground together; the former, heedless of the blows that showered upon him, wrenching his hands tighter and tighter in the garments about the murderer’s breast, and never ceasing to call for help with all his might.
domestication: 这三个旁观者看起来都惊呆了，他们没有进行任何干预，这时候那个男孩和那个人一起滚倒在地上。那个男孩无视头顶上如雨点般落下的拳脚，死死抓住凶手胸口的衣服，一边大声呼救。尽管他遍体鳞伤，但仍然紧紧抓住不放，一刻也不肯放松。
foreignization: 这三个旁观者似乎都十分惊呆了。他们没有进行任何干预，男孩和男人一起在地上打滚；前者，置身于落在他身上的拳脚之下，将自己的手紧紧抓住凶手胸前的衣服越握越紧，并且一直大声呼救。

source: “What? What?” interposed Mr. Bumble: with a gleam of pleasure in his metallic eyes. “Not run away; he hasn’t run away, has he, Noah?”
domestication: “什么？什么？”班布尔先生插嘴问道，眼睛里闪着兴奋的光。“不会吧，他没跑了吧，诺亚？”
foreignization: “什么？什么？”班布尔先生插话道，眼中闪着一丝愉快的光芒：“没跑掉吧，他不会跑掉吧，是吗，诺亚？”

source: “I’m glad to hear it,” said Sikes, looking grimly at Oliver; “for the sake of his young carcase: as would otherways have suffered for it. Come here, young ’un; and let me read you a lectur’, which is as well got over at once.”
domestication: “听说你没事，我真高兴，”赛克斯恶狠狠地瞪着奥利弗说，“毕竟为这个小身板着想，不然他得受苦。过来，小家伙，我给你上一课，趁早解决了也好。”
foreignization: “我很高兴听到这个消息，”赛克斯说着，严肃地看着奥利弗；“为了他年轻的身体：否则他会因此而受苦。过来，小家伙，让我给你讲一个课堂，这最好马上解决掉。”

source: “Never,” rejoined Monks; “nor against me either. See here! But don’t move a step forward, or your life is not worth a bulrush.”
domestication: “休想！”蒙克斯反驳道，“也不要对我来这套。看清楚！但别朝前动一步，否则你的命不如芦苇一般轻贱。”
foreignization: “永远不会，”蒙克斯回答说，“也不要对我这样做。看看吧！但是不要朝前移动一步，否则你的生命不如一根蒲草秆值钱。”

source: “I don’t quite know, ma’am,” said Oliver, without taking his eyes from the canvas; “I have seen so few that I hardly know. What a beautiful, mild face that lady’s is!”
domestication: “我不太清楚，夫人，”奥利弗说，眼睛仍然盯着画布。“我见过的太少了，根本没法儿比较。但那位女士的脸多么漂亮啊，简直是面带慈容！

source: “No; not even by flash Toby Crackit,” replied Sikes. “He says he’s worn sham whiskers, and a canary waistcoat, the whole blessed time he’s been loitering down there, and it’s all of no use.”
domestication: “不行，连托比·克拉基特都没成功，”塞克斯回答道。“他说他戴了假胡子，穿了一件金黄色的马甲，在那儿晃悠了这么久，可是一点用也没有。”

source: “I repeat that I firmly believe it. To prove to you that I am disposed to trust you, I tell you without reserve, that we propose to extort the secret, whatever it may be, from the fear of this man Monks. But if—if—” said the gentleman, “he cannot be secured, or, if secured, cannot be acted upon as we wish, you must deliver up the Jew.”
domestication: 我再次强调，我坚信这一点。为了让你相信我会信任你，我毫无保留地告诉你，我们计划从这个叫蒙克斯的家伙那里以某种方式获取秘密。但是，如果我们抓不到他，或者就算抓到了也无法按我们的意愿对付他，那么你就必须交出犹太人。
foreignization: “我再次重申，我坚信这一点。为了证明我有意愿相信你，我毫无保留地告诉你，我们计划通过激发这个叫蒙克斯的人的恐惧来逼迫他吐露秘密，不管这个秘密是什么。但是，如果——如果——”先生说，“我们无法控制他，或者，即使控制了他，也不能按照我们的期望行事，那么你必须交出犹太人。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

But if—if—” said the gentleman, “he cannot be secured, or, if secured, cannot be acted upon as we wish, you must deliver up the Jew.”

source: “We must make up for lost time when we’ve got things into a good train. That’s all I know,” said Sikes.
domestication: “我们必须赶上失去的时间，当一切都步入正轨后就必须加把劲儿，这是我唯一知道的，”赛克斯说。

source: Mr. Losberne had been feeling the patient’s pulse during this short dialogue; but he now rose from the chair by the bedside, and remarked, that if the officers had any doubts upon the subject, they would perhaps like to step into the next room, and have Brittles before them.
domestication: 洛斯伯恩先生一直在感觉病人的脉搏，当他们聊天的时候，他现在从床边的椅子上站起来，说，如果执法人员对这件事还有疑虑，也许他们想去隔壁房间，把布里特尔斯叫过来当面对质一下。
foreignization: 洛斯伯恩先生在这段简短的对话期间一直在摸病人的脉搏；但他现在从床边的椅子上站起来，并且说，如果警官们对这个问题有所怀疑，他们可能愿意到隔壁房间，把布里特尔斯叫到他们面前来。

source: “What a delicious thing is a oyster!” remarked Mr. Claypole, after he had swallowed it. “What a pity it is, a number of ’em should ever make you feel uncomfortable; isn’t it, Charlotte?”

messages_foreignization.content:

“What a delicious thing is a oyster!” remarked Mr. Claypole, after he had swallowed it. “What a pity it is, a number of ’em should ever make you feel uncomfortable; isn’t it, Charlotte?”
“一只牡蛎是多么美味的事啊！”克莱波尔先生在吞下它之后说。“真可惜，很多这样的东西会让你感到不舒服，不是吗，夏洛特？”

source: This sounded so like a falsehood, that the old gentleman looked somewhat sternly in Oliver’s face. It was impossible to doubt him; there was truth in every one of its thin and sharpened lineaments.
domestication: 这话听起来简直像是在编瞎话，老爷脸色一下子就沉了下来，他严肃地盯着奥利弗的脸。然而，他又无法怀疑奥利弗，因为他那张瘦削、清秀的脸上，每一个细节都写着真诚。
foreignization: 这听起来如此像是一个谎言，以至于老绅士的脸上出现了一丝严肃的表情，他凝视着奥利弗的脸。无法怀疑他；他的每一个细长而锋利的面部特征都显现出真实的神情。

messages_domestication.content:

source: “Bill, Bill!” gasped the girl, wrestling with the strength of mortal fear,—“I—I won’t scream or cry—not once—hear me—speak to me—tell me what I have done!”
domestication: “比尔，比尔！”那个女孩喘着粗气，努力抑制着恐惧的冲击，“我、我不会尖叫也不会哭泣——一次都不会，你听我说，跟我说话，告诉我我做了什么！”她拼命地抓住比尔，眼泪在眼眶里打转，却强忍着没有让它流出来，满脸写着求救和恐惧的表情。
foreignization: “比尔，比尔！”女孩喘着气，挣扎着要摆脱死亡的恐惧——“我——我不会尖叫或哭泣——一次都没有——听我说——跟我说话——告诉我我做了什么！”

source: “I didn’t take it,” stammered Noah, no longer stretching out his legs like an independent gentleman, but coiling them up as well as he could under his chair; “it was all her doing; yer’ve got it now, Charlotte, yer know yer have.”
domestication: 诺亚结结巴巴地说：“我没拿，我根本没拿！”他不再像个潇洒的绅士一样翘腿，而是把腿尽可能地蜷缩在椅子底下，小声地嘟囔道：“都是她干的，你现在有证据了，是吧，夏洛特，你明知道的！”

source: “I should like,” faltered the child, “if somebody that can write, would put a few words down for me on a piece of paper, and fold it up and seal it, and keep it for me, after I am laid in the ground.”
domestication: 孩子结结巴巴地说：“我希望有人能帮我写几句话，写在纸上，折起来封好，然后在我离开人世之后替我保管。”

source: “Ay, I do,” returned the girl, meeting his gaze. “I shall be glad to have him away from my eyes, and to know that the worst is over. I can’t bear to have him about me. The sight of him turns me against myself, and all of you.”
domestication: “哎，我是这么想的”，女孩回答道，同时与他对视。“我巴不得他赶快离开我的视线，这样一来最糟糕的事也就结束了。有他在我身边让我实在受不了，他的出现让我讨厌自己，也讨厌你们所有人。”
foreignization: “是的，我会，”女孩回答道，迎着他的目光。“我很高兴他离开我的视线，知道最坏的情况已经过去。我受不了让他在我身边，他的出现让我反感自己和你们所有人。”

“Ay, I do,” returned the girl, meeting his gaze. “I shall be glad to have him away from my eyes, and to know that the worst is over. I can’t bear to have him about me. The sight of him turns me against myself, and all of you.”
“哎，我是这么想的”，女孩回答道，同时与他对视。“我巴不得他赶快离开我的视线，这样一来最糟糕的事也就结束了。有他在我身边让我实在受不了，他的出现让我讨厌自己，也讨厌你们所有人。”

source: “Humph!” rejoined the doctor. “There is nothing very alarming in his appearance. Have you any objection to see him in my presence?”
domestication: “哼！”医生应道。“他长得不像有甚么大毛病，跟我一起去瞧瞧他，你有甚么反对的吗？”
foreignization: “哼！”医生回答说，“他看起来没什么特别令人担心的。你有反对在我在场的情况下见他吗？”

source: “Nothing bad, I hope?” said the stranger, stopping short, and turning a startled look on his companion.
domestication: “不会出什么差错吧？”那个陌生人突然停下脚步，转过头来，惊讶地看着同伴。
foreignization: “没有什么坏事吧？”陌生人说，突然停住脚步，转过头来，对他的同伴投去一惊讶的目光。

source: “Stop, stop! don’t take him away! For Heaven’s sake stop a moment!” cried the new comer, breathless with haste.
domestication: “住手，住手！别带他走啊！为了上帝的份上，停一下吧！”新来的那个人气喘吁吁地大声喊道。
foreignization: “停，停！不要带他走！为了上帝的缘故停下来片刻！”新来的那个人气喘吁吁地哭喊道。

source: “No, no,” rejoined Fagin, finding his voice. “It’s not—you’re not the person, Bill. I’ve no—no fault to find with you.”
domestication: "不，不”，费金开口说话了，“不是你， 빌，是另外一个人。我没什么可抱怨你的。”
foreignization: “不，不，”费金找回了自己的声音，回答道。“不是——你不是那个，人，比尔，我对你没有——任何不满。”

“No, no,” rejoined Fagin, finding his voice. “It’s not—you’re not the person, Bill. I’ve no—no fault to find with you.”
"不，不”，费金开口说话了，“不是你， 빌，是另外一个人。我没什么可抱怨你的。”

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“No, no,” rejoined Fagin, finding his voice. “It’s not—you’re not the person, Bill. I’ve no—no fault to find with you.”
“不，不，”费金找回了自己的声音，回答道。“不是——你不是那个，人，比尔，我对你没有——任何不满。”

source: Oliver had been dozing; but looked worse, and was more feverish than he had appeared yet. Being assisted by the doctor, he managed to sit up in bed for a minute or so; and looked at the strangers without at all understanding what was going forward—in fact, without seeming to recollect where he was, or what had been passing.
domestication: 奥利弗一直在打瞌睡，但现在看起来更加糟糕，发烧得更厉害了。在医生的帮助下，他勉强坐起身来，坐在床上一会儿，他望着陌生人，完全没有搞清楚发生了什么事——实际上，就好像他根本记不得自己在哪里，或者刚才发生了什么一样。他只是茫然地看着他们，一脸迷糊的样子。
foreignization: 奥利弗一直处于瞌睡状态，但看起来更糟糕，且比之前更加发烧。在医生的帮助下，他设法坐起身来，坐在床上一两分钟；并看着陌生人，没有完全明白发生了什么事——事实上，似乎根本记不得自己在哪里，也不知道发生了什么事。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Oliver had been dozing; but looked worse, and was more feverish than he had appeared yet. Being assisted by the doctor, he managed to sit up in bed for a minute or so; and looked at the strangers without at all understanding what was going forward—in fact, without seeming to recollect where he was, or what had been passing.
奥利弗一直在打瞌睡，但现在看起来更加糟糕，发烧得更厉害了。在医生的帮助下，他勉强坐起身来，坐在床上一会儿，他望着陌生人，完全没有搞清楚发生了什么事——实际上，就好像他根本记不得自己在哪里，或者刚才发生了什么一样。他只是茫然地看着他们，一脸迷糊的样子。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Oliver had been dozing; but looked worse, and was more feverish than he had appeared yet. Being assisted by the doctor, he managed to sit up in bed for a minute or so; and looked at the strangers without at all understanding what was going forward—in fact, without seeming to recollect where he was, or what had been passing.
奥利弗一直处于瞌睡状态，但看起来更糟糕，且比之前更加发烧。在医生的帮助下，他设法坐起身来，坐在床上一两分钟；并看着陌生人，没有完全明白发生了什么事——事实上，似乎根本记不得自己在哪里，也不知道发生了什么事。

source: Mr. Bumble sat in the workhouse parlour, with his eyes moodily fixed on the cheerless grate, whence, as it was summer time, no brighter gleam proceeded, than the reflection of certain sickly rays of the sun, which were sent back from its cold and shining surface. A paper fly-cage dangled from the ceiling, to which he occasionally raised his eyes in gloomy thought; and, as the heedless insects hovered round the gaudy net-work, Mr. Bumble would heave a deep sigh, while a more gloomy shadow overspread his countenance. Mr. Bumble was meditating; it might be that the insects brought to mind, some painful passage in his own past life.
domestication: 班布尔先生坐在济贫院客厅里，愁容满面地盯着没有火焰的壁炉，夏天的太阳只能从冷冰冰的炉子上反射出几缕无力的光线。他偶尔会抬头看着从天花板上悬挂下来的纸制苍蝇笼，里面那些毫无顾忌的苍蝇在五颜六色的网眼周围飞舞，让班布尔先生不禁发出深深的叹息，他的脸上也蒙上了一层更加阴郁的阴影。看起来他正在沉思，也许是那些苍蝇让他想起了自己过去生活中一些痛苦的回忆。
foreignization: 班布尔先生坐在济贫院的客厅里，他的眼睛郁郁寡欢地盯着寒冷的壁炉，在夏季，壁炉里没有比阳光中某些病态的光线更明亮的光芒，这些光线从它冰冷、发光的表面反射回来。一张纸做的苍蝇笼子从天花板上悬挂下来，他有时会把眼光抬起来，陷入阴郁的沉思；当那些不顾一切的昆虫在五彩的网状物周围飞舞时，班布尔先生会发出一声深深的叹息，而他的脸上投下了一层更加阴郁的阴影。班布尔先生正在沉思；可能是这些昆虫让他想起了自己过去生活中的一些痛苦经历。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Mr. Bumble sat in the workhouse parlour, with his eyes moodily fixed on the cheerless grate, whence, as it was summer time, no brighter gleam proceeded, than the reflection of certain sickly rays of the sun, which were sent back from its cold and shining surface. A paper fly-cage dangled from the ceiling, to which he occasionally raised his eyes in gloomy thought; and, as the heedless insects hovered round the gaudy net-work, Mr. Bumble would heave a deep sigh, while a more gloomy shadow overspread his countenance. Mr. Bumble was meditating; it might be that the insects brought to mind, some painful passage in his own past life.
班布尔先生坐在济贫院客厅里，愁容满面地盯着没有火焰的壁炉，夏天的太阳只能从冷冰冰的炉子上反射出几缕无力的光线。他偶尔会抬头看着从天花板上悬挂下来的纸制苍蝇笼，里面那些毫无顾忌的苍蝇在五颜六色的网眼周围飞舞，让班布尔先生不禁发出深深的叹息，他的脸上也蒙上了一层更加阴郁的阴影。看起来他正在沉思，也许是那些苍蝇让他想起了自己过去生活中一些痛苦的回忆。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Mr. Bumble sat in the workhouse parlour, with his eyes moodily fixed on the cheerless grate, whence, as it was summer time, no brighter gleam proceeded, than the reflection of certain sickly rays of the sun, which were sent back from its cold and shining surface. A paper fly-cage dangled from the ceiling, to which he occasionally raised his eyes in gloomy thought; and, as the heedless insects hovered round the gaudy net-work, Mr. Bumble would heave a deep sigh, while a more gloomy shadow overspread his countenance. Mr. Bumble was meditating; it might be that the insects brought to mind, some painful passage in his own past life.

班布尔先生坐在济贫院的客厅里，他的眼睛郁郁寡欢地盯着寒冷的壁炉，在夏季，壁炉里没有比阳光中某些病态的光线更明亮的光芒，这些光线从它冰冷、发光的表面反射回来。一张纸做的苍蝇笼子从天花板上悬挂下来，他有时会把眼光抬起来，陷入阴郁的沉思；当那些不顾一切的昆虫在五彩的网状物周围飞舞时，班布尔先生会发出一声深深的叹息，而他的脸上投下了一层更加阴郁的阴影。班布尔先生正在沉思；可能是这些昆虫让他想起了自己过去生活中的一些痛苦经历。

source: “Keep back the dog, Bill!” cried Nancy, springing before the door, and closing it, as the Jew and his two pupils darted out in pursuit. “Keep back the dog; he’ll tear the boy to pieces.”
domestication: “快拦住狗， 빌！”南希大喊一声，突然冲到门前，把门关上，这时，那个犹太人和他的两个徒弟已经朝外面窜去追赶。“拦住狗，别让它把孩子撕成碎片！”
foreignization: “拦住狗， 比尔！”南希哭喊着，在门前跳起并关闭了门，因为犹太人和他的两个学生冲了出去追赶。“拦住狗；他会把男孩撕成碎片。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Keep back the dog, Bill!” cried Nancy, springing before the door, and closing it, as the Jew and his two pupils darted out in pursuit. “Keep back the dog; he’ll tear the boy to pieces.”
“快拦住狗， 빌！”南希大喊一声，突然冲到门前，把门关上，这时，那个犹太人和他的两个徒弟已经朝外面窜去追赶。“拦住狗，别让它把孩子撕成碎片！”

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Keep back the dog, Bill!” cried Nancy, springing before the door, and closing it, as the Jew and his two pupils darted out in pursuit. “Keep back the dog; he’ll tear the boy to pieces.”
“拦住狗， 比尔！”南希哭喊着，在门前跳起并关闭了门，因为犹太人和他的两个学生冲了出去追赶。“拦住狗；他会把男孩撕成碎片。”

source: “That’s the second,” observed the woman with much deliberation. “The first is, what may the communication be worth?”
domestication: “这算是第二点了，”女人慢慢地说，“第一点是，这个消息到底值多少钱？”

source: This brief direction to Charley Bates, and his recent antagonist, was softly and immediately obeyed. There was no sound of their whereabout, when the Dodger descended the stairs, bearing the light in his hand, and followed by a man in a coarse smock-frock; who, after casting a hurried glance round the room, pulled off a large wrapper which had concealed the lower portion of his face, and disclosed: all haggard, unwashed, and unshorn: the features of flash Toby Crackit.
domestication: 道格尔对查理·贝茨和他刚刚的对手简短地发出指令，这个指令被立即执行了，两人马上消失在黑暗中。等到道格尔提着灯走下楼梯时，这里已经恢复了沉寂。在他身后跟着一个穿着粗糙工作服的人，这个人迅速扫视了一下房间，然后脱掉了蒙在脸上的大围巾，露出了他的面容，原来是那个面目全非、蓬头垢面、胡子拉碴的托比·克拉基特。
foreignization: 这简短的命令被给予查理·贝茨和他最近的对手，立刻被轻轻地服从了。在Dodger带着手中的灯下楼时，周围没有他们的任何声音，后面跟着一个穿着粗糙罩衫的人；这个人在房间里快速扫视了一眼后，脱掉了一件大斗篷，这件斗篷遮住了他脸的下半部分，露出了：憔悴、没洗脸、胡子拉碴的特征：闪电托比·克雷基特的五官。

道格尔对查理·贝茨和他刚刚的对手简短地发出指令，这个指令被立即执行了，两人马上消失在黑暗中。等到道格尔提着灯走下楼梯时，这里已经恢复了沉寂。在他身后跟着一个穿着粗糙工作服的人，这个人迅速扫视了一下房间，然后脱掉了蒙在脸上的大围巾，露出了他的面容，原来是那个面目全非、蓬头垢面、胡子拉碴的托比·克拉基特。

source: “I am going to sit here, as long as I think proper, ma’am,” rejoined Mr. Bumble; “and although I was not snoring, I shall snore, gape, sneeze, laugh, or cry, as the humour strikes me; such being my prerogative.”
domestication: 班布尔先生回答说：“我想坐就坐，想坐多久就坐多久，夫人。我虽然没在打瞌睡，但我可以打瞌睡，也可以哈欠、擤鼻涕、大笑或大哭，这都是我的权利。”
foreignization: “我要在这里坐一会儿，只要我认为合适，夫人，”班布尔先生回答道；“虽然我不是在打瞌睡，我将会打瞌睡，哈欠，打喷嚏，大笑或哭泣，按照我的心情；作为我的特权。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“I am going to sit here, as long as I think proper, ma’am,” rejoined Mr. Bumble; “and although I was not snoring, I shall snore, gape, sneeze, laugh, or cry, as the humour strikes me; such being my prerogative.”
班布尔先生回答说：“我想坐就坐，想坐多久就坐多久，夫人。我虽然没在打瞌睡，但我可以打瞌睡，也可以哈欠、擤鼻涕、大笑或大哭，这都是我的权利。”

messages_foreignization.content:

“I am going to sit here, as long as I think proper, ma’am,” rejoined Mr. Bumble; “and although I was not snoring, I shall snore, gape, sneeze, laugh, or cry, as the humour strikes me; such being my prerogative.”
“我要在这里坐一会儿，只要我认为合适，夫人，”班布尔先生回答道；“虽然我不是在打瞌睡，我将会打瞌睡，哈欠，打喷嚏，大笑或哭泣，按照我的心情；作为我的特权。”

source: “I beg your pardon, Mr. Harry,” said Giles: giving a final polish to his ruffled countenance with the handkerchief; “but if you would leave the postboy to say that, I should be very much obliged to you. It wouldn’t be proper for the maids to see me in this state, sir; I should never have any more authority with them if they did.”
domestication: “劳驾您了，哈里先生，”吉尔斯说着，用手帕给自己揩了揩刚才被抓乱的脸，“要是您能让马夫代替我说一声就好了，我会很感激的。如果女佣们看到我现在这个样子，就没法管她们了，我也就失去了威严。”
foreignization: “我请求原谅，哈利先生，”盖尔斯说着，用手帕给他凌乱的脸再次擦拭了一遍；“但是如果你能让马夫来说这件事，我将非常感激你。如果女仆们看到我现在这个样子就不合适了，先生；如果她们看到的话，我以后就再也不会在她们面前拥有任何权威了。”

source: “Then, if your lot had been differently cast,” rejoined Rose; “if you had been even a little, but not so far, above me; if I could have been a help and comfort to you in any humble scene of peace and retirement, and not a blot and drawback in ambitious and distinguished crowds; I should have been spared this trial. I have every reason to be happy, very happy, now; but then, Harry, I own I should have been happier.”
domestication: "那假如命运对你我另有安排呢，”罗斯回答说，“假如你也不是高高在上，而是与我更接近一些；假如我能在宁静的乡村生活中成为你的依靠和安慰，而不是在豪华社交场合里成为你的累赘；那么，我也许就能避免这番磨难了。现在，我有足够的理由感到幸福，真的非常幸福，但那时，哈利，我承认，我会更加幸福。"
foreignization: "如果命运让我们的处境有所不同的话，”罗丝回答说，“如果你只稍微高我一等，而不是离我这么远；如果我能在某种平静和退休的谦逊场景中成为你的帮助和安慰，而不是在野心勃勃和杰出的圈子里成为一个污点和障碍，我就能避免这次考验。我现在有足够的理由感到幸福，非常幸福，但那时，哈里，我承认我会更幸福。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Then, if your lot had been differently cast,” rejoined Rose; “if you had been even a little, but not so far, above me; if I could have been a help and comfort to you in any humble scene of peace and retirement, and not a blot and drawback in ambitious and distinguished crowds; I should have been spared this trial. I have every reason to be happy, very happy, now; but then, Harry, I own I should have been happier.”
"那假如命运对你我另有安排呢，”罗斯回答说，“假如你也不是高高在上，而是与我更接近一些；假如我能在宁静的乡村生活中成为你的依靠和安慰，而不是在豪华社交场合里成为你的累赘；那么，我也许就能避免这番磨难了。现在，我有足够的理由感到幸福，真的非常幸福，但那时，哈利，我承认，我会更加幸福。"

“Then, if your lot had been differently cast,” rejoined Rose; “if you had been even a little, but not so far, above me; if I could have been a help and comfort to you in any humble scene of peace and retirement, and not a blot and drawback in ambitious and distinguished crowds; I should have been spared this trial. I have every reason to be happy, very happy, now; but then, Harry, I own I should have been happier.”
"如果命运让我们的处境有所不同的话，”罗丝回答说，“如果你只稍微高我一等，而不是离我这么远；如果我能在某种平静和退休的谦逊场景中成为你的帮助和安慰，而不是在野心勃勃和杰出的圈子里成为一个污点和障碍，我就能避免这次考验。我现在有足够的理由感到幸福，非常幸福，但那时，哈里，我承认我会更幸福。”

source: There is a kind of sleep that steals upon us sometimes, which, while it holds the body prisoner, does not free the mind from a sense of things about it, and enable it to ramble at its pleasure. So far as an overpowering heaviness, a prostration of strength, and an utter inability to control our thoughts or power of motion, can be called sleep, this is it; and yet, we have a consciousness of all that is going on about us, and, if we dream at such a time, words which are really spoken, or sounds which really exist at the moment, accommodate themselves with surprising readiness to our visions, until reality and imagination become so strangely blended that it is afterwards almost matter of impossibility to separate the two. Nor is this, the most striking phenomenon incidental to such a state. It is an undoubted fact, that although our senses of touch and sight be for the time dead, yet our sleeping thoughts, and the visionary scenes that pass before us, will be influenced and materially influenced, by the mere silent presence of some external object; which may not have been near us when we closed our eyes: and of whose vicinity we have had no waking consciousness.
domestication: 有时候，我们会被一种特殊的睡眠偷袭，这种睡眠虽然能让身体陷入沉睡，却不能让思维获得完全的自由。即便是身体被困住了，重得无法动弹，思维也无法控制，然而我们仍然能感受到周围的一切。如果在这种状态下我们做梦了，现实中的声音和言语会奇怪地与我们的梦境融合在一起，直到现实和想象变得难以区分。这种状态还有一种更奇特的现象，那就是尽管我们的触觉和视觉在那一刻可能是麻木的，但我们的梦境和脑海中浮现的画面还是会受到外部物体的影响，而这些物体可能在我们闭眼时并没有靠近我们，我们也没有清醒时的意识去察觉它们的存在。
foreignization: 有时候会有一种睡眠悄悄地袭来，在这种睡眠中，虽然身体被囚禁，但心灵并没有从对周围事物的感知中解脱出来，也没有让它自由地漫游。只要一种压倒性的沉重感、力量的丧失和完全无法控制我们的思维或运动能力可以被称为睡眠，那么这就是睡眠；然而，我们仍然对周围发生的一切都有所意识，如果我们在这种时候做梦，真正被说出的言语或真正在那一刻存在的声音会以惊人的敏捷性地适应我们的幻象，直到现实和想象以如此奇怪的方式融合，以至于之后几乎不可能将两者区分开来。而这并不是这种状态下最引人注目的现象。有一点是毫无疑问的：尽管我们的触觉和视觉在那时处于麻痹状态，但我们的睡眠思维和我们面前的幻象景象仍会受到外部某个物体的单纯而静默的存在的影响，并且这种影响是具有决定性的；这种物体在我们闭眼时可能并不在我们附近，我们也没有清醒时的意识到它的靠近。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

There is a kind of sleep that steals upon us sometimes, which, while it holds the body prisoner, does not free the mind from a sense of things about it, and enable it to ramble at its pleasure. So far as an overpowering heaviness, a prostration of strength, and an utter inability to control our thoughts or power of motion, can be called sleep, this is it; and yet, we have a consciousness of all that is going on about us, and, if we dream at such a time, words which are really spoken, or sounds which really exist at the moment, accommodate themselves with surprising readiness to our visions, until reality and imagination become so strangely blended that it is afterwards almost matter of impossibility to separate the two. Nor is this, the most striking phenomenon incidental to such a state. It is an undoubted fact, that although our senses of touch and sight be for the time dead, yet our sleeping thoughts, and the visionary scenes that pass before us, will be influenced and materially influenced, by the mere silent presence of some external object; which may not have been near us when we closed our eyes: and of whose vicinity we have had no waking consciousness.

有时候，我们会被一种特殊的睡眠偷袭，这种睡眠虽然能让身体陷入沉睡，却不能让思维获得完全的自由。即便是身体被困住了，重得无法动弹，思维也无法控制，然而我们仍然能感受到周围的一切。如果在这种状态下我们做梦了，现实中的声音和言语会奇怪地与我们的梦境融合在一起，直到现实和想象变得难以区分。这种状态还有一种更奇特的现象，那就是尽管我们的触觉和视觉在那一刻可能是麻木的，但我们的梦境和脑海中浮现的画面还是会受到外部物体的影响，而这些物体可能在我们闭眼时并没有靠近我们，我们也没有清醒时的意识去察觉它们的存在。

Translate the following text to Chinese.

There is a kind of sleep that steals upon us sometimes, which, while it holds the body prisoner, does not free the mind from a sense of things about it, and enable it to ramble at its pleasure. So far as an overpowering heaviness, a prostration of strength, and an utter inability to control our thoughts or power of motion, can be called sleep, this is it; and yet, we have a consciousness of all that is going on about us, and, if we dream at such a time, words which are really spoken, or sounds which really exist at the moment, accommodate themselves with surprising readiness to our visions, until reality and imagination become so strangely blended that it is afterwards almost matter of impossibility to separate the two. source: There was nobody inside but a miserable shoeless criminal, who had been taken up for playing the flute, and who, the offence against society having been clearly proved, had been very properly committed by Mr. Fang to the House of Correction for one month; with the appropriate and amusing remark that since he had so much breath to spare, it would be more wholesomely expended on the treadmill than in a musical instrument. He made no answer: being occupied mentally bewailing the loss of the flute, which had been confiscated for the use of the county: so Nancy passed on to the next cell, and knocked there.
domestication: 里面一个人都没有，只有个可怜兮兮、光着脚的歹徒，被抓来是因为他在街上吹笛子，证据确凿，犯了众怒，法官判他进劳教所关一个月，还顺嘴说，他既然有这么多气，可以去踩踏车积点功德，而不是拿来吹奏乐器。 这家伙没吱声，他正在想他的笛子，那东西已经被没收，交给了县政府，心里正惋惜得要命呢。这时，南希就走到下一个牢房门口敲门了。
foreignization: 里面除了一个悲惨的赤脚罪犯以外，一个人也没有，这个人因为演奏长笛而被逮捕，既然他对社会的犯罪行为已经被明确证明，法官范恩先生很恰当地判他在惩教院关一个月，并附带着一句适当且有趣的评论，说既然他有那么多的气可以浪费，那么在跑步机上发泄比在音乐乐器上更有益于健康。他没有回答，因为他的心思被占据着，为没收给县政府的长笛感到遗憾；因此南希继续前往下一个牢房，并在那里敲门。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

There was nobody inside but a miserable shoeless criminal, who had been taken up for playing the flute, and who, the offence against society having been clearly proved, had been very properly committed by Mr. Fang to the House of Correction for one month; with the appropriate and amusing remark that since he had so much breath to spare, it would be more wholesomely expended on the treadmill than in a musical instrument. He made no answer: being occupied mentally bewailing the loss of the flute, which had been confiscated for the use of the county: so Nancy passed on to the next cell, and knocked there.
里面一个人都没有，只有个可怜兮兮、光着脚的歹徒，被抓来是因为他在街上吹笛子，证据确凿，犯了众怒，法官判他进劳教所关一个月，还顺嘴说，他既然有这么多气，可以去踩踏车积点功德，而不是拿来吹奏乐器。 这家伙没吱声，他正在想他的笛子，那东西已经被没收，交给了县政府，心里正惋惜得要命呢。这时，南希就走到下一个牢房门口敲门了。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

There was nobody inside but a miserable shoeless criminal, who had been taken up for playing the flute, and who, the offence against society having been clearly proved, had been very properly committed by Mr. Fang to the House of Correction for one month; with the appropriate and amusing remark that since he had so much breath to spare, it would be more wholesomely expended on the treadmill than in a musical instrument. He made no answer: being occupied mentally bewailing the loss of the flute, which had been confiscated for the use of the county: so Nancy passed on to the next cell, and knocked there.
里面除了一个悲惨的赤脚罪犯以外，一个人也没有，这个人因为演奏长笛而被逮捕，既然他对社会的犯罪行为已经被明确证明，法官范恩先生很恰当地判他在惩教院关一个月，并附带着一句适当且有趣的评论，说既然他有那么多的气可以浪费，那么在跑步机上发泄比在音乐乐器上更有益于健康。他没有回答，因为他的心思被占据着，为没收给县政府的长笛感到遗憾；因此南希继续前往下一个牢房，并在那里敲门。

source: “As you like, my dear, as you like” replied the Jew. “Is there no help wanted, but yours and Toby’s?”
domestication: “随你便，亲爱的，就像你喜欢的那样”，犹太人回答道。“难道除了你和托比之外就没有其他人可以提供帮助了吗？”

source: “The gallows,” continued Fagin, “the gallows, my dear, is an ugly finger-post, which points out a very short and sharp turning that has stopped many a bold fellow’s career on the broad highway. To keep in the easy road, and keep it at a distance, is object number one with you.”
domestication: “绞架”，费金接着说，“可不是什么好玩意儿，小家伙。这是一个丑陋的指路牌，指出了一条非常短暂而又险峻的岔路，这条路已经让无数胆大妄为的家伙走上了绝路。所以，为了能安安稳稳地走宽敞的大路，而不是转入那条死胡同，这就是你需要牢记的第一要点。”
foreignization: “绞刑架，”法金继续说，“绞刑架，我的亲爱的，是一个丑陋的指路牌，它指出了一条非常短暂而急剧的弯道，这个弯道已经阻止了很多胆大的人在宽阔的公路上的事业。要保持在容易的道路上，并且与之保持距离，是你们的首要目标。”

source: “Stay another moment,” interposed Rose, as the girl moved hurriedly towards the door. “Think once again on your own condition, and the opportunity you have of escaping from it. You have a claim on me: not only as the voluntary bearer of this intelligence, but as a woman lost almost beyond redemption. Will you return to this gang of robbers, and to this man, when a word can save you? What fascination is it that can take you back, and make you cling to wickedness and misery? Oh! is there no chord in your heart that I can touch! Is there nothing left, to which I can appeal against this terrible infatuation!”
domestication: “再等一下，”罗斯截住了女孩急忙朝门口走去的路，“再好好想想你的处境和摆脱它的机会吧。你对我有求助之恩，不只是因为你主动提供了这个信息，还因为你是一个几乎已经失去救赎的女人。你难道要回到那帮强盗和那个男人身边，而你只要说句话就能获得自由？是什么迷惑让你回心转意，依恋着邪恶和苦难？哦，有没有哪根心弦我可以拨动？还剩下什么是我可以诉说的，让我来打破这可怕的迷恋？”
foreignization: "再等一下，”罗斯打断道，当那个女孩急忙向门口走去时。“再想想你的处境和你可以摆脱它的机会。你对我有所求：不仅是因为你自愿带来了这个消息，而且因为你是一个几乎没有救赎的女人。你会回到这群强盗中，再回到那个男人身边，而一个字就可以拯救你吗？是什么样的迷惑使你回去，并让你紧握着邪恶和痛苦呢？哦！你心中难道没有我能触动的弦吗？难道没有什么可以让我反对这种可怕的着迷了吗？！

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Stay another moment,” interposed Rose, as the girl moved hurriedly towards the door. “Think once again on your own condition, and the opportunity you have of escaping from it. You have a claim on me: not only as the voluntary bearer of this intelligence, but as a woman lost almost beyond redemption. Will you return to this gang of robbers, and to this man, when a word can save you? What fascination is it that can take you back, and make you cling to wickedness and misery? Oh! is there no chord in your heart that I can touch! Is there nothing left, to which I can appeal against this terrible infatuation!”
“再等一下，”罗斯截住了女孩急忙朝门口走去的路，“再好好想想你的处境和摆脱它的机会吧。你对我有求助之恩，不只是因为你主动提供了这个信息，还因为你是一个几乎已经失去救赎的女人。你难道要回到那帮强盗和那个男人身边，而你只要说句话就能获得自由？是什么迷惑让你回心转意，依恋着邪恶和苦难？哦，有没有哪根心弦我可以拨动？还剩下什么是我可以诉说的，让我来打破这可怕的迷恋？”

source: The host smiled, disappeared, and shortly afterwards returned with a steaming jorum: of which, the first gulp brought the water into Mr. Bumble’s eyes.
domestication: 主持人笑了笑，然后就不见了踪影，但很快他又带着一大壶热气腾腾的东西回来了，当班布尔先生喝了一口后，眼泪都快出来了。
foreignization: 主人微笑着消失了，不久后他带着一大壶冒着热气的液体回来：喝下第一口时，液体的热气让巴伯先生的眼睛里充满了泪水。

source: Oliver did see it in his mind’s eye as distinctly as if he had not altered his position; but he thought it better not to worry the kind old lady; so he smiled gently when she looked at him; and Mrs. Bedwin, satisfied that he felt more comfortable, salted and broke bits of toasted bread into the broth, with all the bustle befitting so solemn a preparation. Oliver got through it with extraordinary expedition. He had scarcely swallowed the last spoonful, when there came a soft rap at the door. “Come in,” said the old lady; and in walked Mr. Brownlow.
domestication: 奥利弗在脑海里依然清晰地看到刚才的情景，就好像他根本没动过一样。但他觉得不应该让这位善良的老太太担心，所以当她看着他的时候，他就微笑了一下。贝德温夫人看到他似乎感觉好些了，于是就放心地继续忙活着，给汤撒盐，弄碎面包，全部都摆在桌子上，准备好后才端给奥利弗。奥利弗很快就把汤喝完了。他刚喝完最后一口汤，还没来得及出声，门外就传来了轻轻的敲门声。“进来，”老太太说。布朗洛先生走了进来。
foreignization: 奥利弗在他脑海的视觉中看到这一幕就像他没有改变他的位置一样清晰；但他认为最好不用烦恼这位仁慈的老太太；所以当她看着他时他温和地微笑；而贝德温夫人，满意的是他感觉更舒适了，把盐撒到并把烤面包弄碎放进肉汤里，所有这些都值得如此庄严的准备。 奥利弗以非常快的速度完成了它。当他几乎吞下最后一勺的时候，门口传来轻轻的敲门声。“进来，”老太太说；布朗洛先生走了进来。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Oliver did see it in his mind’s eye as distinctly as if he had not altered his position; but he thought it better not to worry the kind old lady; so he smiled gently when she looked at him; and Mrs. Bedwin, satisfied that he felt more comfortable, salted and broke bits of toasted bread into the broth, with all the bustle befitting so solemn a preparation. Oliver got through it with extraordinary expedition. He had scarcely swallowed the last spoonful, when there came a soft rap at the door. “Come in,” said the old lady; and in walked Mr. Brownlow.
奥利弗在脑海里依然清晰地看到刚才的情景，就好像他根本没动过一样。但他觉得不应该让这位善良的老太太担心，所以当她看着他的时候，他就微笑了一下。贝德温夫人看到他似乎感觉好些了，于是就放心地继续忙活着，给汤撒盐，弄碎面包，全部都摆在桌子上，准备好后才端给奥利弗。奥利弗很快就把汤喝完了。他刚喝完最后一口汤，还没来得及出声，门外就传来了轻轻的敲门声。“进来，”老太太说。布朗洛先生走了进来。

source: As he expressed himself to this effect, with assumed carelessness, he took a step or two nearer the concealed spy, as the latter could tell from the distinctness with which he heard him mutter, “It must be he!”
domestication: 当他漫不经心地表达这些时，他朝着躲藏的密探走近了一两步，这一点从密探清晰地听到他自言自语“一定是他”的声音中就能听出来。
foreignization: 当他轻描淡写地表达了这一点时，他朝躲藏起来的间谍又迈近了一两步，就像后者能听出他低声嘟囔道：“一定是他！”从他听得很清楚来看

source: “It is,” cried the girl, writhing in agony of her mind; “I cannot leave him now! I could not be his death.”

source: There were assembled, in that lower house of the domestic parliament, the women-servants, Mr. Brittles, Mr. Giles, the tinker (who had received a special invitation to regale himself for the remainder of the day, in consideration of his services), and the constable. The latter gentleman had a large staff, a large head, large features, and large half-boots; and he looked as if he had been taking a proportionate allowance of ale—as indeed he had.
domestication: 那一天，自家院子里一片热闹，下人们都聚集在客厅里。女佣们、布里特尔先生、盖尔斯先生，还有那个修理锅的铁匠也在其中，他因为提供了服务，受到了特别的邀请，可以在剩下的时间里尽情享受美食。最后，当然少不了那个警察。他长得又高又壮，脑袋大，五官粗犷，脚底下是一双巨大的马靴。他看起来就像喝了不少啤酒，事实上确实如此。
foreignization: 在那座家庭议会的下议院里，聚集了女佣们，布里特尔先生，盖尔斯先生，修补锅师傅（他收到了一份特别邀请，考虑到他的服务，他将会享受这一天剩下的时间），以及警察。后者身材魁伟，头很大，五官也很大，穿着又大又笨重的半长靴；看起来他好像喝了一定量的啤酒——事实上，他确实喝了。

The latter gentleman had a large staff, a large head, large features, and large half-boots; and he looked as if he had been taking a proportionate allowance of ale—as indeed he had.

There were assembled, in that lower house of the domestic parliament, the women-servants, Mr. Brittles, Mr. Giles, the tinker (who had received a special invitation to regale himself for the remainder of the day, in consideration of his services), and the constable. The latter gentleman had a large staff, a large head, large features, and large half-boots; and he looked as if he had been taking a proportionate allowance of ale—as indeed he had.
在那座家庭议会的下议院里，聚集了女佣们，布里特尔先生，盖尔斯先生，修补锅师傅（他收到了一份特别邀请，考虑到他的服务，他将会享受这一天剩下的时间），以及警察。后者身材魁伟，头很大，五官也很大，穿着又大又笨重的半长靴；看起来他好像喝了一定量的啤酒——事实上，他确实喝了。

source: At length, when his hands were raw with beating against the heavy door and walls, two men appeared: one bearing a candle, which he thrust into an iron candlestick fixed against the wall: the other dragging in a mattress on which to pass the night; for the prisoner was to be left alone no more.
domestication: 最后，等他敲打厚重的门和墙壁双手都磨破了，有两个人出现了：一个拿着蜡烛，把它插在墙上铁烛台里，另一个拖进来一张床垫子，因为囚犯不能再被留下独自待着了。
foreignization: 终于，当他的双手因为敲打沉重的门和墙壁而变得生痛时，出现了两个人：一个拿着蜡烛的人，把蜡烛塞进了固定在墙上的铁烛台里；另一个拖着一张床垫子，准备在这里过夜；因为囚犯不再被单独留下了。

messages_domestication.content:

At length, when his hands were raw with beating against the heavy door and walls, two men appeared: one bearing a candle, which he thrust into an iron candlestick fixed against the wall: the other dragging in a mattress on which to pass the night; for the prisoner was to be left alone no more.

source: “A boy!” exclaimed Mr. Giles, valiantly, pushing the tinker into the background. “What’s the matter with the—eh?—Why—Brittles—look here—don’t you know?”
domestication: “是个男孩！”吉尔斯先生大声说道，同时把修理锅的人推到一边。“到底出了什么事？嗯……怎么了，布里特尔斯？你看……难道你不知道吗？”

source: But, as neither of these criminals answered to the name of Oliver, or knew anything about him, Nancy made straight up to the bluff officer in the striped waistcoat; and with the most piteous wailings and lamentations, rendered more piteous by a prompt and efficient use of the street-door key and the little basket, demanded her own dear brother.
domestication: 然而，这两个人都没有一个叫奥利弗，也不知道奥利弗是谁，南希于是直冲那位穿条纹马甲的粗鲁警官，放声哀嚎，引得众人同情，她还趁机巧妙地使用了门钥匙和小篮子，弄得更是声泪俱下，要求警官交出她亲爱的弟弟。

source: “And yet,” said the old man, dropping his hands on his knees, “it’s a sad thing, my dear, to lose so much when we had set our hearts upon it.”

source: He went through Islington; strode up the hill at Highgate on which stands the stone in honour of Whittington; turned down to Highgate Hill, unsteady of purpose, and uncertain where to go; struck off to the right again, almost as soon as he began to descend it; and taking the foot-path across the fields, skirted Caen Wood, and so came on Hampstead Heath. Traversing the hollow by the Vale of Heath, he mounted the opposite bank, and crossing the road which joins the villages of Hampstead and Highgate, made along the remaining portion of the heath to the fields at North End, in one of which he laid himself down under a hedge, and slept.
domestication: 他穿过伊斯灵顿，走上位于海格特山的那块纪念惠廷顿的石碑前，然后又向下走海格特山，但他好像没有目的地一样，东倒西歪，根本不知道要去哪里。他刚开始下山，就又拐到右边，走了一条小路，绕过凯恩树林，于是就到了汉普斯特德荒野。 他穿过荒野中低洼的谷地，登上了对面的斜坡，横跨连接汉普斯特德和海格特的道路，继续在荒野的剩余部分行走，直到北端的田地里，他躺在一棵树下睡着了。
foreignization: 他经过伊斯灵顿；登上了在Highgate有纪念惠廷顿的石碑的那座山丘；向下走到Highgate Hill，目的不明确，不确定去哪里；几乎一开始下坡就又向右转；然后取道穿过田野，沿着肯森林的边缘前行，于是就来到了汉普斯特德荒野。穿过希斯河谷的凹地，他登上了对面的河岸，并横跨连接汉普斯特德和Highgate村庄的道路，沿着荒野剩余部分到了诺斯埃нд的田野，在其中一个田野里他躺在树篱下睡着了。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

He went through Islington; strode up the hill at Highgate on which stands the stone in honour of Whittington; turned down to Highgate Hill, unsteady of purpose, and uncertain where to go; struck off to the right again, almost as soon as he began to descend it; and taking the foot-path across the fields, skirted Caen Wood, and so came on Hampstead Heath. Traversing the hollow by the Vale of Heath, he mounted the opposite bank, and crossing the road which joins the villages of Hampstead and Highgate, made along the remaining portion of the heath to the fields at North End, in one of which he laid himself down under a hedge, and slept.
他穿过伊斯灵顿，走上位于海格特山的那块纪念惠廷顿的石碑前，然后又向下走海格特山，但他好像没有目的地一样，东倒西歪，根本不知道要去哪里。他刚开始下山，就又拐到右边，走了一条小路，绕过凯恩树林，于是就到了汉普斯特德荒野。 他穿过荒野中低洼的谷地，登上了对面的斜坡，横跨连接汉普斯特德和海格特的道路，继续在荒野的剩余部分行走，直到北端的田地里，他躺在一棵树下睡着了。

Traversing the hollow by the Vale of Heath, he mounted the opposite bank, and crossing the road which joins the villages of Hampstead and Highgate, made along the remaining portion of the heath to the fields at North End, in one of which he laid himself down under a hedge, and slept.

source: But if Oliver, under these influences, had remained silent while they journeyed towards his birth-place by a road he had never seen, how the whole current of his recollections ran back to old times, and what a crowd of emotions were wakened up in his breast, when they turned into that which he had traversed on foot: a poor houseless, wandering boy, without a friend to help him, or a roof to shelter his head.
domestication: 如果奥利弗在这些影响下仍然保持沉默，他们一路朝着他的出生地前进，这条他从未见过的路，让他回忆起了往事，一股回忆的潮流涌上心头。当他们转入那条他曾经独自徒步行走的路时，各种情绪在他心里涌动，因为他记得自己曾是一个孤儿，流落街头，没人帮助，没房子遮风挡雨。
foreignization: 但是如果奥利弗在这些影响下保持沉默，当他们沿着他从未见过的路朝他的出生地行进时，他的全部回忆之河如何回溯到过去的岁月，以及当他们转入他曾经步行经过的那条路时，在他的胸中唤醒了多少情感：一个贫穷、无家的流浪男孩，没有朋友帮助他，也没有屋顶遮蔽他的头。

source: “I recollect,” said the undertaker. “The jury brought it in, ‘Died from exposure to the cold, and want of the common necessaries of life,’ didn’t they?”
domestication: “这件事我还记得，”殡葬业者说。“陪审团的判决是，他死于受寒和缺乏生活必需品，对吧？”
foreignization: “我记得，”殡仪馆老板说，“陪审团的结论是，‘死因为受寒和缺乏生活必需品’，难道不是这样吗？”
