Then he sat down to rest by the side of the milestone, and began to think, for the first time, where he had better go and try to live.

source: Spring flew swiftly by, and summer came. If the village had been beautiful at first it was now in the full glow and luxuriance of its richness. The great trees, which had looked shrunken and bare in the earlier months, had now burst into strong life and health; and stretching forth their green arms over the thirsty ground, converted open and naked spots into choice nooks, where was a deep and pleasant shade from which to look upon the wide prospect, steeped in sunshine, which lay stretched beyond. The earth had donned her mantle of brightest green; and shed her richest perfumes abroad. It was the prime and vigour of the year; all things were glad and flourishing.
domestication: 春天转瞬即逝，夏天到来了。如果村庄一开始就很漂亮，现在它正处于最繁荣的状态。那些在早些时候看起来枯萎和光秃的树木，现在已经充满了生机和活力；它们伸展着绿色的枝叶，覆盖了干燥的大地，把原本开阔和光秃的地方变成了舒适的角落，在那里可以享受到深深的树荫和阳光普照的美景。整个大地披上了最鲜绿的盛装，散发着最浓郁的芳香。这是年中的鼎盛时期，一切都充满了欢乐和生机。
foreignization: 春天飞逝而去，夏天到来了。如果村庄在刚开始时很美丽，现在它处于充满光泽和豪华的富裕之中。那些在早些月份看起来枯萎和光秃的大树，现在突然迸发出了强壮的生命力和健康；并且伸展开它们的绿色手臂到干渴的大地上，把敞开和光秃的场所转变成选择的角落，在那里，有来自广阔视野深处的一种深沉和愉悦的阴凉，这种视野被阳光所笼罩，铺陈在更远的地方。地球披上了她最亮的绿色斗篷；并向外散发着她最丰富的芳香。这是年度的黄金和精力时期；一切事物都很高兴和繁荣。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Spring flew swiftly by, and summer came. If the village had been beautiful at first it was now in the full glow and luxuriance of its richness. The great trees, which had looked shrunken and bare in the earlier months, had now burst into strong life and health; and stretching forth their green arms over the thirsty ground, converted open and naked spots into choice nooks, where was a deep and pleasant shade from which to look upon the wide prospect, steeped in sunshine, which lay stretched beyond. The earth had donned her mantle of brightest green; and shed her richest perfumes abroad. It was the prime and vigour of the year; all things were glad and flourishing.
春天转瞬即逝，夏天到来了。如果村庄一开始就很漂亮，现在它正处于最繁荣的状态。那些在早些时候看起来枯萎和光秃的树木，现在已经充满了生机和活力；它们伸展着绿色的枝叶，覆盖了干燥的大地，把原本开阔和光秃的地方变成了舒适的角落，在那里可以享受到深深的树荫和阳光普照的美景。整个大地披上了最鲜绿的盛装，散发着最浓郁的芳香。这是年中的鼎盛时期，一切都充满了欢乐和生机。

The great trees, which had looked shrunken and bare in the earlier months, had now burst into strong life and health; and stretching forth their green arms over the thirsty ground, converted open and naked spots into choice nooks, where was a deep and pleasant shade from which to look upon the wide prospect, steeped in sunshine, which lay stretched beyond. The earth had donned her mantle of brightest green; and shed her richest perfumes abroad. It was the prime and vigour of the year; all things were glad and flourishing.

春天飞逝而去，夏天到来了。如果村庄在刚开始时很美丽，现在它处于充满光泽和豪华的富裕之中。那些在早些月份看起来枯萎和光秃的大树，现在突然迸发出了强壮的生命力和健康；并且伸展开它们的绿色手臂到干渴的大地上，把敞开和光秃的场所转变成选择的角落，在那里，有来自广阔视野深处的一种深沉和愉悦的阴凉，这种视野被阳光所笼罩，铺陈在更远的地方。地球披上了她最亮的绿色斗篷；并向外散发着她最丰富的芳香。这是年度的黄金和精力时期；一切事物都很高兴和繁荣。

source: He turned over the leaves. Carelessly at first; but, lighting on a passage which attracted his attention, he soon became intent upon the volume. It was a history of the lives and trials of great criminals; and the pages were soiled and thumbed with use. Here, he read of dreadful crimes that made the blood run cold; of secret murders that had been committed by the lonely wayside; of bodies hidden from the eye of man in deep pits and wells: which would not keep them down, deep as they were, but had yielded them up at last, after many years, and so maddened the murderers with the sight, that in their horror they had confessed their guilt, and yelled for the gibbet to end their agony. Here, too, he read of men who, lying in their beds at dead of night, had been tempted (so they said) and led on, by their own bad thoughts, to such dreadful bloodshed as it made the flesh creep, and the limbs quail, to think of. The terrible descriptions were so real and vivid, that the sallow pages seemed to turn red with gore; and the words upon them, to be sounded in his ears, as if they were whispered, in hollow murmurs, by the spirits of the dead.
domestication: 他开始随便翻阅着书页，但很快就被书中的某些内容吸引住了。原来这是一本关于大犯罪者的生平和试炼的历史书籍，书页已经很脏旧并且被翻阅过很多次。在这里，他读到了令人毛骨悚然的可怕罪行，读到孤独的道路旁发生的秘密谋杀案，读到将尸体藏在深坑和井里，却终究还是被发现的故事，这些故事使罪犯们陷入疯狂，以至于他们在恐惧中坦白了自己的罪行，并乞求被绞死来结束自己的痛苦。在这里，他还读到了这样一些人的故事，他们在深夜的床上（如他们所说）被自己的恶念诱惑和驱使，犯下了令人不寒而栗的血腥罪行，使人想到这些就浑身发抖。那些可怕的描述如此真实逼真，以至于书页似乎从黄色变成了血红色，而文字似乎在他的耳边回荡，如同被死者的灵魂以空洞的低语声诉说的那样。
foreignization: 他翻阅着书页，开始时很随意；但当他发现了一段引起他注意的内容后，他很快就全神贯注地阅读这本书。这是一本关于著名罪犯的生平和审判史；书页因常被人翻阅而变得污脏和磨损。在这里，他读到了那些让人毛骨悚然的可怕罪行；在寂静的小路上秘密进行的谋杀案；将尸体隐藏起来，使人无法发现它们，被埋葬在深坑和井里：尽管它们被深深埋葬，但并不能永远将它们困住，最终在多年后还是将它们暴露了出来，并且使凶手们在看到尸体后发疯，他们因恐惧而承认了自己的罪行，并且大声喊叫着要求被绞死来结束他们的痛苦。在这里，他还读到了这样一些人的故事：他们躺在夜半的床上，被（按照他们自己的说法）诱惑并被自己的邪恶想法所驱使，犯下了如此骇人听闻的血腥罪行，以至于仅仅想到这些就足以使人毛骨悚然，四肢发抖。那些可怕的描述是如此真实和生动，以至于书页似乎被鲜血染红；书中的字句似乎在他的耳边回荡，仿佛是被死者的灵魂在低声诉说。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

He turned over the leaves. Carelessly at first; but, lighting on a passage which attracted his attention, he soon became intent upon the volume. It was a history of the lives and trials of great criminals; and the pages were soiled and thumbed with use. Here, he read of dreadful crimes that made the blood run cold; of secret murders that had been committed by the lonely wayside; of bodies hidden from the eye of man in deep pits and wells: which would not keep them down, deep as they were, but had yielded them up at last, after many years, and so maddened the murderers with the sight, that in their horror they had confessed their guilt, and yelled for the gibbet to end their agony. Here, too, he read of men who, lying in their beds at dead of night, had been tempted (so they said) and led on, by their own bad thoughts, to such dreadful bloodshed as it made the flesh creep, and the limbs quail, to think of. The terrible descriptions were so real and vivid, that the sallow pages seemed to turn red with gore; and the words upon them, to be sounded in his ears, as if they were whispered, in hollow murmurs, by the spirits of the dead.

source: “Meat, ma’am, meat,” replied Bumble, with stern emphasis. “You’ve overfed him, ma’am. You’ve raised a artificial soul and spirit in him, ma’am unbecoming a person of his condition: as the board, Mrs. Sowerberry, who are practical philosophers, will tell you. What have paupers to do with soul or spirit? It’s quite enough that we let ’em have live bodies. If you had kept the boy on gruel, ma’am, this would never have happened.”
domestication: “肉，夫人，肉，”班布尔回答道，脸色严肃。“您给他吃得太好了，夫人，您让他养成了不该有的奢望：像我们这些实际的哲学家，比如董事会的女士们，都会这么告诉您的。穷人关心什么灵魂和精神呢？能让他们活命就不错了。如果您从一开始就让这个男孩只吃些残羹冷饭，这种事就不会发生。”
foreignization: "肉，夫人，肉，”班布尔回答道，他的语气严肃而有力。“你给他吃得太多了，夫人。你在他心里培养了一种人工的灵魂和精神，这是不适合他身份地位的，就像理事会的索韦伯里夫人，这些实际的哲学家会告诉你的。穷人和乞丐们有什么资格拥有灵魂或精神呢？我们允许他们活着就已经够好了。如果你一直让这个男孩喝粥，这种事情就不会发生。”

source: Although I am not disposed to maintain that being born in a workhouse, is in itself the most fortunate and enviable circumstance that can possibly befall a human being, I do mean to say that in this particular instance, it was the best thing for Oliver Twist that could by possibility have occurred. The fact is, that there was considerable difficulty in inducing Oliver to take upon himself the office of respiration,—a troublesome practice, but one which custom has rendered necessary to our easy existence; and for some time he lay gasping on a little flock mattress, rather unequally poised between this world and the next: the balance being decidedly in favour of the latter. Now, if, during this brief period, Oliver had been surrounded by careful grandmothers, anxious aunts, experienced nurses, and doctors of profound wisdom, he would most inevitably and indubitably have been killed in no time. There being nobody by, however, but a pauper old woman, who was rendered rather misty by an unwonted allowance of beer; and a parish surgeon who did such matters by contract; Oliver and Nature fought out the point between them. The result was, that, after a few struggles, Oliver breathed, sneezed, and proceeded to advertise to the inmates of the workhouse the fact of a new burden having been imposed upon the parish, by setting up as loud a cry as could reasonably have been expected from a male infant who had not been possessed of that very useful appendage, a voice, for a much longer space of time than three minutes and a quarter.
domestication: 虽然我不想说在救济院出生是世界上最幸运的事情，但至少对奥利弗·特威斯特来说，这是最好的开始。事实上，刚出生的奥利弗有些不太适应呼吸的这个事儿，这是一件麻烦事，但习惯了就好了，况且我们也离不开它。一开始，他在一个小小的羊毛垫子上断气不断，悬在人间和另一个世界之间，差点儿就去了另一个世界。如果当时他周围都是经验丰富的奶奶、着急的姨妈、老练的保姆和大夫，那么他很快就会没命了。但是他身边只有一个喝了点儿啤酒的穷老太婆和按合同办事的教区医生，所以奥利弗和大自然就这么一较高低。结果，他挣扎了一会儿，就开始哭了起来，向大家宣告：又添了一个寄生虫，要靠大家养活了。他的哭声很大，考虑到他才三分钟零一刻钟有了嗓子，这已经很不错了。
foreignization: 虽然我不倾向于认为在贫民院出生是可能发生在人类身上最幸运和值得羡慕的环境，但我是想说，在这个特定的情况下，对奥利弗·特威斯特来说，最好的事情可能已经发生了。事实是，有很大的困难让奥利弗自己承担呼吸的职责，这是一个麻烦的习惯，但是风俗使其成为我们轻松存在所必需的习惯；有一段时间，他躺在一个小羊毛垫子上，气喘吁吁地悬在这个世界和另一个世界之间：平衡明显偏向于后者。如果在这短暂的时期内，奥利弗被小心的祖母、焦虑的姨妈、有经验的护士和睿智的医生们围绕着，他毫无疑问和确实会在短时间内被杀死。然而，没有人在场，只有一个穷老太婆，她因不常有的啤酒供应而变得迷糊；还有一个教区外科医生，他以合同方式处理这种事情；奥利弗和自然在他们之间奋斗。结果是，在经过几次挣扎后，奥利弗呼吸、打喷嚏，并通过发出一个合理的哭声来通知贫民院的居民，教区已经被施加了新的负担，这个声音是可以从一个没有拥有如此有用的附属物——声音——已经超过三分钟零一刻钟的男婴那里合理期待的。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Although I am not disposed to maintain that being born in a workhouse, is in itself the most fortunate and enviable circumstance that can possibly befall a human being, I do mean to say that in this particular instance, it was the best thing for Oliver Twist that could by possibility have occurred. The fact is, that there was considerable difficulty in inducing Oliver to take upon himself the office of respiration,—a troublesome practice, but one which custom has rendered necessary to our easy existence; and for some time he lay gasping on a little flock mattress, rather unequally poised between this world and the next: the balance being decidedly in favour of the latter. Now, if, during this brief period, Oliver had been surrounded by careful grandmothers, anxious aunts, experienced nurses, and doctors of profound wisdom, he would most inevitably and indubitably have been killed in no time. There being nobody by, however, but a pauper old woman, who was rendered rather misty by an unwonted allowance of beer; and a parish surgeon who did such matters by contract; Oliver and Nature fought out the point between them. The result was, that, after a few struggles, Oliver breathed, sneezed, and proceeded to advertise to the inmates of the workhouse the fact of a new burden having been imposed upon the parish, by setting up as loud a cry as could reasonably have been expected from a male infant who had not been possessed of that very useful appendage, a voice, for a much longer space of time than three minutes and a quarter.

source: Sikes, invoking terrific imprecations upon Fagin’s head for sending Oliver on such an errand, plied the crowbar vigorously, but with little noise. After some delay, and some assistance from Toby, the shutter to which he had referred, swung open on its hinges.
domestication: 赛克斯对发昏的费金怒目而视，骂他怎么派奥利弗来干这种缺德事，他使劲挥舞着撬棍，但尽量不发出声音。过了一会儿，在托比的帮助下，他刚刚提到过的窗板在铰链上嘎吱一声打开了。
foreignization: 赛克斯在呼唤着对法金头部的可怕咒骂，因为他派奥利弗去执行这样的差事，他努力地使用撬棍，但发出很小的声音。在经过一些延迟，以及托比的一些帮助后，他所指的百叶窗在它的铰链上摆动开来。

source: “There!” he said, laying the bundle on the table. “Take care of that, and do the most you can with it. It’s been trouble enough to get; I thought I should have been here, three hours ago.”
domestication: “好了！”他把包裹放在桌子上，“你拿去，尽量处理吧。这东西真不容易弄到，我本来以为三小时前就应该到这里了。”
foreignization: “好了！”他把包裹放在桌子上，说，“照顾好它，并且尽你所能地处理它。要拿到它我已经很麻烦了；我本以为三小时前就应该到这里了。”

source: “Why, look’e, young gentleman,” said Toby, “when a man keeps himself so very ex-clusive as I have done, and by that means has a snug house over his head with nobody a prying and smelling about it, it’s rather a startling thing to have the honour of a wisit from a young gentleman (however respectable and pleasant a person he may be to play cards with at conweniency) circumstanced as you are.”
domestication: "这位小伙子，”托比说，“像我这样守口如瓶的人，能有个安静的房子住，这本身就是件幸事。家里又没有人来打探和乱窥探，你一个年轻人（虽然你是很有礼貌，也很愉快，方便的时候还可以一起玩牌），这样登门拜访，我这个老头子确实是受宠若惊。”
foreignization: “为什么，你看啊，小伙子，”托比说，“当一个人像我这样非常排他并且因此而拥有一个舒适的房子，没有人会窥视和打探，这对于一个处境像你这样的年轻绅士（尽管他在方便时是一个很有尊严和很愉快的牌友）来说，突然光临我的拜访是一个相当意外的事情。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Why, look’e, young gentleman,” said Toby, “when a man keeps himself so very ex-clusive as I have done, and by that means has a snug house over his head with nobody a prying and smelling about it, it’s rather a startling thing to have the honour of a wisit from a young gentleman (however respectable and pleasant a person he may be to play cards with at conweniency) circumstanced as you are.”
"这位小伙子，”托比说，“像我这样守口如瓶的人，能有个安静的房子住，这本身就是件幸事。家里又没有人来打探和乱窥探，你一个年轻人（虽然你是很有礼貌，也很愉快，方便的时候还可以一起玩牌），这样登门拜访，我这个老头子确实是受宠若惊。”

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“Why, look’e, young gentleman,” said Toby, “when a man keeps himself so very ex-clusive as I have done, and by that means has a snug house over his head with nobody a prying and smelling about it, it’s rather a startling thing to have the honour of a wisit from a young gentleman (however respectable and pleasant a person he may be to play cards with at conweniency) circumstanced as you are.”
“为什么，你看啊，小伙子，”托比说，“当一个人像我这样非常排他并且因此而拥有一个舒适的房子，没有人会窥视和打探，这对于一个处境像你这样的年轻绅士（尽管他在方便时是一个很有尊严和很愉快的牌友）来说，突然光临我的拜访是一个相当意外的事情。”

source: For a long time, Oliver remained motionless in this attitude. The candle was burning low in the socket when he rose to his feet. Having gazed cautiously round him, and listened intently, he gently undid the fastenings of the door, and looked abroad.
domestication: 奥利弗就这样一动不动地站在那里，过了很久才慢慢站了起来。这时蜡烛的光线已经快要灭了。他谨慎地环顾四周，认真地倾听了一会儿，然后轻轻地打开门闩，朝外面看去。
foreignization: 一段时间里，奥利弗保持着这个姿势一动不动。蜡烛在灯座上快燃烧完了的时候，他才站起身来。在他小心翼翼地向四周张望，并且仔细地倾听之后，他轻轻地解开门上的扣子，然后向外面看去。

For a long time, Oliver remained motionless in this attitude. The candle was burning low in the socket when he rose to his feet. Having gazed cautiously round him, and listened intently, he gently undid the fastenings of the door, and looked abroad.
一段时间里，奥利弗保持着这个姿势一动不动。蜡烛在灯座上快燃烧完了的时候，他才站起身来。在他小心翼翼地向四周张望，并且仔细地倾听之后，他轻轻地解开门上的扣子，然后向外面看去。

source: “I shall be carried out if I go!” said the girl violently; “and I can make that a job that two of you won’t like to do. Isn’t there anybody here,” she said, looking round, “that will see a simple message carried for a poor wretch like me?”
domestication: “我要是去了，必然会被拖走！”那个女孩愤怒地说。“而且我会让这件事成为你们两个都不会愿意做的事情。这里难道没有一个人，”她环顾四周，说，“能帮像我这样可怜的人送个简单的消息吗？”

source: “I will give fifty pounds,” cried an old gentleman from the same quarter, “to the man who takes him alive. I will remain here, till he come to ask me for it.”
domestication: 这时，同一方向传来一位老绅士的声音：“我愿出五十英镑，悬赏捉拿这个人，必须活捉！我会留在这里，等他亲自来找我领赏。”
foreignization: “我将会给予五十镑，”来自同一区域的一位老先生大声喊道，“给那个活着抓住他的人。我将会留在这里，直到他来向我索要它。”

source: Fagin put several other questions: all with the same drift of ascertaining whether the girl had profited by his unguarded hints; but, she answered them so readily, and was withal so utterly unmoved by his searching looks, that his original impression of her being more than a trifle in liquor, was confirmed. Nancy, indeed, was not exempt from a failing which was very common among the Jew’s female pupils; and in which, in their tenderer years, they were rather encouraged than checked. Her disordered appearance, and a wholesale perfume of Geneva which pervaded the apartment, afforded strong confirmatory evidence of the justice of the Jew’s supposition; and when, after indulging in the temporary display of violence above described, she subsided, first into dullness, and afterwards into a compound of feelings: under the influence of which she shed tears one minute, and in the next gave utterance to various exclamations of “Never say die!” and divers calculations as to what might be the amount of the odds so long as a lady or gentleman was happy, Mr. Fagin, who had had considerable experience of such matters in his time, saw, with great satisfaction, that she was very far gone indeed.
domestication: 法金问了很多问题，都想搞清楚这姑娘有没有领会到他之前的暗示。但是，她回答问题这么痛快，而且对他的打量目光如此无所谓，以至于法金的第一印象得到了证实：这姑娘确实喝多了。南希和犹太人收养的其他女孩一样，有个通病，而且这个毛病在她们年轻时甚至被纵容。她的邋遢模样和房间里弥漫着的杜松子酒气味，更加证实了法金的猜测。之后，她先是发作一阵，随后又恢复了平静，但很快又陷入了百感交集的状态：一会儿哭，一会儿喊“命运不妨”，并且总算着只要有贵族男女快活，赔率就不会太高。法金有过很多类似经验，他很满意地看到南希已经醉得不轻。
foreignization: 法金又问了几个问题：所有这些问题都是试图确定这个女孩是否从他的无意中透露的暗示中得到了好处；但是，她回答得这么爽快，并且对他那审视的目光完全无动于衷，以至于他最初以为她有点醉酒的印象得到了证实。南希确实也不能免受一个在犹太人那些女学生中很常见的弱点的影响；而且在她们年轻的时候，这种弱点反而是被鼓励而不是受到抑制的。她的杂乱无章的外貌，以及弥漫在房间里的大量杜松子酒的气味，给犹太人的假设提供了有力的证实；当她在沉溺于上述暂时的暴力表现之后，首先沉闷下来，然后陷入一种复合的情绪之中：在这种情绪的影响下，她一会儿哭泣，一会儿又说出“永不放弃！”之类的感叹，还有一些关于只要女士或先生幸福，赔率是多少的各种计算时，费金先生作为一个在这一方面有相当丰富经验的人，看到了令他非常满意的事情：她已经陷得很深了。

source: “That’s me!” cried the Jew, falling instantly, into the attitude of listening he had assumed upon his trial. “An old man, my Lord; a very old, old man!”
domestication: “就是我！”那个犹太人大声叫道，同时他马上又恢复了在审判时的听候态度。“是个老头儿，阁下；是个非常、非常老的老头儿！”
foreignization: “是我！”那个犹太人哭喊道，立刻又恢复到审判时他所保持的倾听的姿势。“一个老人，我的大人；一个非常、非常老的老人！”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

“That’s me!” cried the Jew, falling instantly, into the attitude of listening he had assumed upon his trial. “An old man, my Lord; a very old, old man!”
“就是我！”那个犹太人大声叫道，同时他马上又恢复了在审判时的听候态度。“是个老头儿，阁下；是个非常、非常老的老头儿！”
